products.title: Marsilea Hirsuta Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Marsilea hirsuta Vitro Culture® – Unique Clover-Like Foreground Carpet Plant

Sold as: 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing many small plantlets

Marsilea hirsuta is a charming and versatile foreground plant known for its small, clover-shaped leaves and creeping growth habit. Native to Australia, this aquatic fern is popular among aquascapers for its ability to create dense, naturalistic carpets in both high-tech and low-tech aquariums. Depending on light, CO₂, and nutrient levels, the plant can produce various leaf forms—ranging from simple grass-like blades to iconic four-leaf clover shapes—adding a dynamic visual appeal to aquascapes.

Cultivated through sterile Vitro Culture® technology, our Marsilea hirsuta comes free from pests, algae, and snails, offering a healthy, worry-free start for sensitive aquariums, including those housing shrimp and delicate species.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Marsilea hirsuta

  • Common Name: Dwarf Clover

  • Leaf Forms: Varies from simple blades to 4-leaf clover shapes depending on conditions

  • Growth Habit: Low, creeping, carpet-forming

  • Safe for Sensitive Setups: 100% pest, algae, and snail-free from sterile culture

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium light

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ recommended for faster growth; benefits from regular nutrients

  • Growth Method: Horizontal runners to form dense carpet

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended for best results

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates a lush, natural-looking foreground carpet

  • Perfect for Iwagumi and nature-style layouts

  • Offers texture and visual contrast to taller stem plants

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing healthy Marsilea hirsuta plantlets—ready to rinse, acclimate, and plant in your aquarium.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Photos shown are examples of mature growth under optimal conditions. Growth form and speed may vary depending on care, aquarium parameters, and setup. Vitro Culture® plants may require transition time after planting.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Never release Marsilea hirsuta—or any aquarium plant—into natural waterways. Doing so can disrupt local ecosystems. Dispose of unwanted portions responsibly to help preserve aquatic biodiversity.

products.title: Mixed Lotus Seeds – Assorted Colors for Ponds, Water Gardens, and Aquascapes
products.body_html:
Mixed Lotus Seeds – Assorted Colors for Ponds, Water Gardens, and Aquascapes

Transform your pond, water garden, or patio container into a vibrant aquatic paradise with our premium Mixed Lotus Seeds (Nelumbo nucifera)!
This curated seed mix features a stunning range of lotus flowers in pink, white, yellow, and multi-colored varieties — perfect for anyone looking to add breathtaking beauty, tranquility, and color to outdoor spaces. Whether you’re a beginner starting your first water garden or a seasoned aquatic plant enthusiast, these seeds make it easy to grow your own personal oasis.

Key Features and Benefits:

🌸 Assorted Color Varieties:
Enjoy a natural mix of pink, white, yellow, and multi-colored lotus blooms for dynamic, eye-catching displays.

🌿 High Sprouting Potential:
Hand-selected seeds with very high sprout rates when properly scarified and soaked before planting.

💧 Versatile Planting Options:
Perfect for ponds, aquascapes, decorative water bowls, or patio container gardens.

🌱 Beginner and Expert Friendly:
Simple four-step germination process: scarify, soak, sprout, and plant in shallow water.

🐠 Aquatic Ecosystem Benefits:
Provides natural shelter for fish and shrimp while helping to stabilize pond water quality.

How to Grow Lotus Seeds (Nelumbo nucifera) – Step-by-Step Guide:

1. Scarify the Seed:
Lotus seeds are oval with one pointed end and one rounded end.
Do not file the pointed (embryo) end.
Instead, gently file the rounded end until you see the white inner layer.
This helps water enter the seed and encourages germination.

2. Soak the Seed:
Place the scarified seed in a container of warm, clean water (around 75–85°F or 24–29°C). Change the water daily to keep it fresh.

3. Sprouting:
Within 1–7 days, the seed will swell and a sprout will emerge. Allow the sprout to grow 4–6 inches before planting.

4. Transfer to Soil:
Plant the sprouted seed into a pot filled with heavy clay soil or aquatic planting media. Submerge the pot under 2–4 inches of water.

5. Growing in Water:
Place the potted seedling in a sunny, warm location. As it matures, the lotus will send leaves above the water's surface and eventually produce stunning blooms.

Read the blog post: A Complete Guide on How to Grow Lotus Flowers from Mixed Lotus Seeds

Important Note:

🌿 Please be aware:
While our lotus seeds (Nelumbo nucifera) are selected for very high sprouting rates, we do not guarantee the flower color or that every seed will successfully grow. Flower coloration may vary naturally. Proper care, growing conditions, and patience are essential for best results.

Product Specifications:

  • Scientific Name: Nelumbo nucifera

  • Common Name: Lotus

  • Product Type: Mixed Lotus Seeds (Hardy and Tropical Varieties)

  • Light Requirement: Full Sun

  • Planting Medium: Heavy clay soil or aquatic planting media

  • Germination Time: 1–7 days after soaking

  • Ideal Use: Outdoor ponds, decorative water gardens, patio water containers

featured_image.alt: Bring your water garden to life with our Mixed Lotus Seeds — featuring a stunning assortment of pink, white, yellow, and multi-colored lotus varieties. Perfect for ponds, aquascapes, or decorative water features, these seeds are easy to germinate and grow into breathtaking blooms. Each packet includes a natural mix of hardy and tropical lotus seeds, ideal for adding vibrant color, tranquility, and beauty to your outdoor space. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced gardener, growing lotus from seed is

Lotus seeds are incredibly hardy and can remain viable for years when stored properly. Each seed is enclosed in a hard shell that protects it from harsh conditions, but this tough exterior must be prepared correctly before planting to ensure germination.

products.title: Hygrophila Pink Chai (Small Size)
products.body_html:
Hygrophila lancea 'Pink Chai' is a rare and captivating natural hybrid, derived from Hygrophila lancea 'Araguaia' and discovered at South Island Aquarium farm in Singapore. Named 'Chai' after the owner Chai Luan Lim, this extraordinary plant stands out with its vibrant colors and unique appearance, making it a prized addition to any aquarium.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Hygrophila lancea 'Pink Chai'
  • Common Name: Hygrophila sp. Chai
  • Family: Acanthaceae
  • Leaf Color: Neon pink and green hues
  • Growth Form: Elongated leaves

Unique Characteristics

Hygrophila sp. Chai boasts elongated leaves with stunning neon pink and green hues, providing a striking visual element to any aquarium. This stable hybrid ensures baby plants will also exhibit the same vivid pink coloration, although some stems may occasionally revert to the original reddish-brown color of Hygrophila lancea 'Araguaia.'

Care Requirements

To maintain the vibrant colors and healthy growth of Hygrophila lancea 'Pink Chai,' provide the following conditions:

  • Lighting: Moderate to high light
  • Water Parameters: Prefers temperatures between 72-82°F (22-28°C) and a pH range of 6.0-7.5. Sensitive to environmental changes.
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrates are preferred
  • Maintenance: Regular trimming ensures compact growth

Propagation

Hygrophila lancea 'Pink Chai' is easily propagated through stem cuttings. Simply cut and replant healthy stems to encourage new growth.

Benefits and Placement

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds dynamic color and texture to your aquarium
  • Versatility: Suitable for midground and background placement
  • Low Maintenance: Easy to care for with proper conditions

Conclusion

Enhance your aquarium with the rare and vibrant Hygrophila lancea 'Pink Chai.' Its unique appearance, ease of care, and adaptability make it a must-have for any aquascape. Order today and transform your tank into a visually stunning aquatic paradise.

  • Stunning pink, magenta color
  • Sold as young, miniature size Hygrophila Chai
  • Fresh and clean, grow direct from our high technology lab farm 

Disclaimer: Pictures are not the actual plants you will receive, but a sample representation. Unless specific, we don't guarantee aquatic plants will be free of pests, and pesticides, grow emerged or submerge.

products.title: Helanthium Vesuvius (Twisted Chain Sword)
products.body_html:
🌿 Helanthium bolivianum 'Vesuvius' – Twisted Chain Sword with Spiraling Elegance

Helanthium bolivianum 'Vesuvius', formerly known as Echinodorus 'Vesuvius', is a captivating sword plant cultivar selected from a mutation of H. bolivianum 'Angustifolius' at the renowned Oriental nursery in Singapore. Since its commercial introduction in 2006, this unique species has become a standout favorite for its eye-catching spiral leaves and elegant rosette structure. Known by hobbyists as the “Corkscrew Sword,” it brings motion, texture, and contrast to aquascapes.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we grow this plant in its emersed form. Emersed leaves are broader, shorter, and often less twisted—but once submerged in an aquarium, the transformation begins: long, narrow (approximately 4 mm wide), tightly spiraled leaves emerge in a lush rosette, reaching up to 25 cm in height in aquariums. These linear, ribbon-like leaves give a refined, Vallisneria-like appearance with a twist—literally.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Helanthium bolivianum 'Vesuvius'

  • Common Names: Twisted Chain Sword, Corkscrew Sword

  • Leaf Shape: Narrow, spiraling (submerged); broader, straighter (emersed)

  • Leaf Color: Fresh medium to dark green; semi-transparent

  • Growth Form: Rosette with runners (chain sword)

  • Max Height: Typically 20–25 cm submerged

  • Visual Comparison: Resembles Vallisneria but with tightly twisted leaves

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Growth Rate: Moderate; forms runners with daughter plants

  • Light Requirement: Moderate to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Recommended but not essential below pH 7.5

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required (fine gravel, sand, or aquasoil)

  • Fertilization: Needs full macro + micro nutrients in substrate and/or water column

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🪴 What You’ll Receive
Enhance your aquarium with our fresh, high-quality plants, available in convenient formats:

  • Loose Bare-Root Plants: Includes approximately 1–2 loose, bare-root plants, providing flexibility in planting and arrangement.

  • Rooted Bunched: Approximately 1–2 plants, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.

  • Rooted Potted: Includes approximately 1–2 plants, potted in a net pot with rock wool, ensuring stability and ease of placement in your aquarium.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds motion and texture with distinctive twisted leaves

  • Ideal as a midground plant in large aquariums or background in nano tanks

  • Excellent contrast against broadleaf or carpet plants

  • Forms a striking rosette that draws the eye

  • Easily shaped into dense groups or left as a striking solitary plant

⚠️ Disclaimer
This plant is shipped in its emersed form. Upon submersion, the leaves will gradually transition into their final form—narrow, upright, and tightly twisted. Older emersed leaves may melt back, which is normal. Remove any unsightly leaves to encourage healthy submerged growth.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Please never release aquarium plants into natural waterways. Always dispose of plant trimmings responsibly.

featured_image.alt: Helanthium Vesuvius with its signature spiral, ribbon-like green leaves growing in a lush aquarium foreground.
Emersed-grown Corkscrew Sword with broader leaves before aquarium transition.

products.title: Limnophila Heterophylla
products.body_html:
Limnophila Heterophylla, a striking yet easy-to-care-for aquatic plant, is a fantastic addition to any freshwater aquarium. Originating from Southeast Asia, it is renowned for its fast growth and feathery, vibrant green leaves, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts.

This plant is highly adaptable, thriving in a range of water conditions and light levels, though it flourishes best in medium to high lighting. Its rapid growth rate makes it an excellent choice for filling in the background or midground of tanks, creating a lush, dense aquatic landscape. Limnophila Heterophylla is also known for its ability to absorb excess nutrients, helping to maintain water quality and reduce algae growth.

Propagation is simple and efficient; just trim the top of the plant and replant the cutting into the substrate. This process allows for easy cultivation and shaping of your underwater garden.

Limnophila Heterophylla is not only aesthetically pleasing but also creates a healthy environment for your fish and invertebrates, providing shelter and enhancing the overall biodiversity of your aquarium.

Whether you're a seasoned aquarist or just starting, Limnophila Heterophylla is an excellent choice for adding beauty and balance to your aquatic setup.

products.title: Rotala Nanjenshan
products.body_html:
Rotala Nanjenshan is a captivating aquatic plant that promises to bring dynamic color and texture to any freshwater aquarium. Known for its delicate, fine-leaved appearance, this plant is a favorite among aquascapers for its ability to create a dense, bushy backdrop or a vibrant midground cluster.

Originating from Asia, Rotala Nanjenshan thrives in a range of aquatic conditions, but it achieves its fullest potential in environments with high lighting and adequate CO2 supplementation. Under these conditions, its leaves can transform from a lush green to rich golden and pink hues, providing a stunning visual contrast against darker green plants.

This plant's moderate growth rate makes it manageable for beginners yet still satisfying for more experienced aquarists looking to refine their aquascaping skills. Rotala Nanjenshan is not just beautiful but also functional, helping to oxygenate the water and provide shelter for smaller fish and invertebrates.

Whether you're aiming to create a dramatic 'Dutch style' aquarium or simply enhance your existing setup, Rotala Nanjenshan offers both aesthetic beauty and environmental benefits. It’s an excellent choice for anyone looking to elevate their aquatic garden with vibrant color and intricate textures.

products.title: Giant Water Snowflake
products.body_html:
🌿 Giant Water Snowflake (Nymphoides indica) – Floating Aquarium Plant and Pond, Water Garden

Giant Water Snowflake (Nymphoides indica) is an attractive floating aquatic plant known for its large round green leaves and elegant snowflake-like white flowers. This species creates a natural pond-style appearance in aquariums while providing gentle surface coverage that fish and shrimp enjoy.

This Giant Water Snowflake develops solid green floating leaves that can grow over 4 inches (10 cm) in diameter, making it one of the larger species within the Water Snowflake group. When mature and grown under suitable aquarium conditions, the plant may produce some of the largest flowers among Water Snowflake species, with delicate white blooms appearing just above the floating leaf pads.

Easy to grow and highly adaptable, Nymphoides indica thrives when allowed to float freely on the water surface, where its leaves can spread naturally and receive direct light.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Nymphoides indica
Common Name: Giant Water Snowflake
Family: Menyanthaceae

• Attractive round floating leaves that can exceed 4 inches in diameter
• Elegant snowflake-like white flowers when mature
• Provides natural shade and shelter for fish and shrimp
• Excellent floating plant for natural-style aquascapes

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting Needs: Low to high lighting
CO₂ & Fertilizers: Not required but benefits from nutrient-rich water
Growth Method: Floating plant; spreads through leaf nodes and runners
Growth Rate: Moderate

Nutrient Substrate: Not required
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

This plant grows best when allowed to float freely on the aquarium surface, where it can develop larger leaves and eventually produce flowers under stable conditions.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Creates a natural floating canopy effect
• Provides shade and shelter for fish and shrimp
• Adds gentle surface movement and visual depth to aquascapes

Giant Water Snowflake is ideal for nature-style aquascapes, low-tech aquariums, and planted tanks that benefit from floating plant coverage.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Giant Water Snowflake (Nymphoides indica) plants cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®.

Available in convenient pack sizes:

Pack 1 Plant:
Small fresh-cut plant with approximately 1 leaves (no roots or flowers).

Pack 5 Plants:
Five small fresh-cut plants with approximately 1 leaves each (no roots or flowers).

Pack 10 Plants:
Ten small fresh-cut plants with approximately 1 leaves each (no roots or flowers).

Plants are shipped as small fresh-cut plants. Once placed in the aquarium or pond, they will continue to grow and develop additional floating leaves under suitable conditions.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Appearance, size, and coloration may vary depending on growing conditions and season. Plants may arrive in their emersed (farm-grown) form and will transition to their submerged aquarium form after acclimation.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus' – Variegated Lucky Bamboo
products.body_html:
🎋 Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus' – Variegated Lucky Bamboo

Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus', commonly known as Variegated Lucky Bamboo, is an elegant and low-maintenance plant that brings a touch of serenity and natural beauty to any space. Despite its common name, it’s not a true bamboo but a resilient species from the Dracaena family, prized for its striking green leaves edged with creamy white or yellow variegation.

Perfect for both aquariums and decorative indoor arrangements, this plant thrives in water or moist soil, making it ideal for homes, offices, or feng shui displays.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Dracaena sanderiana 'Variegatus'

  • Common Name: Variegated Lucky Bamboo

  • Appearance: Elegant green stalks with glossy leaves featuring creamy white margins

  • Versatile Growth: Can be grown in water, hydroponic setups, or soil

  • Symbolism: Represents luck, prosperity, and positive energy in feng shui culture

  • Low Maintenance: Ideal for beginners and busy plant owners

🌱 Growth & Care

  • Lighting: Prefers bright, indirect light but tolerates low-light conditions

  • Watering: Keep roots submerged in clean, chlorine-free water; change weekly

  • Temperature: Thrives in 65–90°F (18–32°C)

  • Humidity: Moderate to high humidity preferred

  • Substrate: Can grow in soil, pebbles, or water with proper support

  • Fertilization: Use a mild liquid fertilizer monthly during growing season

🖼️ Display Ideas

  • Perfect for modern home decor, office desks, or zen garden arrangements

  • Combine multiple stems in glass vases or decorative pots for a lush, elegant look

  • Ideal for feng shui placement to invite harmony and prosperity

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as fresh-cut, unrooted individual plants:
1 Plant: Approximately 10+ inches tall, ready for water or soil placement.

⚠️ Disclaimer

This plant is shipped fresh-cut and unrooted. Upon arrival, place the stem in clean, chlorine-free water to encourage root development. Avoid direct sunlight to prevent leaf burn.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Plant Owner

Certain plant varieties may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release houseplants or aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy
products.body_html:
🌿 Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy – Compact, Wavy-Leaf Aquatic Plant

Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy is a slow-growing, compact aquarium plant distinguished by its beautifully curled, ruffled leaf edges. This hardy selection of the Cardinal Flower has a very bushy underwater growth form, making it ideal for smaller aquariums and precise aquascaping layouts. While its leaf size is similar to the original Lobelia cardinalis, its denser, more compact habit offers a unique texture and structure for your tank.

At Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, we cultivate Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy in its emersed form, where the tops of the leaves display a fresh green hue while the undersides show a striking red-purple color. When grown submerged, the foliage transitions to lush green, with its distinctive wavy edges adding visual interest. This variety is still relatively new and rarely available in the aquarium trade, making it a sought-after choice for collectors and aquascaping enthusiasts.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Unique Wavy Leaves: Curled, ruffled edges create a textured and decorative look.

  • Compact & Bushy Growth: Perfect for small tanks and foreground to midground placement.

  • Color Variation: Green tops with red-purple undersides in emersed growth; transitions to green when submerged.

  • Rare in Trade: A newer Lobelia cardinalis selection not commonly available.

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high lighting for healthy, compact growth.

  • CO₂ & Nutrients: Benefits from CO₂ injection and balanced fertilization, though not strictly required.

  • Growth Method: Cultivated emersed at Aquarium Plants Factory nursery.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended for strong root development.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes.

  • Propagation: Easily propagated by cuttings, like other stem plants. Trimming the main shoot encourages multiple side shoots.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds refined texture with curled leaf edges.

  • Works beautifully in dense groupings for Dutch-style layouts.

  • Single, well-ramified plants also make attractive focal points.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 3 individual, loose-cut stems without root

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 3 stems without root bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 3 stems without root potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Minor leaf melting or color transition is normal when adjusting from emersed to submerged growth. Healthy stems will regrow and adapt to their underwater environment.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Please do not release Lobelia Cardinalis Wavy or any aquatic plants into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings responsibly. Check your local regulations before purchase.

products.title: Dwarf Baby Tears
products.body_html:
🌿 Dwarf Baby Tears (Micranthemum callitrichoides) – Iconic Foreground Carpet Plant for High-End Aquascapes

Dwarf Baby Tears, also known as HC Cuba, is one of the most sought-after carpet plants in the aquascaping world. With its tiny, vibrant green leaves and dense, creeping growth, HC creates a lush, lawn-like foreground that adds realism, texture, and depth to Nature, Iwagumi, and ADA-inspired aquascapes.

Perfect for nano tanks and high-tech setups, this species rewards aquascapers with one of the most beautiful carpets achievable in planted aquariums.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Micranthemum callitrichoides
  • Common Names: Dwarf Baby Tears, HC Cuba
  • Growth Form: Dense, compact, low-growing carpet
  • Leaf Size: Extremely small, bright green, rounded leaves
  • Placement: Foreground

💧 Ideal Growing Conditions

  • Light: Medium–High to Very High
  • CO₂: Strongly recommended for tight, healthy carpeting
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate required
  • Water Parameters: pH 5.5–7.5 | 68–82°F (20–28°C)

  • Perfect for foreground in nano tanks and display aquascapes

products.title: Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Pink'
products.body_html:
Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Pink' is a vibrant terrarium, vivarium, or bog plant that is sometimes used as an aquatic plant decoration, despite not being a true aquatic species. This plant can be submerged for several months under the right conditions, but it requires periodic emersed growth to restore its energy and continue thriving.

Key Features:

  • Striking Appearance: Features vibrant pink leaves that add a splash of color to any setup, whether terrestrial or aquatic.
  • Dual Habitat: Can survive underwater for several months but needs to be grown emersed for a few months to recover and regain strength.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Submerged Growth: Can be submerged in aquariums for up to several months. After this period, it should be transitioned to emersed growth.
  • Emersed Growth: Requires high humidity and wet soil when grown above water, especially during recovery phases.
  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions, which helps maintain its vibrant color.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Versatile Use: While not a true aquatic plant, its ability to survive underwater makes it a popular alternative for hobbyists seeking unique decorations.
  • Temporary Aquatic Plant: Provides an attractive and colorful addition to aquariums for several months, developing roots and sometimes growing a few inches taller during its submerged period.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Periodic Transition: To ensure longevity, alternate the plant between submerged and emersed conditions every few months.
  • High Humidity: When grown emersed, maintain consistently high humidity and keep the soil wet to support recovery and growth.

Conclusion:

Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Pink' is a versatile and eye-catching plant that can enhance the beauty of terrariums, vivariums, bog gardens, and aquariums. Its ability to survive underwater for extended periods makes it a unique choice for aquarists looking to add a splash of color to their setups. By alternating between submerged and emersed growth, you can enjoy the vibrant presence of Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Pink' in various environments.

Enhance your terrarium, vivarium, or aquarium with the stunning and versatile Alternanthera Ficoidea 'Pink', and enjoy its vibrant colors and adaptability.

products.title: Bolbitis Heudelotii (African Water Fern)
products.body_html:
Bolbitis heudelotii, also known as African water fern or Congo fern, is a species of aquatic fern found in Africa. It is a popular plant in the aquarium hobby due to its attractive appearance and ease of care.

Bolbitis heudelotii has a rhizomatous growth habit, meaning it spreads by sending out horizontal stems called rhizomes. It typically grows to a height of 10-20 inches (25-50 cm) and has long, narrow leaves that can reach up to 18 inches (45 cm) in length. The leaves are dark green in color and have a glossy texture.

In the aquarium, Bolbitis heudelotii can be grown attached to rocks or driftwood, or planted directly in the substrate. It prefers low to moderate lighting and a neutral pH range of 6.5-7.5. It is a slow-growing plant that requires little maintenance, but will benefit from occasional fertilization and CO2 supplementation.

Bolbitis heudelotii is also found in the wild in streams and rivers in tropical Africa, where it serves an important ecological role as a primary producer and as a habitat for fish and other aquatic organisms.

  • Sold as Bare-root plants, or Potted plants
  • Bare-root plants | approx. 6 leaf, bare-root, loose rhizome
  • Potted plants | approx. 6 leaf plant potted with rockwool and plastic net pot

products.title: Alternanthera Pink Sunset
products.body_html:
🌸 Alternanthera reineckii ‘Pink Sunset’ – A Radiant Mutation of Underwater Elegance

Alternanthera reineckii ‘Pink Sunset’ is a breathtaking new mutation of the beloved Alternanthera reineckii, first introduced to the United States by Aquarium Plants Factory nursery in 2021. At first glance, this hybrid resembles Alternanthera reineckii 'Rosanervig', but its finer stems and elegantly narrower leaves set it apart as a distinct masterpiece of color and form.

Under strong light and nutrient-rich conditions, ‘Pink Sunset’ reveals a stunning gradient of magenta and rose-pink hues that shimmer through the water—making it one of the most striking stem plants for vibrant, artistic aquascapes.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Alternanthera reineckii ‘Pink Sunset’

  • Common Name: Pink Sunset Alternanthera

  • Origin: Cultivar; introduced by Aquarium Plants Factory nursery (U.S.A., 2021)

  • Form & Texture: Upright stem plant with slender, lanceolate leaves featuring glossy pink-to-magenta undersides and greenish tops.

  • Growth Height: 20–40 cm – perfect for midground to background placement.

  • Growth Rate: Moderate – responds well to trimming and replanting.

  • Temperature Tolerance: 20–28°C – ideal for tropical aquariums.

  • Water Conditions: pH 6–7.5 | Soft to moderately hard water.

  • Difficulty Level: Moderate – thrives with consistent lighting and nutrients.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high light required to maintain vivid pink coloration.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Supplemental CO₂ and regular fertilization enhance color and density.

  • Propagation: Simply cut and replant healthy stem tops; roots form easily in substrate.

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes – produces compact leaves in humid terrarium or paludarium conditions.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Complements Ludwigia palustris, Rotala H’ra, and Bucephalandra varieties beautifully.

  • Excellent for colorful mid- to background groupings or accent highlights.

products.title: Aciotis Acuminifolia Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Aciotis acuminifolia in Vitro Culture® – A Rare South American Marvel for Advanced Planted Tanks

Aciotis acuminifolia is a highly uncommon aquarium plant and the first aquatic representative from the tropical plant family Melastomataceae, which includes ornamental genera like Medinilla and Tibouchina. Originally introduced to the U.S. hobby in 2005 under the trade name "São Francisco Irecienu", this species remained a mystery until a DNA analysis in 2009 correctly identified it as Aciotis acuminifolia (Mart. ex DC.) Triana—a plant native to the South American tropics, most likely collected near the Rio São Francisco in eastern Brazil.

Now available in our sterile Vitro Culture® 4-inch bag, this plant is cultivated using advanced Tissue Culture Technology to ensure a 100% pest-free, algae-free, and pesticide-free start. It’s a unique addition for advanced hobbyists looking to diversify their scape with a scientifically rare and visually distinctive stem plant.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Aciotis acuminifolia (Mart. ex DC.) Triana

  • Common Name: São Francisco Irecienu (Trade Name)

  • Leaf Color: Submersed leaves appear green, yellow, or reddish with vibrant red undersides; coloration intensifies with high light

  • Growth Form: Upright, four-edged stems with cross-opposed leaves; produces lateral shoots under good care

  • Vitro Culture® Format: 4-inch sealed bag with multiple healthy plantlets in sterile gel or liquid

  • Placement: Midground or background in high-tech aquariums

  • Growth Rate: Moderate under optimal conditions

  • Difficulty Level: Hard

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high; strong light enhances red pigmentation and leaf structure

  • CO₂: Strongly recommended for submersed growth and coloration

  • Water Parameters: Soft water preferred | pH 5.5–7.0 | Temperature 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • Nutrients: Requires a nutrient-rich environment; root tabs and water column dosing advised

  • Substrate: Adaptable; develops an extensive root system over time

🌱 Emersed vs. Submersed Growth:

  • Submersed growth yields softer leaves with curled margins and red undersides

  • Emersed growth produces more serrated leaves and may flower with pale pink, 4-petaled blooms

  • Emersed conditions require high humidity but are relatively easy to maintain

🧪 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • 100% Pest-Free: No algae, snails, or pathogens

  • Clean Start: Ideal for sensitive tanks and advanced scapers

  • Extended Shelf Life: Store under light until planting

  • Ready to Use: Rinse, separate, and plant directly into your layout

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Exotic Collector’s Piece: A plant with true rarity and scientific intrigue

  • Color Contrast: Deep red leaf undersides offer warm contrast in green-heavy tanks

  • Dynamic Growth: Grows upright with lateral shoots, perfect for structured layouts

  • Botanical Depth: Adds unique botanical diversity to high-end or biotope-style aquascapes

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Fresh, healthy Aciotis acuminifolia cultivated using Vitro Culture® tissue culture technology and sealed in sterile media for optimal quality and transport.

Pack Options Available:

  • 1 Bag: One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing healthy Aciotis acuminifolia plantlets.
  • 5 Bags: Five 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bags containing healthy Aciotis acuminifolia plantlets.
  • 10 Bags: Ten 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bags containing healthy Aciotis acuminifolia plantlets.

Each bag contains multiple young plantlets ready to rinse, separate, acclimate, and plant in your aquarium or high-humidity emersed setup.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Images are representative. Plant size, color, and leaf shape may vary depending on lighting, CO₂, and nutrient availability. Submersed growth requires stable, soft water conditions. Insufficient lighting or CO₂ may result in stunted growth or leaf loss.

products.title: Flame Moss
products.body_html:
Taxiphyllum sp. Flame (Flame Moss) is a unique and captivating moss that adds an artistic touch to any freshwater aquarium. Renowned for its flame-like growth pattern, where the fronds grow upward in twisting, wavy shapes, this moss creates an eye-catching vertical effect that enhances the aquascape design.

Key Features:

  • Distinctive Growth Pattern: Flame Moss grows vertically with curling fronds resembling dancing flames, offering a stunning visual texture and movement within the tank.
  • Versatile Aquascaping: Perfect for attaching to driftwood, rocks, or any other aquarium decor, making it an excellent choice for aquascaping enthusiasts looking to add a touch of natural elegance.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under low to moderate lighting conditions, making it suitable for both beginner and advanced aquarists.
  • Water Parameters: Flame Moss prefers temperatures between 68°F to 77°F (20°C to 25°C) and a pH range of 5.0 to 7.5, adapting well to a variety of freshwater setups.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Natural Shelter & Breeding Ground: Provides excellent cover for shrimp, fry, and other small aquatic creatures, serving as a safe haven and breeding ground.
  • Easy Maintenance: Requires minimal care, making it a hassle-free option for those looking to add greenery to their aquariums without extensive upkeep.
  • Nutrient Absorption: Helps improve water quality by absorbing excess nutrients, contributing to a balanced aquatic environment.

Propagation and Care:

  • Simple Propagation: Can be easily propagated by dividing and attaching small sections to new surfaces in the tank.
  • Low Maintenance: Does not require CO2 supplementation and thrives in various conditions, making it a hardy and adaptable moss for all aquascapers.

Conclusion:

Taxiphyllum sp. Flame (Flame Moss) is an ideal moss for aquascaping enthusiasts seeking to add a dynamic and striking vertical element to their aquarium. Its unique flame-like growth pattern, easy care requirements, and benefits to aquatic life make it a popular choice for enhancing both the beauty and health of freshwater aquariums. Add Flame Moss to your aquascape for a visually stunning and low-maintenance addition that will captivate and charm.

  • Sold as a small loose portion, approx. 2" x 2" in size
  • Perfect to attached to driftwood, rock, or stone

products.title: Guppy Grass
products.body_html:
Najas guadalupensis, commonly known as Guppy Grass, is a popular aquatic plant species highly regarded for its versatility and aesthetic appeal. This submerged plant is native to North and Central America, specifically found in regions such as Texas, Mexico, and the Caribbean.

Key Features:

  • Versatile Growth: Thrives in a variety of aquatic setups, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists.
  • Aesthetic Appeal: Features long, slender stems with whorls of bright green leaves, providing a natural and attractive look to any aquarium.

Benefits:

  • Rapid Propagation: Known for its fast growth rate, Guppy Grass can quickly create lush, densely planted aquariums.
  • Habitat Creation: Provides a safe and comfortable habitat for fish, fry, and other small aquatic organisms, making it a favorite among breeders.
  • Water Purification: Acts as a natural water purifier by absorbing excess nutrients, helping to prevent algae growth and maintain water quality.
  • Shelter and Protection: Dense vegetation offers shelter for shy or vulnerable fish, contributing to their overall well-being.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Water Parameters: Thrives in a wide range of conditions but prefers slightly alkaline to neutral pH levels (6.5 to 7.5).
  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting enhances growth and vibrancy.
  • Nutrient Requirements: Benefits from a nutrient-rich substrate or liquid fertilizers.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming is necessary to manage rapid growth and maintain the desired appearance.
  • Propagation: Easily propagated through side shoots, allowing for easy expansion or sharing with fellow aquarists.

Conclusion:

Najas guadalupensis (Guppy Grass) is an excellent choice for those looking to add a versatile, visually appealing, and beneficial plant to their aquarium. Its rapid growth, ease of care, and ability to enhance the overall health of the aquatic environment make it a favored selection among aquarists. products.title: Limnophila Hippuridoides
products.body_html:
Limnophila Hippuridoides, an eye-catching stem plant native to the Malayan Peninsula, is a standout in the aquatic gardening world. Often mistaken for its relative, Limnophila aromatica, Limnophila Hippuridoides sets itself apart with its distinctive structure, featuring significantly more than three leaves per whorl. This lush, vibrant plant infuses any aquarium with an exotic flair, making it a top choice for aquascaping professionals and enthusiasts alike.

Renowned for its striking coloration and intricate leaf configuration, Limnophila Hippuridoides is a prized addition to any aquascape. Originating from Southeast Asia, it enhances aquariums with its dynamic and exotic presence, ensuring a visually appealing and lively aquatic environment. This plant's popularity in the aquascaping community underscores its beauty and versatility in creating natural underwater landscapes.

Key Features:

  • Stunning Coloration: Under optimal conditions, Limnophila Hippuridoides develops a rich range of colors, from green to a deep reddish-purple, especially on the undersides of its finely feathered leaves. This makes it a striking focal point in any aquascape.

  • Unique Leaf Structure: The plant’s narrow, elongated leaves are arranged in whorls along the stem, creating a dense, bushy appearance. This structure not only adds visual interest but also provides excellent cover for small fish and invertebrates.

  • Rapid Growth: Limnophila Hippuridoides is known for its fast growth, making it an excellent choice for filling in background spaces quickly. It can reach impressive heights under the right conditions, contributing to a lush, full appearance in the aquarium.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Requires medium to high lighting to bring out its full color potential and encourage robust growth. Under lower light, the plant may lose some of its vibrant coloration and become more green.

  • CO2 and Nutrients: While it can grow without CO2 supplementation, adding CO2 and providing a nutrient-rich substrate or liquid fertilizers will significantly enhance its growth rate and coloration.

  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and thrives in water temperatures between 22°C and 28°C.

Aquarium Placement:

  • Background Placement: Due to its height and dense growth habit, Limnophila Hippuridoides is best suited for background placement in the aquarium, where it can create a lush, colorful backdrop.

  • Group Planting: Planting in clusters will maximize its visual impact, creating a vibrant, bushy area that can serve as a contrast to other plants with different textures and colors.

Care Level: Moderate. While it’s generally easy to grow, maintaining its vibrant color and compact growth requires good lighting, CO2 supplementation, and a consistent nutrient supply.

Propagation:

  • Stem Cuttings: Propagating Limnophila Hippuridoides is simple and effective through stem cuttings. Cuttings can be replanted in the substrate, where they will quickly develop roots and grow into new plants.

Conclusion:

Limnophila Hippuridoides is a fantastic choice for aquarists looking to add a splash of color and texture to their aquarium. Its rapid growth, stunning coloration, and ease of care make it a popular choice for creating dynamic and visually appealing aquascapes. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced aquarist, this plant is sure to enhance the beauty of your underwater garden.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Tiger Stripe Frogbit
products.body_html:
🌿 Tiger Stripe Frogbit (Limnobium laevigatum 'Tiger Stripe')

Tiger Stripe Frogbit is a rare and eye-catching cultivar of Limnobium laevigatum, prized for its deep red and dark brown variegated leaves that resemble wild tiger markings. This floating species brings a bold, exotic flair to the water's surface while offering practical benefits like shade and nutrient absorption. Unlike common Amazon Frogbit, this variety grows noticeably slower, making it a better choice for controlled aquascapes where overgrowth is a concern.

  • Compact Growth: Slower-growing than standard Amazon Frogbit, reducing overgrowth concerns

  • Shade Provider: Creates natural cover for fish, shrimp, and fry below

  • Low Maintenance: Ideal for both beginner and experienced aquascapers

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting enhances leaf structure and coloration

  • Water Temperature: 70°F to 82°F (21°C to 28°C)

products.title: Pogostemon Sampsonii
products.body_html:
Pogostemon sampsonii is a captivating and rare aquarium plant from southern China, prized for its fine, willow-like foliage and flowing growth habit. Once misidentified as Limnophila punctata 'Blume' and Pogostemon cf. pumilus, this species was accurately classified by aquatic botanist Christel Kasselmann. A member of the Lamiaceae family, Pogostemon sampsonii thrives in both planted aquariums and nature aquascapes, where its soft, delicate texture creates contrast and movement underwater.

Key Features & Benefits

  • 🌿 Elegant Fine-Leaved Structure – Adds soft, flowing texture to the foreground or midground.

  • 💧 Ideal for Aquascaping – Perfect for nature aquariums, Iwagumi layouts, and Dutch-style tanks.

  • 💡 Thrives with CO₂ and Strong Light – Vibrant, lush growth with proper lighting and fertilization.

  • 🐠 Fish and Shrimp Friendly – Provides gentle cover for small aquatic animals.

  • 🌱 Rare Species – A collector's gem recently reclassified and gaining popularity in the hobby.

Transform Your Aquascape with Graceful Movement

Pogostemon sampsonii is ideal for aquarists seeking a plant that combines rarity with visual impact. Its delicate, elongated leaves sway gently in water flow, adding lifelike motion to your fish tank. When cultivated with CO₂ supplementation, nutrient-rich substrate, and high-intensity lighting, this plant forms dense, bushy clusters that enhance the natural feel of your freshwater planted aquarium.

products.title: Saururus Cernuus (Lizard's Tail)
products.body_html:
Saururus cernuus – Unique Heartleaf Emerged Plant for Aquariums & Paludariums

Saururus cernuus, commonly known as Lizard’s Tail, is a versatile and visually striking aquatic plant native to North America. Known for its distinctive heart-shaped leaves and elegant upright growth, this plant thrives in high-humidity environments and shallow water conditions, making it an excellent choice for paludariums, ripariums, Wabi-Kusa setups, and transitional zones in aquariums.

This plant is typically offered in its emersed form, allowing it to adapt quickly to a variety of semi-aquatic and marginal aquatic environments. Its foliage can develop a rich, bright green color with a graceful arching habit, and in emersed conditions, it may even produce delicate white flower spikes resembling a lizard’s tail.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Saururus cernuus

  • Common Names: Lizard’s Tail, Heartleaf

  • Leaf Shape: Heart-shaped, bright green, lush foliage

  • Growth Form: Upright stem plant with arching stems and broad leaves

  • Light Requirement: Low to medium

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Temperature Range: 60–82°F (16–28°C)

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5; soft to moderate hardness

  • Growth Rate: Medium to fast

  • Placement: Ideal for emersed sections in paludariums, ripariums, and marginal aquarium zones

🧪 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under low to moderate light; high humidity enhances leaf health

  • Temperature: Best between 68–78°F for vigorous growth

  • Substrate: Can grow in wet soil or rooted in rockwool; prefers nutrient-rich base

  • Water Depth: Shallow or marginal; thrives in transitional wet zones

  • Fertilization: General liquid or substrate fertilization helps support lush foliage

💡 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Emersed Appeal: Perfect for paludariums, rimless tanks, and shallow water displays

  • Architectural Form: Adds height, structure, and soft movement to aquascapes

  • Natural Accent: Pairs beautifully with mosses, ferns, and hardscape

  • Seasonal Bloomer: May produce fragrant, white tail-like flowers in emersed setups

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

  • Growth Speed: Moderate to fast

  • Propagation Method: Via stem cuttings or underground rhizome division

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – optimal in emersed form

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Available in three convenient planting formats to suit your aquatic or semi-aquatic design:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approx. 3–4 loose, fresh-cut stems without roots

  • Bunched Plants: Approx. 3–4 fresh-cut stems bundled with plant weight for easy placement

  • Potted Plants: Approx. 3–4 fresh-cut stems potted in rockwool within a net pot

Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis', also known as Cryptocoryne tonkinensis, is a beautiful aquatic plant that is highly sought after by aquarium enthusiasts. The plant has long, narrow leaves that can range in color from green to reddish-brown, depending on the lighting and nutrient levels in the water. Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis' is a relatively low-maintenance plant that can adapt to a wide range of water parameters, making it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced hobbyists.

In addition to its ornamental value, Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis' also provides practical benefits to aquariums. It can help to absorb excess nutrients and provide shelter for aquatic inhabitants, creating a more balanced and healthy aquatic environment. Due to its unique appearance and practical benefits, Cryptocoryne crispatula 'Tonkinensis' has become a popular choice for aquascaping projects and is highly valued among aquatic plant collectors.

products.title: Alternanthera Red Broad Leaf
products.body_html:
🌺 Alternanthera Red Broad Leaf — Vibrant Terrarium/Vivarium & Bog Plant (Not a True Aquatic)

Alternanthera Red Broad Leaf brings bold, ruby-red foliage and a lush, broad-leaf texture to terrariums, vivariums, paludariums, and bog gardens. While not a true aquatic, it can be used as a temporary aquarium decoration—surviving several months submerged under strong light—then should be returned to emersed conditions to restore vigor and color.

🌟 Key Features

  • Striking Color & Form: Broad, red leaves create an instant focal point in naturalistic displays.

  • Versatile Placement: Excellent for terrariums, ripariums, paludariums, and bog edges; suitable as short-term submerged décor.

  • Easy Propagation: Trim and replant healthy tips to thicken growth.

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions (Emersed)

  • Humidity: High humidity with consistently moist soil (never dry).

  • Lighting: Moderate to high light enhances deep red coloration.

  • Temperature: 70–82°F (21–28°C) preferred; avoid cold drafts.

  • Fertilization: Regular, balanced nutrients; root-zone feeding improves leaf size and color.

Temporary Submerged Use (Aquarium):

  • Can be grown submerged for a few months with strong light and stable parameters.

  • Expect rooting and a bit of vertical growth; color may soften underwater.

  • Plan to transition back to emersed conditions periodically to recover energy.

🔁 Care & Maintenance

  • Periodic Transition: For longevity, alternate the plant between submerged and emersed every 2–3 months (timing may vary by setup).

  • Trim & Replant: Regular tip trims keep growth compact and encourage bushiness.

  • Moisture Management: In emersed culture, keep substrate wet and humidity high during recovery after an aquarium stint.

🧪 Growth & Propagation Snapshot

  • Growth Method: Emersed primary; submerged temporary.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required (rich, moist soil when emersed).

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes (recommended/primary).

  • Propagation: Stem cuttings.

📦 What You’ll Receive

  • Sold as loose Fresh-cut Plants, Bunched plants, or Potted plants
  • Fresh-cut plants | approx. 5 stems, loose fresh-cut plants
  • Bunched plants | approx. 5 fresh-cut plants with foam, and lead weight
  • Potted plants | approx. 5 fresh-cut plants with net pot and rock wool

⚠️ Disclaimer

This is not a true aquatic species. Long-term submersion will lead to gradual decline. For best results and plant health, rotate back to emersed culture regularly. Coloration varies with light, nutrients, and culture method.

Myriophyllum sp. “Roraima” is a stunning fine-leaf aquarium stem plant prized for its vibrant orange, reddish, and brown tones under proper lighting. Native to the Brazilian region of Roraima, this fast-growing milfoil creates beautiful feathery foliage that sways gently in the water current, giving aquascapes a soft and natural appearance.

With its delicate texture and rapid growth, this species quickly develops into lush bushes when trimmed regularly. It is an excellent plant for filling space in the midground or background of larger aquariums and works especially well as a color accent among green plants.

Scientific Name: Myriophyllum sp. “Roraima”
Common Name: Roraima Milfoil
Family: Haloragaceae

• Beautiful reddish-brown to orange coloration under strong lighting
• Fine feathery leaves arranged in whorls along the stem
• Very fast growth rate ideal for filling new aquascapes
• Excellent accent or background plant for planted aquariums

Lighting Needs: Medium to high lighting for best coloration
CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ recommended but can grow without it; benefits from complete macro and micronutrients
Growth Method: Stem plant propagated through cuttings
Growth Rate: Very fast

Nutrient Substrate: Not Required
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

With regular trimming, the plant becomes bushier and produces dense clusters of stems. Cuttings can easily be replanted to create fuller groups.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Adds rich warm color contrast to green aquatic plants
• Creates soft flowing texture due to its delicate feathery foliage
• Ideal for background planting or large midground bushes

This species works particularly well in Dutch-style aquascapes or planted aquariums where vibrant color and fine textures are desired.

products.title: Myriophyllum Mattogrossense Golden Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Myriophyllum mattogrossense 'Golden' Vitro Culture® – Rare Golden Aquascape Stem Plant

Myriophyllum mattogrossense 'Golden' Vitro Culture® is a rare and elegant whitish variety of Myriophyllum mattogrossense, prized for its delicate feather-like foliage and striking pale coloration. While the standard species displays light green tones, the “Golden” variety develops extremely pale yellow to whitish leaves, creating a dramatic contrast in planted aquariums.

This beautiful stem plant adds softness, texture, and brightness to aquascapes, making it an excellent choice for background placement or large planted layouts where its unique coloration can stand out against darker green plants.

Because of its delicate nature, Myriophyllum “Golden” requires stable and well-maintained aquarium conditions to thrive. It performs best in aquariums with a consistent nutrient supply, adequate carbon availability, and a mature biological environment.

Under strong lighting and optimal conditions, the plant develops very pale golden to almost white foliage with subtle reddish tips, creating a striking visual highlight in planted aquariums. In lower lighting conditions, the leaves may appear more greenish.

This plant grows relatively quickly when provided with proper nutrients and CO₂. As a branching stem plant, it responds well to trimming and can be easily propagated by cutting and replanting healthy stems. Regular pruning helps maintain a dense, attractive plant group while preventing overcrowding.

A mature tank environment is highly recommended for this species. Newly established aquariums often experience unstable nutrient levels and algae outbreaks, and Myriophyllum “Golden” is particularly susceptible to algae. When algae attach to the leaves, the plant may lose its signature pale coloration. Maintaining strong plant growth and good algae control is key to successfully growing this variety.

Grown using Aquarium Plants Factory® Vitro Culture® technology, these plants are cultivated in sterile laboratory conditions and delivered free from algae, pests, snails, and harmful pathogens, providing a clean and healthy start for your aquarium.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Myriophyllum mattogrossense 'Golden'
Common Name: Golden Mattogrossense Watermilfoil
Growth Type: Stem Plant
Placement: Midground / Background

• Beautiful golden-green feathered foliage
• Creates soft flowing texture in aquascapes
• Fast-growing under proper lighting
• Excellent plant for nature-style aquariums
• Ideal for both beginner and advanced aquascapers

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting Needs: Medium to High
CO₂: Not Required (but recommended for faster growth)
Growth Rate: Medium to Fast
Placement: Midground to Background

Myriophyllum mattogrossense grows vertically and produces many side shoots as it matures. Simply trim the stems and replant the cuttings to propagate and create dense plant groups.

This species adapts well to many aquarium conditions and responds beautifully to nutrient-rich water and strong lighting.

Nutrient Substrate: Not Required
Can Be Grown Emersed: No

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Creates soft, flowing texture that contrasts with broad-leaf plants
• Adds bright golden color highlights to planted aquariums
• Perfect for nature aquariums and Dutch-style layouts
• Helps create depth and background layering in aquascapes

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Myriophyllum mattogrossense 'Golden' Vitro Culture® grown in sterile laboratory conditions and sealed in 4-inch tissue culture bags.

Each 4-inch bag contains numerous small plants grown in nutrient gel. Once rinsed and separated, the culture typically yields many individual plantlets ready for planting in your aquarium.

Images shown are for reference only. As a natural product, plant size, shape, and coloration may vary depending on growing conditions and aquarium environment.

Vitro Culture® plants are grown emersed in sterile laboratory conditions. When introduced to an aquarium, the plants may undergo a short transition period as they adapt to submerged growth.

products.title: Hottonia Palustris Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Hottonia Palustris – Water Violet Vitro Culture Aquarium Plant

Hottonia palustris, commonly known as Water Violet, is a delicate and elegant aquarium stem plant loved by aquascapers for its soft, feathery foliage and unique star-shaped leaf arrangement. Its bright lime-green leaves grow in organized whorls around the stem, creating a beautiful snowflake-like texture that adds depth and contrast to planted aquariums.

This plant is especially popular in Dutch-style aquascapes, where its fine leaf structure contrasts beautifully with broader-leaf plants. As it grows, Hottonia palustris forms dense, bushy clusters that gently sway with water movement, bringing a natural and dynamic look to the aquarium.

Our vitro culture plants are grown in sterile laboratory conditions, ensuring they are 100% pest-free, algae-free, and snail-free, making them perfect for shrimp tanks and carefully maintained planted aquariums.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Hottonia palustris
Common Name: Water Violet
Family: Primulaceae

• Beautiful feathery lime-green foliage with a snowflake-like appearance
• Unique whorled leaf structure that creates soft texture in aquascapes
• Excellent midground or background plant for planted aquariums
• Forms dense, bushy clusters with regular trimming

🌱 Growth & Care

Lighting Needs: Medium to high lighting
CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ recommended for fuller growth but not required
Growth Method: Stem plant propagated by cuttings
Growth Rate: Medium

Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

With proper lighting and nutrients, Hottonia palustris develops lush bushy groups that can be trimmed and replanted to create fuller aquascapes.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Adds fine, feathery texture to planted aquariums
• Creates soft movement as leaves sway in water flow
• Ideal for midground or background planting in aquascapes

Its bright green coloration and delicate structure make it an excellent contrast plant alongside broader-leaf species or darker green plants.

products.title: Micranthemum Monte Carlo
products.body_html:
🌿 Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo' – Lush, Fast-Spreading Carpet Plant

Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo', commonly known as Monte Carlo, is one of the most popular carpeting plants for planted aquariums. Its small, round, bright green leaves create a dense, vibrant carpet that brings a lush, natural look to any aquascape. Unlike some other carpeting species, Monte Carlo is relatively fast-growing and adaptable, making it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced aquascapers.

At Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, we cultivate Monte Carlo emersed for stronger growth and easier adaptation to your tank. When submerged with good light and CO₂, this plant will quickly spread across the substrate, creating a beautiful foreground or midground carpet.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo'

  • Common Names: Monte Carlo, MC, New Large Pearl Grass

  • Leaf Shape: Small, round

  • Leaf Color: Bright green

  • Growth Form: Low-growing carpet, creeping runners

  • Placement: Foreground to midground

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Light Requirement: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Strongly recommended for dense carpeting

  • Propagation: Trim runners and replant

Nutrient Substrate: Required
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🪴 What You’ll Receive

  • Fresh-cut Bunched: Approx. 8+ stems, bunched with foam and plant weight

  • Fresh-cut Potted: Approx. 8+ stems, potted with net pot and rockwool

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates a lush, vibrant green carpet across your substrate

  • Provides natural shelter and foraging areas for shrimp and nano fish

  • Enhances depth and perspective in aquascapes

  • Excellent for Iwagumi and Nature Aquarium styles

Monte Carlo plants are sold freshly cut and may initially melt or lose leaves during transition to submerged growth. With proper care, light, and CO₂, they will quickly rebound and spread across your aquarium.

featured_image.alt: Micranthemum Monte Carlo, a captivating aquatic plant, brings the allure of a lush meadow to freshwater aquariums. Hailing from the southern regions of North America, this plant is celebrated for its ability to transform underwater landscapes into verdant carpets of green.

products.title: Vallisneria Americana
products.body_html:
🌿 Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica' – Twisting Corkscrew Val for Flowing Foreground & Midground Texture

Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica', also known as Corkscrew Vallisneria or Twisted Val, is a unique and eye-catching variety of Vallisneria with narrow, bright green leaves that twist along their length. Unlike the towering growth of other Vallisneria species, this compact cultivar reaches only 8–12 inches (20–30 cm) in height, making it perfect for midground or even large foreground placements in aquascapes.

Native to North America and widely cultivated in Asia, Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica' forms elegant spirals of foliage that create subtle motion in the water column. When planted in dense groups, the twisting leaves add beautiful texture and contrast to an otherwise linear layout. It’s a hardy, beginner-friendly plant that spreads quickly via runners and adapts to a wide range of tank conditions.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Vallisneria americana 'Asiatica'

  • Synonyms: Twisted Vallisneria, Corkscrew Vallisneria

  • Common Name: Corkscrew Val

  • Twisted Green Leaves: Narrow, spiraled leaves create dynamic visual texture

  • Compact Size: Grows to 8–12 inches, ideal for midground planting

  • Beginner-Friendly: Easy to grow, fast to propagate, and low maintenance

  • Runner Propagation: Quickly forms a thick colony with regular offshoots

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high light enhances leaf form and color

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ not required; grows best with root tabs or nutrient substrate

  • Growth Method: Spreads via runners; forms dense spiraled clusters over time

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds movement and texture to midground or foreground zones

  • Provides contrast against broadleaf or red-stemmed species

  • Perfect for softening the transition between low and tall background plants

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual loose, bare-root plants

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Leaf twist intensity and coloration may vary slightly depending on lighting and nutrients. Some initial melting is normal upon introduction. Stable conditions promote quick propagation and healthy regrowth.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Always follow your local guidelines and never release aquarium plants into natural water systems. Dispose of plant trimmings responsibly.

products.title: Micranthemum Monte Carlo Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo' Vitro Culture® – Lush, Fast-Growing Carpet for Vibrant Aquascapes

Enhance your aquarium with the lush, fast-growing beauty of Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo', one of the most popular modern carpeting plants in the aquascaping world. Commonly known as Monte Carlo, MC, or New Large Pearl Grass, this versatile species forms a dense, bright green carpet that brings a natural meadow-like feel to both small and large planted tanks.

A favorite for Iwagumi layouts, Dutch aquascapes, and nature-style aquariums, Monte Carlo offers a perfect balance of easy growth and high visual impact. Compared to more demanding carpets like Hemianthus callitrichoides (HC), Monte Carlo is much easier to manage, making it an excellent choice for aquarists of all skill levels.

Now available in Vitro Culture® 4-inch bag, this offering is lab-grown under sterile conditions to ensure a pest-free, algae-free start—perfect for sensitive tanks, shrimp aquariums, and pristine aquascapes.

  • Origin: South America (Argentina)

  • Leaf Characteristics: Small, round to oval bright green leaves on creeping stems

  • Growth Rate: Moderate to fast; forms dense carpet under good conditions

  • Light Demand: Medium to high; promotes compact, low growth

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required but greatly improves density and speed of carpet formation

  • Water Parameters: Adaptable; pH 6.0–7.5

products.title: Subwassertang Moss (Round Pellia)
products.body_html:
Subwassertang Moss – Unique Liverwort for Naturalistic Aquascapes

Subwassertang Moss (Lomariopsis lineata), also known as Round Pellia or Loma Fern, is a captivating, beginner-friendly aquatic plant prized for its translucent, ruffled fronds and gentle, flowing appearance. Though often mistaken for a moss or seaweed, this plant is technically the prothallus (juvenile stage) of a fern. First gaining popularity in Southeast Asia, Subwassertang is now beloved worldwide for its low-maintenance care and versatile aquascaping applications.

Ideal for planted aquariums of all sizes, it attaches easily to wood, rock, or decor, forming lush, bushy clusters over time. Whether used in nano tanks, shrimp setups, or nature-style layouts, Subwassertang brings a soft, forest-floor look and provides valuable shelter for aquatic invertebrates.

🌿 Key Features of Subwassertang Moss

  • Scientific Name: Lomariopsis lineata

  • Common Names: Subwassertang, Round Pellia, Loma Fern

  • Plant Type: Liverwort / Prothallus (fern gametophyte)

  • Leaf Form: Rounded, frilly, translucent green fronds forming dense, tangled mats

  • Epiphytic Nature: Can be tucked or glued onto driftwood, rocks, or decor—eventually self-attaching

  • Ideal for Beginners: Grows in a wide range of water parameters and lighting levels

✅ Optimal Growing Conditions

  • Lighting: Low to medium

  • CO₂: Not required (optional for faster, denser growth)

  • Substrate: Not needed—attach to hardscape or let it float

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.8

    • Temperature: 59–82°F (15–28°C)

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits

  • Natural Look: Adds texture and visual interest with its soft, flowing form

  • Shelter for Shrimp & Fry: Provides hiding spaces and grazing areas for shrimp, baby fish, and microfauna

  • Minimal Maintenance: Slow-growing and resistant to algae—ideal for long-term scapes

  • Floating or Attached: Can be used as a floating plant or fixed to stone, wood, or mesh

🌱 Why Choose Subwassertang Moss?

Unlike traditional mosses, Subwassertang Moss offers a unique visual and textural experience with its rounded, vibrant fronds. It stands out in shaded aquascapes, complements rock and wood layouts, and requires little maintenance, making it a favorite for low-tech and nature-style setups alike. For aquarists who want something different—yet easy—this is the perfect choice.

📦 What You’ll Receive

  • Loose Portion: Includes a small, loose portion of Subwassertang Moss (approx. 2" x 2")

  • Ready to Use: Can be attached directly to your aquascaping elements with glue, thread, or simply tucked into crevices

🔒 Disclaimer

Photos shown are sample representations. Actual portions may vary slightly in size and density. Unless stated otherwise, plants may arrive in either emersed or submerged form and may carry natural variability in color and shape. Not guaranteed to be free of pests or pesticides.

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Never release Subwassertang Moss—or any aquarium plant—into natural waterways. Doing so can disrupt local ecosystems. Dispose of any unwanted portions responsibly to help preserve aquatic biodiversity.

products.title: Hygrophila Difformis | Bulk Pack
products.body_html:
🌿 Hygrophila Difformis – Water Wisteria Bulk Pack | Fast-Growing Aquarium Stem Plant

Hygrophila difformis, commonly known as Water Wisteria, is one of the most popular and beginner-friendly aquarium plants in the hobby. This fast-growing aquatic stem plant is prized for its beautiful lace-like, fern-shaped leaves and vibrant green coloration that instantly adds lush density to planted aquariums.

Native to the Indian subcontinent, Water Wisteria is extremely adaptable and can thrive in a wide range of aquarium conditions. Its rapid growth helps absorb excess nutrients from the water column, making it an excellent natural ally for maintaining balanced and algae-controlled aquariums.

This Bulk Pack of Hygrophila Difformis is perfect for aquascapers, breeders, and planted tank enthusiasts who need large quantities of healthy stems to create dense backgrounds, jungle-style aquascapes, or nutrient-absorbing plant mass in aquariums.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Hygrophila difformis
Common Name: Water Wisteria
Plant Type: Freshwater stem plant

• Fast Growing: One of the fastest growing aquarium plants, ideal for stabilizing new tanks.
• Beautiful Leaf Structure: Features delicate, deeply lobed leaves that resemble aquatic ferns.
• Beginner Friendly: Extremely hardy and adaptable to various aquarium conditions.
• Excellent Nutrient Absorber: Helps reduce algae by consuming excess nutrients.
• Versatile Placement: Can be used as a midground or background plant in aquascapes.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting: Low to High lighting
CO₂ Requirement: Not required, but growth improves with CO₂
Growth Rate: Fast
Placement: Midground to Background
Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

Propagation is extremely simple. Just trim the stems and replant the cuttings into the substrate. Each cutting will quickly develop new roots and continue growing.

Because of its rapid growth, Hygrophila difformis is commonly used in new aquariums to help establish biological balance and improve water quality.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Creates lush jungle aquascapes with dense, feathery foliage

Water Wisteria can also develop different leaf shapes depending on whether it grows submerged or emersed, giving aquascapers unique visual variety.

products.title: Anubias Peacock | Bulk Pack

Anubias Peacock is a beautiful and distinctive Anubias variety prized for its elegant leaf shape and textured foliage. Its leaves feature slightly elongated tips and a graceful, fan-like arrangement that resembles the elegant spread of a peacock’s tail, giving this plant its unique name.

This hardy rhizome aquarium plant is perfect for aquascapers looking to quickly add structure and lush greenery to their aquariums. The Bulk Pack option (5, 10, or 20 plants) allows hobbyists, aquascapers, and aquarium stores to instantly create dense clusters of Anubias on driftwood, rocks, or decorative hardscape.

Like most Anubias species, Anubias Peacock is extremely adaptable and beginner-friendly, thriving in both low-tech and high-tech aquariums. Its slow growth and durable leaves make it resistant to fish nibbling and suitable for a wide range of freshwater setups.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Anubias barteri “Peacock”
Common Name: Anubias Peacock
Plant Type: Rhizome / Epiphyte

• Elegant elongated leaves arranged in a fan-like pattern
• Extremely hardy and adaptable freshwater aquarium plant
• Perfect for attaching to driftwood, rocks, or aquarium décor
• Ideal for beginners and low-tech aquariums
• Bulk pack allows quick creation of lush aquascapes

Nutrient Substrate: Not Required
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Anubias Peacock grows from a thick horizontal rhizome. The rhizome should not be buried in substrate; instead, attach the plant to driftwood or rocks using aquarium glue or thread. Over time, the roots will naturally anchor the plant to the surface.

Propagation is simple—just divide the rhizome once the plant has grown several leaves.

• Excellent for shrimp tanks and community aquariums

products.title: Samolus Parviflorus Red
products.body_html:
Samolus parviflorus 'Red' — A Rare Splash of Red for Your Aquascape

Samolus parviflorus 'Red' is a unique and visually striking aquatic plant prized for its vivid coloration and easy adaptability. Native to the limestone-rich rivers of South China, this rare beauty offers a rosette-style growth pattern and deep red hues when grown submerged—making it an exceptional choice for aquascapers who want to introduce bold, natural tones into their underwater landscapes.

Unlike many "red" plants that require high maintenance, Samolus 'Red' thrives with moderate care, making it accessible for both beginners and experienced hobbyists.

Key Features, Benefits & Growing Tips:

Emerges green when grown emersed but transitions into brilliant shades of red when fully submerged under moderate to high light.

🌿 Rosette Growth Form:
Creates low, compact clusters perfect for midground accents or foreground highlights in planted aquariums.

💧 Adaptable & Easy to Care For:
Tolerates a wide range of water parameters and grows well with or without CO₂ injection (though CO₂ and strong lighting intensify the red coloration).

✂️ Simple Propagation:
Can be propagated easily by replanting side shoots or separated stems, allowing for fast carpet expansion.

🐠 Perfect For:
Nature aquariums, contrast-driven layouts, biotope-style tanks, and hobbyists wanting a rare, standout species in their collection.

Why Choose Samolus parviflorus 'Red' for Your Planted Aquarium?

Adding depth and vibrant contrast to a planted tank can be challenging—but Samolus parviflorus 'Red' makes it effortless. Its brilliant submerged coloration immediately draws the eye, providing a bold counterbalance to green foliage plants. Hardy, undemanding, and visually captivating, it’s an excellent choice for aquascapers aiming to create a dynamic and colorful aquascape without the intense care often associated with red plants.

Whether you’re crafting a lush nature aquarium, designing a contest-level tank, or simply want something rare and beautiful, Samolus 'Red' is a rewarding and impactful addition.

products.title: Water Lettuce
products.body_html:
🌿 Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes) | Floating Aquarium & Pond Plant

Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes) is a classic floating plant known for its beautiful, rosette-shaped leaves that resemble small heads of lettuce. Its soft, velvety foliage and long dangling roots provide excellent cover and natural filtration for ponds and aquariums. Water Lettuce thrives in calm, nutrient-rich waters, helping to shade your aquatic environment and reduce algae growth.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Natural Filtration: Absorbs excess nutrients, improving water quality.

  • Floating Growth: Perfect for ponds, water gardens, and outdoor tubs.

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Velvety green leaves form charming rosettes atop the water surface.

  • Fish & Wildlife Benefits: Provides shade and protection for fish, frogs, and other aquatic life.

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high; prefers full sun in outdoor ponds.

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5; temperature 70–85°F (21–29°C)

  • Placement: Floating on the water surface; no planting needed.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Reduces algae by blocking light and absorbing excess nutrients.

  • Creates a natural, lush surface for ponds and outdoor water features.

  • Provides refuge for fry, shrimp, and aquatic critters.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as a small portion of loose,  Lettuce rosettes (approximately 1–2 plants, depending on size and season).

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Water Lettuce is sensitive to frost and should not be used in outdoor settings during freezing temperatures. In tropical and subtropical climates, this plant can spread rapidly. Always monitor and maintain responsibly.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Alabama (AL), California (CA), Florida (FL), Louisiana (LA), Mississippi (MS), South Carolina (SC), Texas (TX), Wisconsin (WI), Ohio (OH), Maine (ME), Puerto Rico (PR).

Please respect local regulations and do not release this plant into natural waterways. Always follow state laws and practice responsible plant care.
