products.title: Alternanthera Rosanervig
products.body_html:
Alternanthera Rosanervig is a captivating aquatic plant celebrated for its striking pink veins and vibrant coloration. This unique plant is a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts, adding a splash of color and dynamic contrast to any freshwater aquarium.


Key Features:

  • Distinctive Veining: Alternanthera Rosanervig features bright green leaves with prominent pink veins, creating a stunning visual effect that enhances any aquascape.
  • Vibrant Coloration: Under optimal conditions, the leaves can develop shades of pink, red, and even deep magenta, making this plant a focal point in your aquarium.

Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Requires moderate to high lighting to maintain its vibrant colors and healthy growth. Adequate lighting will highlight the plant's unique veining and overall coloration.
  • CO2 and Nutrients: CO2 supplementation and regular fertilization with both macro and micronutrients are beneficial for enhancing the plant's growth and vibrant colors. A nutrient-rich substrate is also recommended.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0-7.5) and soft to moderately hard water. Ideal water temperature ranges from 22-28°C (72-82°F).

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The striking pink veins and colorful leaves of Alternanthera Rosanervig add a dramatic and beautiful element to any aquarium, creating a vibrant and dynamic underwater landscape.
  • Versatility: Suitable for various aquascaping styles, this plant can be used as a midground or background plant, providing depth and contrast in your aquarium layout.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides excellent cover and hiding spots for fish and shrimp, contributing to a more natural and secure environment for your aquatic inhabitants.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Pruning: Regular pruning helps maintain the plant's shape and encourages bushier growth. Trimmed stems can be replanted to propagate new plants.
  • Planting: Can be planted directly in the substrate or anchored to rocks and driftwood, offering flexibility in aquascaping.

Conclusion:

Alternanthera Rosanervig is a stunning and versatile plant that can significantly enhance the beauty of your aquarium. Its unique pink veins, vibrant coloration, and ease of care make it an excellent choice for both novice and experienced aquarists. Create a breathtaking underwater landscape with this remarkable plant and enjoy the vivid splash of color it brings to your aquarium.

products.title: Hydrocotyle Tripartita Mini
products.body_html:
Hydrocotyle Tripartita 'Mini' – Fast-Spreading Foreground Carpet Plant

Hydrocotyle Tripartita 'Mini', also known simply as Mini Hydrocotyle, is a popular carpeting plant in the aquascaping world, known for its tiny clover-like leaves, dense growth, and fast-spreading runners. This vibrant foreground plant delivers a lush, textured appearance that instantly elevates the look of any planted aquarium or nature-style aquascape.

Key Features & Benefits

  • Scientific Name: Hydrocotyle Tripartita 'Mini'

  • Common Name: Mini Hydrocotyle, Japanese Pennywort Mini

  • Leaf Shape: Small, bright green, clover-like leaves

  • Growth Style: Low-growing, creeping carpet with horizontal runners

  • Lighting Needs: Medium to high light for compact, lush growth

  • CO₂ Requirements: Strongly recommended for dense, fast spreading

  • Placement: Foreground or accent areas in aquascapes

  • Propagation: Easily propagated by trimming and replanting runners

  • Ideal Use: Perfect for nature aquariums, Iwagumi layouts, nano tanks

Why Choose Hydrocotyle Tripartita 'Mini'?

Hydrocotyle Tripartita 'Mini' solves the challenge of achieving a lush, fast-growing carpet without the fragility of more delicate species. Its compact size makes it especially appealing for nano aquariums or detailed foreground work, while its rapid horizontal growth ensures quicker coverage of open substrate areas.

This plant thrives in CO₂-injected setups with moderate to strong lighting, producing tight, vibrant green carpets that add depth and softness to hardscape features like rocks and driftwood. It also responds well to trimming, encouraging bushier growth and easy propagation—making it a favorite among both professional aquascapers and enthusiastic beginners.

Whether you're crafting a minimalist Iwagumi or a vibrant nature aquarium, Hydrocotyle Tripartita 'Mini' brings contrast, coverage, and visual balance to your underwater world.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Green Gecko
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko' commonly known as Cryptocoryne Green Gecko, is a stunning aquarium plant known for its lighter green coloration and unique leaf texture. This variety is ideal for adding a touch of vibrant green to the foreground and midground areas in your aquarium, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko'
  • Common Name: Cryptocoryne Green Gecko
  • Family: Araceae
  • Leaf Color: Light green
  • Growth Form: Compact, rosette

Unique Characteristics

Cryptocoryne Green Gecko is prized for its bright, light green leaves, which provide a striking contrast against the darker green foliage typically found in aquariums. Its unique leaf texture adds visual interest and depth, making it perfect for creating intricate and visually captivating aquascapes.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure the best growth and vibrant color for Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko' provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Low to moderate intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial but not essential
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 22-28°C (72-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for and is suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure low to moderate light levels to maintain its vibrant light green coloration.
  • CO2 Injection: Regular CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and color intensity.
  • Trimming: Minimal pruning is required; simply remove any decaying leaves to maintain plant health.
  • Fertilization: Occasional fertilization supports vibrant growth and overall health.

Aquascaping Uses

Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko' is versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Foreground Plant: Ideal for creating a lush, green carpet in the foreground area.
  • Midground Plant: Can be used to add a touch of green to the midground area.
  • Accent Plant: Its bright light green leaves make it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The vibrant light green leaves of Cryptocoryne Green Gecko provide excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko' is straightforward. This plant propagates through runners. Simply separate the runners and replant them in the substrate, where they will develop roots and grow into new plants.

Cryptocoryne Wendtii 'Green Gecko' is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a vibrant and eye-catching plant. Its stunning light green leaves, compact growth, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

  • Sold as a small bare-root plants
  • Young plants grow in a controlled laboratory environment

products.title: Ammannia Crassicaulis
products.body_html:
🌿 Ammannia Crassicaulis – Bronze-Red Stem Beauty

Ammannia crassicaulis (formerly sold under the name Nesaea) is an elegant stem plant admired for its graceful form and rich, warm coloration. At Aquarium Plants Factory®, we cultivate this species in its emersed form, where the leaves appear green. Once transitioned to submerged growth under intense lighting, CO₂ injection, and nutrient-rich conditions, the plant develops stunning bronze, orange, and red tones, making it a beautiful focal plant for advanced aquascapes.

While similar to Ammannia gracilis, this variety displays a softer, golden-bronze hue instead of bright red. It thrives best when stems are spaced apart to allow proper water movement and light penetration.

🌟 Key Features

• Scientific Name: Ammannia crassicaulis
• Common Name: Red Ammannia
• Family Name: Lythraceae
• Native To: Africa and Madagascar
• Growth Form: Upright stem plant with narrow lanceolate leaves
• Coloration: Green (emersed) → Bronze-Red (submerged, high light)
• Lighting: Intense lighting required for best color
• Requirements: Demanding – requires CO₂ and nutrient-rich substrate
• Growth Rate: Moderately fast under good conditions

🌱 Growth & Propagation

• Lighting Needs: High to intense lighting for vivid coloration
• CO₂ & Fertilization: Strongly recommended for lush and vibrant growth
• Spacing: Avoid crowding; allow space for light and flow between stems
• Substrate: Prefers nutrient-rich substrate for strong root development
• Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, cultivated emersed at Aquarium Plants Factory®

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Provides a warm bronze-red contrast in planted aquariums
• Ideal for mid to background placement in Dutch or Nature-style layouts
• Adds texture, height, and color variation to aquascapes

📦 What You’ll Receive

• Fresh Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots.
• Fresh Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ stems bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
• Fresh Cut Potted: About 6+ stems potted in a net pot with rockwool, providing stability and healthy growth.

⚠️ Transition Note

When transitioning from emersed to submerged growth, older leaves may melt back. This is normal as the plant adapts. New underwater growth will soon appear with brilliant bronze-red coloration under strong light and CO₂ conditions.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️

Be a responsible aquatic plant owner. Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Water Hyacinth
products.body_html:
🌿 Water Hyacinth – Floating Pond Plant

Water Hyacinth (Eichhornia crassipes) is a fast-growing floating aquatic plant admired for its striking lavender-blue flowers and lush, glossy green foliage. This plant is highly effective at improving pond health while adding a vibrant tropical feel to water gardens.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Eichhornia crassipes

  • Type: Free-floating aquatic plant

  • Leaves: Rounded, glossy, bright green with spongy stalks that allow them to float

  • Flowers: Beautiful lavender to violet blooms with a yellow accent spot (seasonal)

  • Growth Habit: Rapid growth, multiplies easily under warm, sunny conditions

  • Benefits: Provides shade, shelter for fish, and absorbs excess nutrients from the water

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Full sun to partial shade (6+ hours recommended for best flowering)

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Not required — absorbs nutrients directly from the water

  • Growth Method: Free-floating, no planting or substrate required

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No — strictly aquatic and free-floating

🖼️ Aquascaping & Pond Benefits

  • 🌞 Natural Shade: Large floating leaves help reduce algae growth by shading pond surfaces

  • 🐟 Fish Shelter: Roots provide hiding spots and breeding areas for koi, goldfish, and other pond fish

  • 🌊 Water Quality: Excellent at filtering and removing excess nutrients, helping keep water clear

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as fresh, healthy Water Hyacinth plants carefully inspected before shipping:

  • Sold as a portion with 2-3 leaf
  • Beautiful flower when established

⚠️ Disclaimer

Water Hyacinth grows quickly in warm conditions. While beneficial in controlled ponds, it can become invasive if released into natural waterways. Always manage growth responsibly.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium or pond plants into the wild.

products.title: Alternanthera Ocipus
products.body_html:
Alternanthera Ocipus is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. It is native to South America and is known for its attractive foliage, which can range in color from green to red, pink, or purple. It is often grown as an ornamental plant in gardens, containers, and as a houseplant.

The plant grows to a height of 30-40 cm and has a spreading habit, with delicate, oval-shaped leaves. It produces small, white or pink flowers in clusters that bloom in summer. Alternanthera Ocipus prefers moist, well-drained soils in a warm, sunny location. It is relatively low maintenance and can be propagated from stem cuttings or by division.

products.title: Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice]
products.body_html:
Tropical water lilies are a beautiful addition to any pond or water garden, and they come in a wide variety of colors and sizes. When purchasing Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice], you will receive a selection of water lilies chosen by the grower based on availability and the best performers for your growing zone.

The packages typically include a variety of colors, such as pink, red, white, and yellow, and may also include various sizes ranging from small to large. The grower will choose the best selection for you based on the zone that you live in, as well as the specific conditions of your pond or water garden.

Tropical water lilies are called "tropical" because they are native to warmer climates and cannot tolerate cold temperatures. They require full sun and warm water to thrive, making them ideal for warmer regions. Tropical water lilies are also known for their large, showy blooms that can reach up to 12 inches in diameter.

When you receive your Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice], you should follow the instructions that come with them for planting and care. It is essential to make sure that you choose the right location for your water lilies, as they require warm water and at least 6 hours of direct sunlight each day to thrive.

Overall, purchasing a Tropical Water Lilies Packages [Grower's Choice] is an excellent way to add beauty and color to your water garden or pond, and you can trust that the grower has chosen the best selection of water lilies for your growing zone and conditions. However, if you live in a colder region, it is important to note that tropical water lilies may not survive the winter outdoors, and you may need to bring them indoors or replace them with hardy water lilies.

  • This combo package contains 6 different tropical water lilies plants
  • We don't label the species. Randomly pick, can't request a specific species

Disclaimer: Pictures are not the actual plants you will receive, but a sample representation. Unless specific, we don't guarantee aquatic plants will be free of pests, pesticides, grow emerged, or submerge.

Scientific Name

 Nymphaea species

Soil type

 Clay soil

Color

 Varies

Sunlight

 Full sun or partial shade

PLANTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR WATER LILY

There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

products.title: Dwarf Hairgrass
products.body_html:
Eleocharis pusilla, commonly known as Dwarf Hairgrass in the planted aquarium community, is a grass-like plant that is perfect for creating lush carpet foregrounds in fish tanks and aquariums. This easy-to-care-for plant adds a natural, meadow-like appearance to any aquascape. Eleocharis Pusilla can be compared with the popular Eleocharis Acicularis and Eleocharis Acicularis 'Mini'.

Key Features:

  • Grass-Like Appearance: Features fine, grass-like leaves that form a dense carpet, enhancing the natural look of your aquarium.
  • Small Flower Spike: When grown above water, Eleocharis pusilla produces a very small head or spike of flowers, only a few millimeters long.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions.
  • Water Parameters: Adaptable to a range of freshwater conditions, making it suitable for various aquarium setups.

Propagation:

  • Runners: Easily propagates through runners, quickly creating a thick, green carpet in your aquarium.
  • Growth Rate: Moderate growth rate, allowing for manageable maintenance and a continuously lush appearance.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a beautiful, natural carpet effect to the foreground of your aquarium, enhancing the overall aquascape.
  • Easy Care: Ideal for both beginner and experienced aquarists due to its low maintenance requirements.

Market Information:

  • Trade Name: Eleocharis pusilla has been sold as Eleocharis parvula in the aquarium trade for quite some time.
  • Species Clarification: Most Eleocharis parvula sold in the market is actually Eleocharis pusilla, as Eleocharis parvula is a brackish water species not suitable for freshwater aquariums.

Conclusion:

Eleocharis pusilla (Dwarf Hairgrass) is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to add a lush, grass-like carpet to their freshwater aquariums. Its easy propagation through runners and ability to thrive in various conditions make it a favorite among the aquarium community. By choosing Eleocharis pusilla, you ensure a vibrant, natural aquascape that enhances the beauty of your underwater environment.

Transform your aquarium with the elegant and natural beauty of Eleocharis pusilla and enjoy a thriving, verdant aquascape.

products.title: Christmas Moss Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Vesicularia montagnei, commonly known as Christmas Moss or Xmas Moss, is a beloved aquatic plant among aquarists for its unique, festive appearance and versatile growth habits. This moss features a slow growth rate and thrives best with the addition of CO2 and medium to high lighting conditions. When provided with these optimal conditions, Christmas Moss exhibits lush, green fronds that resemble the branches of a Christmas tree.

For best results, Christmas Moss should be attached to rocks, stones, or driftwood within the aquarium. Over time, it will develop roots and naturally adhere to these surfaces, creating a beautiful, natural aquascape. Like other types of moss, Christmas Moss adds a soft, textured look to the aquarium, making it a perfect choice for aquascaping.

In addition to its aesthetic appeal, Christmas Moss offers practical benefits for your tank's ecosystem. It provides hiding places and breeding grounds for small fish and shrimp, enhancing the biodiversity of your aquarium.

Ideal for both beginners and experienced hobbyists, Vesicularia montagnei (Christmas Moss) is a must-have for creating a vibrant, healthy, and visually appealing underwater environment. Whether you're designing a lush, green aquascape or seeking to add a touch of natural beauty to your tank, Christmas Moss is a perfect choice.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Flamingo
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne Flamingo is a highly sought-after aquatic plant known for its striking pink and white variegated leaves, making it a standout addition to any freshwater aquarium. This unique plant, often referred to simply as "Crypt Flamingo" adds a touch of vibrant color and elegance to aquascapes, enhancing the overall aesthetic appeal.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Coloration: Features stunning pink and white variegated leaves that create a beautiful contrast in your aquarium.
  • Compact Growth: Grows in a compact form, making it ideal for foreground or midground placement in aquascapes.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Prefers moderate to high lighting conditions to maintain its vibrant coloration.
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Thrives in a nutrient-rich substrate, which supports healthy growth and vibrant leaf color.
  • Water Parameters: Adaptable to a range of water conditions, but prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a unique and colorful element to aquascapes, enhancing the visual appeal of your aquarium with its bright pink and white leaves.
  • Versatile Placement: Suitable for both foreground and midground placement, providing flexibility in aquascaping designs.
  • Easy Care: Known for its hardiness, making it suitable for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Regular Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages new growth.
  • Stable Environment: Ensure consistent water quality and nutrient availability for optimal growth.

Conclusion:

Cryptocoryne Flamingo is an exceptional choice for aquarists looking to add a splash of color and uniqueness to their freshwater aquariums. Its vibrant pink and white variegated leaves and easy care requirements make it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts. By incorporating Cryptocoryne Flamingo into your aquarium, you can enjoy a stunning and dynamic underwater landscape.

products.title: Rotala Wallichii Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Rotala wallichii is a beautiful stem plant that grows in South-East Asian waters that are soft and slightly acidic. It blooms with flowers that are bright purple. Even though it's not easy to grow, this plant is very popular.

Rotala wallichii was a stem plant with small, finely-toothed, reddish-pink to pink leaves. It looks best when there are a lot of stems in a group. It can also be used in the foreground and in smaller tanks if it is cut at the right time. Adding CO2 to the air helps plants grow a lot. It likes water that is soft and a bit acidic.

Its vibrant, wavy foliage offers rich texture and a calm visual rhythm—perfect for shaded areas, driftwood accents, or low-tech setups.

products.title: Eleocharis Acicularis
products.body_html:
Eleocharis acicularis, commonly known as Hairgrass, is a delicate, grass-like plant perfect for creating lush carpet foregrounds in fish tanks and aquariums. With its fine, needle-pointed leaves, this plant typically reaches a height of 5-20 cm, making it ideal for adding a natural look to your aquascape. We also carry a mini version of Eleocharis Acicularis, behold Eleocharis Acicularis 'Mini'.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.5 to 7.5) and temperatures between 68°F to 82°F (20°C to 28°C).

Propagation:

Additional Options:

Conclusion:

Eleocharis acicularis is an excellent choice for aquarists seeking to add a delicate, grass-like carpet to their aquarium. Its fine, needle-pointed leaves and easy propagation through runners make it a popular choice for creating natural-looking aquascapes. Whether you choose the standard or 'Mini' version, Eleocharis acicularis will enhance the beauty and functionality of your aquarium, providing both aesthetic appeal and practical benefits for your aquatic life.

products.title: Bucephalandra Pandora Queen
products.body_html:
🌸 Bucephalandra ‘Pandora Queen’ – Iridescent Royal Jewel of Borneo

Bucephalandra ‘Pandora Queen’ is a magnificent and rare Bucephalandra variety admired for its striking iridescence and regal appearance. Native to the riverbanks of Borneo, this elegant plant showcases medium-sized leaves that shimmer in shades of blue, violet, and emerald depending on lighting and angle—earning it the name “Pandora Queen.” Its graceful form and radiant color make it a centerpiece in any aquascape, while its hardy, slow-growing nature ensures easy care and long-lasting beauty.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. ‘Pandora Queen’

  • Common Name: Buce Pandora Queen

  • Royal Iridescence: Leaves display stunning blue, green, and violet tones that shift under high or RGB lighting.

  • Elegant Leaf Shape: Medium-length, slightly wavy leaves with a soft metallic luster.

  • Growth Form: Compact clumping habit, perfect for driftwood, stones, or as a midground accent.

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Tolerates a wide range of aquarium conditions; suitable for both beginners and collectors.

  • Epiphytic Nature: Easily attaches to hardscape—rhizome should not be buried.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium light; stronger lighting enhances iridescent colors.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Optional, but recommended for faster growth and more vibrant tones.

  • Growth Method: Rhizome-based; allow the rhizome to rest above the substrate.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required—absorbs nutrients directly from the water column.

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – excellent for paludariums, terrariums, and emersed displays.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds a luxurious iridescent glow and royal color shift to planted aquariums.

  • Perfect for creating contrast and highlights among green or red aquatic species.

  • Excellent centerpiece for nano and mid-sized aquascapes.

  • Enhances both naturalistic and collector-style layouts with depth and visual richness.

📍 Cultivation & Sustainability

At Aquarium Plants Factory’s nursery, all of our Bucephalandra species are 100% farm-raised—never taken from the wild. Each plant originates ex-vitro (grown from sterile lab culture) before being transitioned to hydroponic growth in our controlled environment. This ensures exceptional quality, pest-free plants, and sustainable preservation of Borneo’s native ecosystems.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose Bare-Root or Potted plants:

  • 1 Bare-Root: Approximately 5+ leaves

  • 5 Bare-Root: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Bare-Root: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 1 Potted: Approximately 10+ leaves

  • 5 Potted: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 10 Potted: Approximately 100+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra species, minor leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new water parameters. This is normal and temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Sold as loose Bare-Root or Potted plants:

  • 1 Bare-Root: Approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Bare-Root: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Bare-Root: Approximately 50+ leaves total

  • 1 Potted: Approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Potted: Approximately 25+ leaves total

  • 10 Potted: Approximately 50+ leaves total

⚠️ Disclaimer

Like most Bucephalandra species, minor leaf melt may occur after introduction as the plant adjusts to new water parameters. This is normal and temporary—new submersed leaves will regrow from the rhizome once the plant stabilizes.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: APF Plant Weights
products.body_html:
Despite being commonly called 'lead weights' in the hobby, our weights are crafted from a Zinc Magnesium alloy, making them entirely harmless to every resident of your aquarium.
Use plant weight to wrap around the base, and bottom part of the plants. For best results, use an  APF White Foam™ to protect the stem of aquarium plants from direct contact with a lead weight. Bury the base of aquarium plants in the substrate to promote root growth. 
  • APF Plant Weights for Aquarium Plants
  • Safe for Aquarium use, not toxic to fish, snail, or shrimp
  • Easy to keep plants weighted, anchors down
  • Won't rust, flexible & easy to bent, reuse, and fully recyclable
  • Tips: Use more lead weight to anchor the larger plant

products.title: Eleocharis Pusilla Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Eleocharis pusilla, commonly known as Dwarf Hairgrass in the planted aquarium community, is a popular grass-like plant ideal for creating lush carpet foregrounds in fish tanks and aquariums. Notably, it has often been sold as Eleocharis parvula in the aquarium trade. However, it is important to note that almost all Eleocharis parvula available in the market is actually Eleocharis pusilla because Eleocharis parvula is a brackish water species, unsuitable for freshwater aquariums.

Key Features:

  • Grass-Like Appearance: Features fine, grass-like leaves that form a dense, attractive carpet, enhancing the natural look of your aquarium.
  • Small Flower Spike: When grown above water, Eleocharis pusilla produces very small heads or spikes of flowers, only a few millimeters long.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions.
  • Water Parameters: Adaptable to a range of freshwater conditions, making it suitable for various aquarium setups.

Propagation:

  • Runners: Easily propagates through runners, quickly creating a thick, green carpet in your aquarium.
  • Growth Rate: Moderate growth rate, allowing for manageable maintenance and a continuously lush appearance.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a beautiful, natural carpet effect to the foreground of your aquarium, enhancing the overall aquascape.
  • Easy Care: Ideal for both beginner and experienced aquarists due to its low maintenance requirements.

Conclusion:

Eleocharis pusilla (Dwarf Hairgrass) is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to add a lush, grass-like carpet to their freshwater aquariums. Its easy propagation through runners and ability to thrive in various conditions make it a favorite among the aquarium community. By choosing Eleocharis pusilla, you ensure a vibrant, natural aquascape that enhances the beauty of your underwater environment.

Transform your aquarium with the elegant and natural beauty of Eleocharis pusilla and enjoy a thriving, verdant aquascape.

Eleocharis Pusilla can be compared with the popular Eleocharis Acicularis and Eleocharis Acicularis 'Mini'.

  • Sold as a Tissue Culture plants with vitamin liquid/gel
  • Bag size: Approx. 4 inches (yield many young plants)
  • Sustainable growth aquarium plants species
  • Tissue Culture Technology | 100% Free from Snail, Algae, Pathogen, and Bacteria plus Extended Shelf Life on all in Vitro Plants

products.title: Dwarf Hairgrass Mini
products.body_html:
Eleocharis acicularis 'Mini', also known as Eleocharis sp. 'Mini', Dwarf Hairgrass 'Mini', Eleocharis sp. Belem, or Japanese Hairgrass, is a highly sought-after aquatic plant for creating stunning foreground carpets in aquariums. This variant of hairgrass stays exceptionally compact and short, reaching approximately 3-5 cm in height, making it the shortest growing hairgrass available.

Key Features:

  • Compact Growth: Stays short at approximately 3-5 cm, making it ideal for creating dense, lush carpets.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives in very high light conditions, which encourages the compact and curling leaf growth.

Propagation:

  • Runners: Easily propagates via runners, allowing it to quickly spread and form a dense, lush lawn.
  • Growth Rate: Fast-growing under optimal conditions, making it perfect for creating a vibrant carpet.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a beautiful, natural carpet effect to the foreground of your aquarium, enhancing the overall aquascape design.
  • Low Maintenance: Its compact growth and easy propagation make it suitable for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

Comparison:

  • Shortest Hairgrass: Currently the shortest growing hairgrass (3-5 cm), even smaller than the popular Eleocharis pusilla (Parvula).

Conclusion:

Eleocharis acicularis 'Mini' (Dwarf Hairgrass 'Mini') is an excellent choice for aquarists seeking to create a stunning, dense carpet in their aquariums. Its compact size, unique leaf shape, and ease of propagation make it a standout addition to any aquascape. Whether you're aiming for a lush lawn or a detailed foreground, Eleocharis acicularis 'Mini' provides the perfect solution for your aquascaping needs.

Transform your aquarium with the elegant and compact beauty of Eleocharis acicularis 'Mini' and enjoy a vibrant, natural underwater landscape.

products.title: Littorella Uniflora Vitro Culture (American Micro Sword)
products.body_html:
Littorella Uniflora, commonly known as American Micro Sword or US Micro Sword, is a versatile and attractive plant native to the United States. This grass-like plant is perfect for terrariums, ponds, and aquariums, making it a popular choice among aquarists and hobbyists.

Key Features:

  • Grass-like Appearance: Resembles the well-known Lilaeopsis in appearance, adding a natural, green touch to your aquatic or terrestrial setups.
  • Versatile Growth: Suitable for terrariums, ponds, and aquariums, providing flexibility in various environments.
  • Easy Propagation: Propagates easily via runners, allowing it to develop a full lawn carpet over time.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Planting: Each rosette can be planted a few millimeters apart to encourage spreading and growth.
  • Lighting: Thrives in moderate to high lighting conditions, which help maintain its vibrant green color.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5) and temperatures between 68°F to 78°F (20°C to 26°C).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Ideal for Carpets: Excellent for creating lush carpet effects in the foreground of fish tanks and aquariums.
  • Natural Aesthetic: Adds a natural and appealing look to your aquatic environment, enhancing the overall visual appeal.
  • Foreground Placement: Perfect for foreground planting, providing a soft, green lawn-like appearance.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Regular Trimming: Trim regularly to maintain a neat and uniform carpet.
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Grows well in nutrient-rich substrates that provide essential nutrients for healthy growth.
  • Moderate Maintenance: Requires moderate care, making it suitable for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

Conclusion:

Littorella Uniflora (American Micro Sword, US Micro Sword) is an excellent choice for aquarists and hobbyists looking to create a lush, green carpet in their aquariums, terrariums, or ponds. Its grass-like appearance and easy propagation make it a standout addition to any setup. By providing the right conditions and care, you can enjoy the natural beauty and versatility of Littorella Uniflora in your aquatic or terrestrial environments.

Enhance your aquarium, terrarium, or pond with the natural charm of Littorella Uniflora and enjoy the lush, green landscape it brings to your space.

products.title: Ludwigia Pantanal Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Ludwigia Pantanal Vitro Culture® – Rare & Striking Stem Plant

Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'Pantanal', commonly known as Ludwigia Pantanal, is one of the most coveted stem plants in the aquascaping hobby. Originating from the Pantanal region of South America, this rare species is admired for its narrow, needle-like leaves and breathtaking pinkish to fiery red coloration when grown under optimal conditions.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we offer this rarity in our premium Vitro Culture® 4-inch sealed bag—ensuring you receive a 100% pest-free, algae-free, and disease-free plant. Cultivated in sterile laboratory conditions, Ludwigia Pantanal arrives healthy, clean, and ready to thrive in your aquarium.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'Pantanal'

  • Common Name: Ludwigia Pantanal

  • Origin: Pantanal wetlands, South America

  • Appearance: Narrow, needle-like leaves arranged in whorls; coloration ranges from pink to deep red depending on lighting and nutrients

  • Growth Habit: Tall, upright stem plant that forms dense, bushy groups in aquascapes

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting Needs: High lighting required for vibrant red and pink tones

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Strongly recommended for optimal growth and coloration

  • Water Parameters: Prefers soft, slightly acidic water with good circulation

  • Growth Method: Submersed stem plant with fast vertical growth

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for healthy development and vivid colors

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – but shows its best coloration and leaf structure submerged

🌟 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • 100% pest-free, algae-free, disease-free

  • Grown in sterile lab conditions for maximum purity

  • Healthy, vigorous starter plants for guaranteed success

  • Longer shelf life and easier transition into aquariums

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds dramatic red coloration to aquascapes

  • Serves as a focal background plant with striking contrast

  • Perfect for high-tech aquascapes aiming for professional-level layouts

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag of Ludwigia Pantanal

  • Each bag contains multiple healthy plantlets, ready for immediate planting

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Ludwigia Pantanal is considered a challenging plant, best suited for experienced aquascapers. It requires high light, CO₂, and a nutrient-rich environment to display its stunning red hues. Expect a short adaptation period after planting.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please check your local regulations before introducing new plants. Never release aquarium plants into natural waterways.

Bacopa monnieri, also known as Water Hyssop, is a popular aquarium stem plant known for its rounded, vibrant green leaves and upright, bushy growth. Cultivated in a sterile Vitro Culture environment, this 4-inch bag yields numerous young plantlets that are ready to establish and flourish in a wide variety of freshwater aquariums.

Ideal for background or midground placement, Bacopa monnieri is highly adaptable and thrives in both low-tech and high-tech setups. Its crisp, green foliage adds vertical texture and a fresh, natural feel to planted tanks, while also serving as a gentle hiding space for small fish and shrimp.

  • Light Demand: Low to medium

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required, but enhances growth and density

  • Growth Rate: Medium

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

products.title: Cyperus Helferi Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Cyperus Helferi – Vitro Culture Aquarium Grass Plant

Cyperus helferi is a graceful grass-like aquatic plant admired for its long, narrow, bright green leaves that gently sway with water movement. Native to Southeast Asia, this elegant species brings natural height and flowing texture to aquascapes, making it an excellent choice for midground to background placement in planted aquariums.

Unlike many typical rosette plants, Cyperus helferi develops tall ribbon-like foliage that creates a natural underwater grass effect. When planted in groups, it forms beautiful clumps that move softly with the aquarium current, adding life and depth to aquascape layouts.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Cyperus helferi
Common Name: Helfer’s Cyperus / Aquarium Umbrella Grass
Family: Cyperaceae

• Elegant grass-like leaves that sway naturally in water flow
• Bright green coloration that complements most aquascapes
• Unique rosette plant with tall flowing foliage
• Ideal for midground to background planting in aquariums

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting Needs: Medium to high lighting
CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ recommended for optimal growth; benefits from nutrient-rich substrate and fertilizers
Growth Method: Rosette plant propagated through runners and division
Growth Rate: Medium

Nutrient Substrate: Recommended
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Under stable aquarium conditions, Cyperus helferi develops longer flowing leaves and gradually forms beautiful clusters. As the plant matures, it may produce side shoots that can be separated and replanted.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Creates natural flowing movement in aquascapes
• Excellent plant for adding vertical height and soft texture
• Perfect for midground or background placement in planted tanks

Cyperus helferi is especially popular in nature-style aquascapes, where its long grass-like leaves help create a natural riverbank or meadow effect.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Cyperus helferi Vitro Culture® cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient pack sizes:

products.title: Pogostemon Deccanensis Vitro Culture (Pogostemon Erectus)
products.body_html:
🌿 Pogostemon deccanensis Vitro Culture® – Indian Star Plant with Lush Needle-Like Foliage

Enhance your aquarium with the striking beauty of Pogostemon deccanensis, also known as the Indian Star plant. Formerly mislabeled in the hobby as Pogostemon erectus, this stunning species has since been correctly identified as Pogostemon deccanensis. Now cultivated using our Vitro Culture® method, it arrives completely pest-free, algae-free, and pesticide-free, ensuring a clean and healthy start for any aquascape.

This stem plant is known for its fine, needle-like foliage that resembles conifer branches, creating compact vertical clusters of vibrant green. Its lush, feathery texture adds motion and contrast when planted among broader-leaved or red-hued plants—making it a dynamic choice for both midground and background areas of the aquarium.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Pogostemon deccanensis

  • Synonym: Pogostemon erectus (obsolete)

  • Common Name: Indian Star Plant

  • Leaf Appearance: Fine, needle-like green foliage, dense and bushy

  • Growth Form: Upright vertical stems with compact foliage clusters

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Difficulty Level: Easy to Medium

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high for compact and healthy growth

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ beneficial but not strictly required; regular fertilization promotes optimal color and density

  • Growth Method: Submerged only

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates soft, textured clusters resembling miniature conifers

  • Adds vibrant light green contrast to darker or red plant species

  • Great for midground structure or soft background layering

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple healthy Pogostemon deccanensis plantlets—ready to rinse, acclimate, and plant in your aquarium.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Newly introduced Pogostemon deccanensis may take time to adjust to submerged conditions. Yellowing or melt during early stages is common and will improve with stable conditions and light.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Never release Pogostemon deccanensis—or any aquarium plant—into natural waterways. Doing so can disrupt local ecosystems. Dispose of unwanted portions responsibly to help preserve aquatic biodiversity.

products.title: American Frogbit
products.body_html:
🌿 American Frogbit – Graceful Floating Aquatic Plant

American Frogbit (Limnobium spongia) is a beautiful, easy-to-care-for floating plant native to North America. With its round, lily-like leaves and dangling feathery roots, it adds a touch of elegance to ponds, aquariums, and paludariums. This versatile species thrives in still or slow-moving waters, offering both aesthetic beauty and functional benefits for your aquatic ecosystem.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Limnobium spongia

  • Common Name: American Frogbit

  • Growth Form: Floating plant with circular green leaves and fine, trailing roots

  • Low Maintenance: Grows well under a wide range of light and water conditions

  • Aquarium & Pond Friendly: Suitable for both indoor aquariums and outdoor ponds

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium light for steady growth

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Not required but will benefit from occasional liquid fertilization

  • Growth Method: Floating (no substrate needed)

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Shade & Shelter: Offers cover for fish and shrimp, helping reduce stress

  • Nutrient Absorption: Helps control algae by absorbing excess nutrients

  • Natural Aesthetic: Creates a calm, natural water surface look

📦 What You’ll Receive:
Sold as a small portion – approx. 1–2 individual plants

⚠️ Disclaimer:
As a floating plant, American Frogbit may spread quickly in nutrient-rich water. Regular thinning may be required to maintain light penetration for plants below.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Please do not release American Frogbit or any aquatic plants into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings responsibly. Check your local regulations before purchase.

featured_image.alt: Small portion of American Frogbit, 1–2 plants, ready for aquarium or pond use

products.title: Anubias Broad White Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Anubias Broad White Vitro Culture® – Rare Variegated Aquarium Plant

Anubias Broad White Vitro Culture® is a rare and highly sought-after variegated aquatic plant admired for its striking white and green marbled leaves. This beautiful Anubias variety features broad foliage with dramatic variegation that creates stunning contrast in planted aquariums and aquascapes.

Known for its hardy nature and slow, compact growth, Anubias Broad White is an excellent choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists. Its bold coloration makes it a standout centerpiece when attached to driftwood, rocks, or decorative hardscape in freshwater aquariums.

Each plant is cultivated using Vitro Culture® tissue culture technology, ensuring it arrives 100% pest-free, algae-free, and snail-free. Grown in sterile laboratory conditions, these plants provide the cleanest and healthiest start for your aquarium.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Anubias barteri var. Broad White
Common Name: Anubias Broad White
Plant Type: Rhizome / Epiphyte

• Rare White Variegation with beautiful marbled white and green leaves
• Broad Leaf Structure that creates bold visual impact in aquascapes
• Extremely Hardy & Adaptable plant suitable for most freshwater aquariums
• Vitro Culture® Lab-Grown Plants free from pests, algae, and snails

🌱 Growth & Care

Lighting: Low to medium (tolerates higher light)
CO₂: Not required
Growth Rate: Slow

Placement: Foreground to midground
Propagation: Rhizome division

For best results, attach Anubias Broad White to driftwood, rocks, or aquarium décor rather than burying the rhizome in substrate. Over time, the plant will produce beautiful clusters of variegated leaves that enhance the natural look of your aquascape.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Adds rare white variegation rarely seen in aquarium plants
• Perfect for nature aquariums and aquascaping layouts
• Excellent for nano tanks, shrimp tanks, and planted aquariums

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Anubias Broad White cultivated using Vitro Culture® tissue culture technology and sealed in sterile media for optimal quality and transport.

Pack Options Available:

1 Bag: One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing healthy Anubias Broad White plantlets.
5 Bags: Five 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bags containing healthy Anubias Broad White plantlets.
10 Bags: Ten 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bags containing healthy Anubias Broad White plantlets.

Each bag contains multiple young plantlets ready to rinse, separate, acclimate, and plant in your aquarium or high-humidity emersed setup.

⚠️ Tissue Culture Notice

Plants grown in tissue culture are produced in sterile laboratory environments and may appear smaller than mature aquarium plants. Once introduced into the aquarium with proper lighting and nutrients, they will gradually adapt and develop into fully established plants.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner.

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted in some U.S. states. Always check your local regulations before purchasing. Never release aquarium plants into natural waterways.

products.title: Echinodorus Pink Princess Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌸 Echinodorus grisebachii 'Pink Princess' Vitro Culture® – Elegant Rosette in Soft Pink Tones

Echinodorus grisebachii 'Pink Princess', commonly known as the Pink Princess Sword, is a stunning compact sword plant that enchants aquascapers with its delicate blush-pink to soft rose-colored foliage. Cultivated in a 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag, this variety delivers approximately 4 small, healthy plantlets grown in a sterile, algae-free, and snail-free environment—ideal for a clean start in both low-tech and high-tech aquariums.

With its upright, medium-sized rosette and uniquely soft pastel coloration, the Pink Princess Sword adds a gentle touch of color and contrast to planted tanks. Under strong lighting and nutrient-rich substrate, the pink hues become more pronounced, while the plant retains a neat, manageable form ideal for midground placement.

✨ Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Echinodorus grisebachii 'Pink Princess'

  • Common Name: Pink Princess Sword

  • Growth Form: Upright, compact rosette with smooth, lanceolate leaves

  • Coloration: Pale pink to soft rose under strong lighting

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Recommended for optimal coloration

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Placement: Midground to background in smaller tanks

🌿 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

  • Lighting: Medium to high; stronger lighting enhances pink coloration

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate is required for healthy growth

🌊 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • Sterile & Safe: Free from algae, snails, and harmful pathogens

  • High Yield: Each 4-inch bag contains approximately 4 young plantlets

  • Perfect for Sensitive Setups: Safe for shrimp, nano tanks, and aquascaping contests

  • Easy to Plant: Compact plantlets adapt quickly to aquarium conditions

🔁 Propagation & Growth:

  • Propagation: Produces adventitious plantlets on submerged flower stalks

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – ideal for paludariums and high-humidity setups

📦 What You’ll Receive:

  • Vitro Culture® Bag (4-inch):
    You will receive one sealed 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag containing approximately 4 small Pink Princess Sword plantlets, cultivated in a sterile lab environment.
    Simply rinse the nutrient gel under clean water, gently separate the plantlets, and plant them in your substrate.

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Coloration may vary based on lighting intensity, nutrients, and tank conditions. Younger plantlets may appear light green and gradually develop pink tones as they mature. Avoid burying the crown during planting.

featured_image.alt: Echinodorus grisebachii 'Pink Princess', also known as Pink Amazon Sword, is an exquisite and straightforward-to-care-for aquatic plant that requires a nutrient-rich substrate to attain its full potential. Its vibrant pink to reddish coloration and exceptional leaf structure make it a fantastic addition to any aquarium.

products.title: Hygrophila Difformis Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Hygrophila difformis – Water Wisteria

Vitro Culture® Clean Aquarium Plant | Lab-Grown Tissue Culture

Hygrophila difformis, commonly known as Water Wisteria, is a fast-growing and highly adaptable freshwater aquarium plant known for its beautiful lace-like foliage and vibrant green coloration. Native to the Indian subcontinent, this versatile stem plant quickly develops into lush, bushy growth that adds texture and depth to planted aquariums.

Its finely divided leaves create a soft, feathery appearance that contrasts beautifully with broader-leaf plants. Hygrophila difformis is also valued for its ability to absorb excess nutrients, helping stabilize aquarium conditions and reduce algae growth.

This Vitro Culture® lab-grown version offers a pristine start for aquascaping, completely free from algae, pests, snails, and pathogens.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Hygrophila difformis
Common Name: Water Wisteria
Family: Acanthaceae

• Beautiful finely divided green foliage that creates a soft aquascape texture
• Fast-growing stem plant suitable for beginners and advanced aquascapers
• Helps absorb excess nutrients and improve aquarium stability
• Excellent midground or background plant for planted aquariums

🌱 Growth & Propagation

Lighting: Low to Medium (stronger light promotes fuller growth)
CO₂: Not required but enhances growth density
Growth Rate: Fast
Placement: Midground to Background

Hygrophila difformis propagates easily through stem cuttings. Simply trim healthy stems and replant them into the substrate to produce new plants and encourage bushier growth.

Nutrient Substrate: Not Required
Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🧫 Vitro Culture® Advantages

• 100% Pest-Free: No snails, algae, or parasites
• Lab-Grown Quality: Produced in sterile laboratory conditions
• Multiple Plantlets: Each culture contains numerous young plants
• Safe for Sensitive Tanks: Ideal for shrimp tanks and premium aquascapes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

• Creates lush green contrast with delicate feathery foliage
• Adds natural movement and texture to aquascapes
• Helps establish planted tanks quickly due to fast growth

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh Vitro Culture® Hygrophila difformis prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in multiple pack sizes.

Each portion comes in a sealed 4-inch sterile Vitro Culture® bag containing numerous young plantlets grown in nutrient gel.

1 Bag: One 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple healthy plantlets ready for separation and planting.

5 Bags: Five 4-inch Vitro Culture® bags, ideal for filling larger aquascapes or multiple aquariums.

10 Bags: Ten 4-inch Vitro Culture® bags for large aquascaping projects or professional setups.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Tissue culture plants are grown emersed in sterile laboratory conditions. After planting in the aquarium, plants may transition to submerged growth and develop new leaves adapted to underwater conditions.

This transition is normal and indicates healthy acclimation.

products.title: Rotala Sunset
products.body_html:
Rotala ramosior 'Sunset' is a beautiful aquatic plant that adds a vibrant and colorful element to any freshwater aquarium. This plant features long, thin leaves that can range from green, orange-red to purple tone, depending on the light and nutrient conditions. As a slow-growing plant, Rotala ramosior 'Sunset' requires moderate to high lighting and regular fertilization to thrive. Its vibrant colors make it a popular choice for aquascaping, providing a visually appealing contrast to other plants in the tank. In addition to its aesthetic appeal, this plant also provides valuable benefits to aquariums, including oxygenation and nutrient absorption, making it a valuable asset to any aquatic ecosystem. With its stunning appearance and practical benefits, Rotala ramosior 'Sunset' is a prized possession among aquatic plant enthusiasts and a must-have for any planted tank.

There are different Rotala ramosior species on the market, such as Rotala ramosior 'Sunset' or Rotala ramosior 'Florida'. Rotala ramosior 'Sunset' has rounder leaves and Rotala ramosior 'Florida' has more pointy leaves.

products.title: Bacopa Salzmannii
products.body_html:
🌸 Bacopa salzmannii sp. Araguaia (‘Pink’)

Color-shifting stem plant for vibrant aquascapes

Bacopa salzmannii sp. Araguaia (‘Pink’) is a striking stem plant prized for its dynamic color transformation and clean, rounded leaf form. Grown emersed at our Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, it shows green foliage; once submerged, it develops light green to light orange tones that can deepen toward pink to purple under stronger light and dialed-in nutrients. This is a rare collector’s variety with fast growth and refined texture that stands out in mid-ground and background groupings.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bacopa salzmannii

  • Common Name: Bacopa salzmannii ‘Pink’ (Araguaia)

  • Color Shift: Emersed green ➝ submerged light green to light orange, pink, and purple highlights

  • Leaf Shape: Roundish, slightly elliptical leaves for a soft, premium look

  • Growth: Fast—readily forms lush bushes with routine trimming

  • Placement: Midground / Background

  • Rarity: Limited availability in the trade; a standout collector’s plant

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Difficulty: Advanced

  • Lighting: Medium – High (higher light = stronger pink/purple)

  • CO₂: Required for best color and compact growth

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required (root tabs or rich aquasoil recommended)

  • Water Parameters: 72–82 °F (22–28 °C), pH 6.0–7.5

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Fast growth fills space quickly; ideal for color “bands” or accent bushes

🔎 Variant Note

products.title: Cuphea Anagalloidea
products.body_html:
Cuphea anagalloidea is a delicate small stem plant that requires a lot of care.
The brilliant shade of scarlet that may be found on the upper surface of the submerged leaves is brought out to its fullest potential when exposed to bright light. This plant was initially introduced into the aquarium hobby under the name Rotala sp. Araguaia; however, it was later determined that it belongs to the Cuphea species. Cuphea Anagalloidea can only grow vertically. It looks best when used in a group in the middleground as a color accent. The upper edges of the leaves turn a bright crimson to orange-red color under high lighting, but the stem remains green. Sufficient lighting, with plenty CO2 and nutrients, and soft water are all recommended.

products.title: Microsorum Pteropus Trident
products.body_html:
🌿 Java Fern ‘Trident’ – Leptochilus pteropus (Formerly Microsorum pteropus)

Elegant Forked-Leaf Epiphyte for Natural Aquascapes

Leptochilus pteropus ‘Trident’, previously known as Microsorum pteropus ‘Trident’, is a refined and highly sought-after Java Fern variety featuring distinctive forked, trident-shaped leaves. This unique structure creates a flowing, intricate appearance that adds depth, texture, and movement to any aquascape.

As a hardy epiphyte, this plant thrives when attached to driftwood, stones, or decorative hardscape. It requires minimal maintenance and adapts easily to a wide range of aquarium conditions, making it an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced aquascapers.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Leptochilus pteropus ‘Trident’

  • Previous Name: Microsorum pteropus ‘Trident’

  • Common Name: Trident Java Fern

  • Unique Leaf Structure: Distinctive forked, trident-shaped leaves with elegant, flowing form

  • Epiphytic Growth: Attaches easily to driftwood, rock, or décor—no substrate required

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Thrives in a wide range of water conditions

products.title: Bucephalandra Theia Green
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Theia Green is a captivating and rare aquatic plant known for its striking green leaves and unique texture. This plant is highly sought after by aquarists for its beauty and versatility, making it a prized addition to any freshwater aquarium. Bucephalandra is a rheophyte/epiphyte plant, naturally growing over rocks and wood in fast-flowing waters. It takes a very long time to develop into a thick, compact carpet in nature.

Key Features:

  • Striking Green Leaves: Bucephalandra Theia Green boasts vibrant green leaves with a unique, wavy texture. Its lush foliage adds a touch of elegance and a splash of color to any aquascape.

  • Compact Growth: This plant grows in a compact, bushy form, making it ideal for small tanks or as a foreground and midground plant in larger aquariums.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Versatile Attachment: Bucephalandra Theia Green can be easily attached to driftwood, rocks, and other aquarium decorations using fishing line or thread until it establishes its roots. This versatility allows for creative and diverse aquascaping designs.

  • Lighting and Water Parameters: Thrives in low to moderate lighting conditions. It prefers temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C) and slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.0 to 7.5).

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Enhancement: The vibrant green color and textured leaves of Bucephalandra Theia Green enhance the visual appeal of any aquarium, creating a lush and dynamic underwater landscape.

  • Habitat Creation: This plant provides excellent hiding spots and breeding grounds for small fish and invertebrates, contributing to a balanced and healthy aquarium ecosystem.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Minimal Care Requirements: Bucephalandra Theia Green is relatively easy to care for, requiring only occasional trimming and basic water parameter monitoring.

  • Nutrient Absorption: While it benefits from a nutrient-rich water column, this plant does not require CO2 supplementation. However, CO2 can enhance its growth and vibrancy.

Commitment to Sustainability:

Due to the popularity of Bucephalandra species, they have been heavily collected from the wild, leading to a risk of extinction. At Aquarium Plants Factory, we are committed to providing farm-raised and tissue culture Bucephalandra species to save forests and protect the natural rivers and rainforests in Indonesia.

Conclusion:

Bucephalandra Theia Green is a stunning and versatile plant that brings a touch of natural beauty and elegance to any freshwater aquarium. Its unique foliage and easy care make it a standout addition to any aquascape. Perfect for attaching to driftwood, rocks, and other decorations, Bucephalandra Theia Green is a must-have for aquarists looking to create a lush and vibrant underwater environment.

Transform your aquarium with the exquisite Bucephalandra Theia Green and support sustainable aquascaping practices. Enjoy the serene and captivating beauty it provides.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in convenient formats:

  • Rooted Bunched: Approximately 1-2 plants with some roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Rooted Potted: About 1-2 plants with some roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is a rare and captivating aquatic plant that adds a unique touch of elegance to any aquarium. Known for its velvety, dark green leaves and easy care requirements, this plant is a favorite among aquarists looking to enhance their aquascapes with a distinctive and eye-catching element. Perfect for both beginners and experienced aquarists, Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is a must-have for any planted tank.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong

  1. Velvety Dark Green Leaves: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong boasts rich, velvety leaves that display a deep green hue, sometimes with subtle iridescent highlights. This unique texture and coloration make it a standout feature in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, providing depth and structure to your aquarium.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is known for its resilience and adaptability. It thrives in a range of water conditions and requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. This natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for creating biotope aquariums that mimic its natural environment.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and attach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer supports healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance its development but is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: Moderate to strong water flow helps mimic the plant’s natural environment, keeping its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The velvety, dark green leaves of Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong add a stunning visual effect, enhancing the beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Velvet Leaf Entikong is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

Whether you're new to the hobby or an experienced aquarist, Water Sprite is an excellent addition to your aquarium setup.

Key Features of Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  1. Beautiful Fern-Like Leaves: Water Sprite is prized for its delicate, lace-like leaves that create a light, airy look. The fresh green foliage brings a touch of nature's elegance into your tank, making it a visual treat.

  2. Versatile Growing Options: Whether you want it floating on the water surface or anchored in the substrate, Water Sprite adapts beautifully. This flexibility allows for creative aquascaping, letting you design a tank that's uniquely yours.

  3. Rapid Growth: Known for its fast growth, Water Sprite quickly fills in gaps, providing cover and helping to keep algae at bay by absorbing excess nutrients. It's a great plant for those looking to establish a lush aquarium in no time.

  4. Natural Habitat: Originating from tropical regions worldwide, Water Sprite thrives in warm, freshwater environments. Its ability to adapt to different conditions makes it ideal for tropical biotope aquariums.

  5. Easy Propagation: Growing your collection of Water Sprite is simple. New plants sprout from the leaves, making it easy to propagate and share with friends or spread throughout your tank.

Care Tips for Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting is best for Water Sprite, ensuring robust growth and vibrant green leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This adaptable plant prefers temperatures between 68-82°F (20-28°C) and a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It thrives in various water conditions, making it a resilient choice.
  • Substrate: While Water Sprite can float, it also does well anchored in the substrate. A nutrient-rich substrate is beneficial but not essential since it absorbs nutrients from the water.
  • Fertilization: Regular use of a balanced aquarium fertilizer promotes healthy growth. CO2 supplementation isn't necessary but can enhance growth.
  • Water Flow: Moderate water flow keeps the leaves clean and simulates its natural environment, helping to prevent debris accumulation.

Benefits of Adding Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) to Your Aquarium

  • Stunning Visuals: The fern-like leaves of Water Sprite add a striking natural element to your tank, creating a serene and attractive underwater landscape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: It provides excellent cover for fish and invertebrates, making your aquarium a more dynamic and engaging environment.
  • Improved Water Quality: Water Sprite helps maintain a clean tank by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water, reducing the likelihood of algae growth.

Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) is an outstanding choice for anyone looking to enhance their aquarium with lush greenery and improve the overall health of their aquatic environment. Its rapid growth, ease of care, and beautiful appearance make it a standout addition to any tank.

products.title: Ludwigia Meta
products.body_html:
🌿 Ludwigia Meta – Rare Red Stem Plant from Colombia

Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'Meta', commonly known as Ludwigia Meta, is a rare and magnificent stem plant native to the Departamento del Meta in central Colombia. Admired for its fine, whorled leaves and breathtaking color transformation, this plant is still considered a true rarity in the aquarium trade.

At the Aquarium Plants Factory nursery, Ludwigia Meta is cultivated in its emersed form, where the leaves are green in color. Once transitioned into the aquarium, it develops its signature orange to deep red hues, depending on lighting and nutrient conditions. This makes it an extraordinary choice for aquascapers seeking vibrant contrast and a truly exotic centerpiece.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'Meta'

  • Origin: Departamento del Meta, central Colombia

  • Appearance: Narrow leaves, about 5 cm long and 2 mm wide, arranged in elegant whorls of ~11 leaves

  • Coloration: Brilliant orange to deep red under strong lighting and nutrient-rich conditions

  • Growth Habit: Upright stems reaching up to 30 cm tall and 8 cm wide, with little branching

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Strong lighting required for vibrant coloration

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ injection and balanced macro- and micronutrients are highly recommended

  • Water Parameters: Prefers soft water with carbonate hardness below 4 °dH

  • Growth Method: Submersed stem plant; emersed form produces green leaves and striking yellow flowers

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for healthy growth and coloration

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – produces elliptical green leaves and yellow flowers on reddish-brown stems when grown above water

  • Propagation: Easily propagated by cuttings; floating stems may develop emersed shoots and flowers

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds spectacular red-orange tones to mid- or background layouts

  • Contrasts beautifully against light green plants, creating dramatic depth

  • Offers a rare centerpiece plant with colors similar to Ludwigia “Pantanal” but with easier care requirements

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Choose the format that suits your aquascaping needs:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 6+ unrooted stems

  • Bunched Plants: 6+ fresh-cut stems without root, bundled with foam and plant weight

  • Potted Plants: 6+ fresh-cut stems potted in rockwool within a 2-inch net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Ludwigia Meta ships in its emersed green form. Once submerged and provided with strong light and nutrients, it will gradually develop its iconic orange to red hues. Slight leaf melt during transition is normal.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please check your local regulations before introducing new plants. Never release aquarium plants into natural waterways.

Close-up of Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata ‘Meta’ with narrow whorled leaves
A healthy potted of premium Ludwigia Meta from Aquarium Plants Factory, ready for planting in a aquarium tank.

products.title: Ludwigia Pantanal
products.body_html:
🌿 Ludwigia Pantanal – Intense Red-Orange Beauty from South America’s Wetlands

Ludwigia Pantanal, is a crown jewel among aquatic stem plants—prized for its electrifying display of deep red and fiery orange tones. Native to the Pantanal wetlands of South America, this finely textured, whorled-leaf species transforms any aquascape into a vibrant masterpiece when grown under optimal conditions.

At Aquarium Plants Factory’s nursery, we cultivate this plant in its emersed form, where it develops upright green stems and slender leaves. When submerged in a high-tech aquarium with strong lighting, CO₂ injection, and a nutrient-rich substrate, it undergoes a dramatic transformation—producing striking coloration and bushy vertical growth. Its finely whorled leaf arrangement and vibrant tones make it one of the most stunning midground to background plants in the hobby.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'Pantanal'

  • Common Names: Ludwigia Pantanal

  • Growth Form: Finely textured, whorled-leaf stem plant

  • Leaf Color: Green in emersed form; transforms to vivid red and orange when submerged

  • Lighting Needs: High

  • CO₂ Requirements: Strongly recommended for coloration and compact growth

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Temperature: 70–82°F (21–28°C)

  • pH Range: 5.5–7.0

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Speed: Moderate to fast with CO₂ and strong lighting

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for optimal growth and coloration

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – green in nursery form

  • Propagation: Cuttings

🎯 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • One of the most vividly colored Ludwigia species available

  • Finely textured foliage contrasts beautifully with broad-leaf plants

  • Perfect for Dutch-style layouts and high-energy planted tanks

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Choose the format that suits your aquascaping needs:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Approximately 6+ unrooted stems

  • Bunched Plants: 6+ fresh-cut stems without root bundled with foam and plant weight

  • Potted Plants: 6+ fresh-cut stems potted in rockwool within a 2-inch net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:
Ludwigia Pantanal is a demanding species that requires high light, pressurized CO₂, and stable nutrient dosing to achieve its vibrant coloration and healthy growth. It is not suitable for low-tech setups. Emersed plants will arrive green and may undergo a transitional phase with some leaf melt before adapting to submerged growth.

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Please check local regulations before introducing any plants to outdoor ponds or waterways. Never release aquarium plants into natural habitats.

products.title: Ludwigia sp. White
products.body_html:
Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'White', commonly known as Ludwigia sp. White or Ludwigia sp. Pink, is a stunning and versatile aquatic plant that thrives in high-tech planted aquariums. This plant is known for its delicate, pink, and white leaves that add a touch of elegance and color to any aquascape.

Key Features:

  • Striking Appearance: Features delicate leaves that develop beautiful pink and white hues when grown submerged, creating a visually appealing contrast in your aquarium.
  • Versatile Growth: Can be grown both emersed and submerged, adapting its leaf structure accordingly.

Optimal Conditions:

  • High Light: Requires high lighting conditions to maintain its vibrant coloration and healthy growth.
  • CO2 Supplementation: Benefits significantly from ample CO2 supply, promoting robust and vibrant growth.
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Thrives in soil-based nutrient substrates that provide essential nutrients for optimal development.
  • Natural Sunlight: Grows best with plenty of natural sunlight, making it ideal for aquariums with good natural light exposure.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a unique and colorful element to aquascapes with its pink and white leaves, enhancing the overall visual appeal of the aquarium.
  • Versatile Placement: Suitable for midground or background placement, providing depth and contrast in high-tech planted aquariums.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Delicate Handling: Handle with care when transitioning from emersed to submerged growth to avoid damaging the delicate leaves.
  • Regular Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages bushier growth.
  • Consistent Conditions: Ensure stable water quality, CO2 levels, and nutrient availability for the best growth results.

Conclusion:

Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'White' (Ludwigia sp. White/Pink) is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to add a touch of elegance and vibrant color to their high-tech planted aquariums. Its delicate pink and white leaves and unique growth patterns make it a standout addition to any aquascape. With proper care, including high light, CO2 supplementation, and a nutrient-rich substrate, this plant will thrive and transform your aquarium into a stunning underwater garden.

Enhance your aquarium with the captivating beauty of Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'White' and enjoy the lush, vibrant transformation it brings to your aquatic environment.
