products.title: Dwarf Watermelon (Lindernia Rotundifolia Variegated)
products.body_html:
Lindernia rotundifolia 'Variegated' – Dwarf Watermelon Stem Plant with Unique Striped Leaves

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we affectionately call this beautiful variety “Dwarf Watermelon” because of its distinctively patterned round leaves, which resemble tiny watermelons. Lindernia rotundifolia 'Variegated' (syn. Lindernia parviflora variegated) is a graceful and fast-growing stem plant perfect for adding visual interest to your aquascape. Its creamy white stripes and strictly upright growth make it an ideal midground focal point or compact background bush when maintained properly.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Lindernia rotundifolia 'Variegated' (syn. Lindernia parviflora variegated)

  • Common Names: Dwarf Watermelon, Variegated Lindernia

  • Unique Appearance: Small, rounded leaves with elegant white longitudinal striping, like a miniature watermelon

  • Growth Habit: Upright stem growth with moderate to high light branching

  • Height: Can reach up to 40 cm; best trimmed to 15–30 cm to prevent uprooting

  • Light Needs: Moderate to high; better light results in bushier, fuller growth

  • CO₂: Not required, but enhances growth and density

  • Water Range: pH 5–8; soft to moderately hard water (0–14° dKH)

  • Temperature Tolerance: Very broad (4–34°C), with 22–26°C being ideal

🧪 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Medium to high; more light = more bushy, compact form

  • Temperature: Performs best at 22–26°C; tolerates a wide range

  • Water Hardness: Suitable for soft to moderately hard water

  • CO₂: Optional but highly beneficial

  • Nutrients: Grows well with general liquid fertilization; not nutrient-sensitive

💡 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Striking Contrast: The variegated leaves pop against green plants and hardscape

  • Midground Favorite: Upright form makes it great for filling space between foreground and background

  • Emersed Growth: Excellent for paludariums or Wabi-Kusa; will produce charming blue-white flowers

  • Fast Coverage: Quick to establish and fill space with proper trimming

  • Easy to Work With: Great for beginners or seasoned aquascapers

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

  • Growth Speed: Fast

  • Propagation Method: Stem cuttings—replant trimmed tops or side shoots

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Enhance your aquarium with our fresh, high-quality plants, available in convenient formats:

  • Loose Bare-Root Plants: Approx. 6+ variegated stems, trimmed and ready for planting

  • Bunched Plants: Approx. 6+ stems bundled with foam and plant weight for easy anchoring

  • Potted Plants: Approx. 6+ stems securely rooted in rockwool within a net pot

⚠️ State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner
Please never release Lindernia rotundifolia—or any aquatic plant—into natural waterways. Always dispose of trimmings responsibly. This species is not currently restricted in the U.S., but please check your local regulations before ordering.

products.title: Limnophila Aromatica
products.body_html:
Limnophila Aromatica, also known as Rice Paddy Herb, is a highly prized aquarium plant known for its aromatic leaves and vibrant green to purple coloration. This versatile plant is perfect for adding texture and color to midground and background areas in your aquarium, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Limnophila Aromatica
  • Common Name: Rice Paddy Herb
  • Family: Plantaginaceae
  • Leaf Color: Green to purple
  • Growth Form: Upright, bushy

Unique Characteristics

Limnophila Aromatica is renowned for its aromatic leaves that emit a pleasant scent when submerged. It originates from Southeast Asia, where it is commonly found in rice paddies and wet areas. This plant transitions from green to purple under high light conditions, creating a stunning visual effect in aquariums.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure the best growth and vibrant color for Limnophila Aromatica, provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Moderate to high intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 20-28°C (68-82°F)
  • pH Range: 5.5-7.5

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for and is suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure moderate to high light levels to maintain its vibrant coloration.
  • CO2 Injection: Regular CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and color intensity.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its form and encourages bushier growth.
  • Fertilization: Occasional fertilization supports vibrant growth and overall health.

Aquascaping Uses

Limnophila Aromatica is versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Midground Plant: Ideal for adding a splash of green to purple in the midground area.
  • Background Plant: Can be used to create a lush, vibrant backdrop.
  • Accent Plant: Its unique color transition makes it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The green to purple leaves of Limnophila Aromatica provide excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Limnophila Aromatica is straightforward. This plant propagates through stem cuttings. Simply cut a healthy stem and replant it in the substrate, where it will develop roots and grow into a new plant.

Limnophila Aromatica is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a vibrant and aromatic plant. Its stunning color transition, aromatic leaves, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6 loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6 fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6 fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Bucephalandra Belindae
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Belindae (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.
  • Sold as a Bunched Plants or Potted Plants.
  • Pictures are not the actual plants you will receive, but a sample representation.

products.title: Echinodorus Aflame Vitro Culture (Purple Knight Sword)
products.body_html:
🛡️ Echinodorus Aflame Vitro Culture – Purple Knight Sword in 4-Inch Bag

Echinodorus Aflame, commonly known as the Purple Knight Sword, is a captivating compact sword plant admired for its deep burgundy to dark purple foliage and striking upright rosette form. Cultivated in a sterile Vitro Culture environment, this plant arrives in a sealed 4-inch bag, containing approximately 3–4 small, healthy plantlets—ready to bring a bold, regal presence to your aquascape.

Perfect as a midground or background accent, the Purple Knight Sword thrives under high light and enriched substrate, with CO₂ supplementation helping it reach its full crimson potential. Its slow to moderate growth makes it manageable, while its color and structure create a commanding focal point.

✨ Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Echinodorus 'Aflame'

  • Common Name: Purple Knight Sword

  • Growth Form: Upright, compact rosette with sword-like leaves

  • Coloration: Deep red to purple-black under optimal light

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Recommended for best color development

  • Growth Rate: Medium

  • Placement: Midground to background

🌿 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

  • Lighting: Strong lighting enhances deep red coloration

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate is required for optimal performance

🌊 Vitro Culture Advantages:

  • Pest-Free & Clean: 100% free from snails, algae, and pesticides

  • High Yield: Each 4-inch bag provides approximately 3–4 plantlets

  • Safe for Shrimp & Fry: Excellent for sensitive or display tanks

  • Compact & Adaptable: Easy to plant and acclimate into new setups

🔁 Propagation & Growth:

products.title: Lagenandra Meeboldii Red Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Lagenandra meeboldii is a freshwater aquatic plant that is native to Southeast Asia, particularly found in India, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh. It belongs to the family Araceae, which includes other popular aquarium plants like Anubias and Cryptocoryne.

Lagenandra meeboldii is a relatively small plant, with leaves that typically grow to around 10-20 cm in length. The leaves are dark green in color and have a slightly glossy texture, with prominent veins that run from the base to the tip of the leaf. The plant is characterized by its unique shape, with the leaves growing in a rosette pattern that gives it a distinctive and attractive appearance.

In aquariums, Lagenandra meeboldii is often used as a midground or background plant, as its size and shape make it an excellent choice for filling in empty spaces and adding depth to the aquascape. It is a relatively hardy plant that can tolerate a wide range of water conditions, including moderate to high lighting and a variety of substrates.

One of the unique features of Lagenandra meeboldii is its ability to change the color of its leaves based on the conditions in which it is grown. Under high light conditions, the leaves can take on a reddish or purplish hue, while under lower light conditions, they may appear more green.

Overall, Lagenandra meeboldii is a beautiful and versatile aquatic plant that can add a unique and attractive element to any aquarium setup. Its hardy nature, ease of care, and ability to adapt to different lighting conditions make it a popular choice among hobbyists and professionals alike.

products.title: Ceratopteris Siliquosa Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Ceratopteris Siliquosa, commonly known as Water Sprite, is a highly versatile and fast-growing aquatic plant that can transform any aquarium into a lush, green oasis. Known for its delicate, fern-like leaves and easy care requirements, Water Sprite is an excellent choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists. This plant can be used as a floating plant, a midground anchor, or as a background plant, offering flexibility in aquascaping designs.

Key Features of Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  1. Delicate, Fern-Like Leaves: Water Sprite boasts finely divided, lace-like leaves that create a beautiful and airy appearance. The light green foliage adds a refreshing and natural look to any aquarium, making it an attractive focal point.

  2. Versatile Growth Options: This plant can be grown either floating on the water surface or anchored in the substrate. Its adaptability allows for creative aquascaping, whether you want a floating canopy or a rooted background plant.

  3. Fast Growth Rate: Ceratopteris Siliquosa is known for its rapid growth, helping to quickly fill in empty spaces in your tank. This fast growth rate also makes it effective at outcompeting algae for nutrients, contributing to a cleaner and healthier aquarium environment.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to tropical regions around the world, Water Sprite thrives in warm, freshwater environments. It naturally grows in shallow waters, making it an ideal choice for replicating tropical biotope aquariums.

  5. Easy Propagation: Propagating Water Sprite is simple and straightforward. New plantlets form along the leaves and can be separated and replanted. This makes it easy to expand your collection and maintain a lush, green aquarium.

Care Tips for Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite)

  • Lighting: Moderate to high lighting is ideal for Water Sprite. Adequate light supports its rapid growth and helps maintain its vibrant green color.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-82°F (20-28°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in a variety of water conditions, making it highly adaptable.
  • Substrate: While Water Sprite can be grown floating, it can also be anchored in the substrate. A nutrient-rich substrate will support its growth, but it can also absorb nutrients directly from the water.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer will promote healthy growth and vibrant foliage. CO2 supplementation is not strictly necessary but can enhance its development.
  • Water Flow: Moderate water flow is ideal for keeping the leaves of Water Sprite clean and free from debris, mimicking its natural environment.

Benefits of Adding Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The delicate, fern-like leaves of Water Sprite add a stunning visual effect, enhancing the natural beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides excellent cover and hiding spots for fish and invertebrates, creating a more dynamic and balanced ecosystem.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Water Sprite helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water, reducing the risk of algae outbreaks.

Ceratopteris Siliquosa (Water Sprite) is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance, fast growth, and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

products.title: Utricularia Graminifolia
products.body_html:
Utricularia Graminifolia is celebrated as one of the most beautiful and popular foreground plants for planted aquariums. Its small size, fluffy appearance, and grass-like lawn effect make it a top choice for aquascaping, especially in nano tanks.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Utricularia Graminifolia
  • Common Name: UG, Grass-leaved Bladderwort
  • Family: Lentibulariaceae
  • Leaf Color: Bright green
  • Growth Form: Dense, carpet-like

Unique Characteristics

Utricularia Graminifolia stands out for its bright green, grass-like leaves that form a lush, dense carpet, creating a stunning visual effect in the foreground of any aquarium. Its compact size and fluffy texture make it ideal for intricate aquascape designs.

Ideal Conditions

To thrive, Utricularia Graminifolia requires specific conditions:

  • Light: High intensity
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich soil
  • Water Temperature: 22-28°C (72-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for, given the right conditions:

  • Lighting: Ensure high light levels to promote healthy growth.
  • Anchoring: Due to its tiny root system, use stainless steel T-pins to anchor the plant to the substrate until it becomes established.
  • Dry Start Method (DSM): The DSM is highly effective for quickly establishing Utricularia Graminifolia, allowing it to access natural CO2 and grow rapidly.

Growth and Propagation

Once adapted to the environment, Utricularia Graminifolia develops a deep root system, spreading through runners to form a continuous carpet. The dry start method (DSM) is particularly effective, as it allows the plant to access an unlimited amount of natural CO2, boosting growth rates and ensuring a fully grown carpet in a short time.

Safety in Aquariums

Despite being a carnivorous plant, Utricularia Graminifolia is safe for aquarium inhabitants. Its tiny traps are designed to capture microscopic organisms and pose no threat to fish, shrimp, or other larger tank inhabitants. This makes it a unique and harmless addition to your aquascape.

Aquascaping Uses

Utricularia Graminifolia is versatile and ideal for various aquascaping applications:

  • Foreground Plant: Perfect for creating a dense, green carpet in the foreground.
  • Nano Tanks: Ideal for small tanks due to its compact and fluffy growth.
  • Carpet Plant: Its grass-like appearance makes it an excellent choice for lush carpeted areas.

The bright green, dense foliage of Utricularia Graminifolia provides excellent contrast and visual interest, enhancing the overall aesthetics of both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Utricularia Graminifolia is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a beautiful and unique foreground plant. Its vibrant green color, fluffy texture, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a stunning underwater landscape.

  • Sold as a small loose bare-root portion
  • Approx. 2" x 2" in size

PLANTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR WATER LILY

 

There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

products.title: Nymphaea Bagdad | Blue Tropical Water Lily
products.body_html:
Transform your water garden into a serene oasis with the Nymphaea 'Bagdad', a breathtaking tropical water lily known for its enchanting sky-blue to light-violet blooms. This prolific bloomer features elegant, star-shaped flowers that rise gracefully above beautifully mottled green foliage, creating a stunning focal point in any pond or large container.1

    Key Features:

    • Stunning Blue Blooms: Features vibrant, multi-petaled flowers with a glowing yellow center, offering a rare and sought-after color palette for aquatic gardens.

    • Mottled Foliage: The pads are not just green; they often showcase unique purple-maroon mottling, providing year-round visual interest even when the plant isn't in bloom.2

    • Fragrant & Prolific: Known for its sweet fragrance and ability to produce multiple flowers throughout the growing season.3

    Optimal Growing Conditions:

    • Sunlight: Requires full sun (at least 6+ hours) to reach its full blooming potential.

    • Water Depth: Thrives in depths of 6 to 18 inches.

    • Climate: As a tropical variety, it loves the heat. In cooler climates, tubers should be stored indoors during winter months.

    Why Choose Nymphaea 'Bagdad'?

    Whether you are a novice enthusiast or a seasoned pond keeper, the 'Bagdad' is a robust grower that rewards minimal care with maximal beauty. Its ability to attract pollinators like bees and butterflies makes it a functional and aesthetic powerhouse for your local ecosystem.

    Planting Tip:

    For the best results, plant your Young Sprouted Tuber in a heavy clay-based soil or specialized aquatic planting medium. Use Water Lily fertilizer tablets monthly during the growing season to encourage those iconic blue blossoms.

    Would you like me to adjust the "Search engine listing" (SEO) preview to match this new description?

    PLANTING INSTRUCTIONS FOR WATER LILY

     

    There are two main types of water lilies, tropical and hardy. The differences between tropical and hardy water lilies are a growing habit. Tropical water lilies don't do well in winter. Hardy water lilies, on the other hand, can tolerate the cold. Tropical water lilies tubers must be removed from the pond and stored in moist conditions to come back in the future years. In warm climates, they can remain in the pond.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Undulatus Red Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne undulatus 'Red' is a variant of the popular aquatic plant species Cryptocoryne undulatus. Cryptocoryne undulatus is a species of aquatic plants belonging to the family Araceae. It is commonly known as water trumpet or wavy leaf crypt.

The 'Red' variety of Cryptocoryne undulatus is characterized by its reddish or bronze-colored leaves. This variation in leaf coloration can be influenced by factors such as lighting conditions, nutrient availability, and water parameters in the aquarium. Generally, higher lighting and nutrient levels can lead to more intense red coloration in the leaves.

Cryptocoryne undulatus 'Red' is a popular choice among aquarium enthusiasts for planted tanks due to its distinctive appearance and relatively easy care requirements. It can add a splash of color and visual interest to aquatic setups. Like other Cryptocoryne species, 'Red' varieties prefer stable water conditions, moderate to low lighting, and a nutrient-rich substrate. They can thrive in a range of water parameters, making them suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists.

In terms of propagation, Cryptocoryne undulatus 'Red' can reproduce through runners, which are horizontal stems that produce new plants. As the runners grow, they develop new plantlets at their tips, which can be separated from the parent plant and replanted to propagate the species.

It's important to note that plant names and variations can sometimes be subject to changes and updates within the aquarium hobby, so I recommend consulting with reputable sources or your local aquarium community for the most accurate and up-to-date information about Cryptocoryne undulatus 'Red' or any other aquatic plants you're interested in.

Rotala ramosior 'Florida' – Purple-Toned Freshwater Stem Plant

Rotala ramosior 'Florida' is a rare and visually captivating aquatic plant prized for its vibrant purple tones when grown submersed in planted aquariums. Native to North America and selectively cultivated for its color intensity, this variety of Rotala offers aquarists a stunning alternative to traditional green plants. Under the right conditions, Rotala ramosior 'Florida' develops rich lavender to deep purple hues—providing a bold pop of color that enhances the depth and vibrancy of any aquascape.

🌿 Key Features & Benefits

  • Scientific Name: Rotala ramosior 'Florida'

  • Common Name: Florida Rotala

  • Color-Changing Beauty: Displays a striking purple hue under high lighting and CO₂-enriched conditions

  • Fine, Delicate Growth: Narrow leaves grow in opposite pairs along vertical stems, creating elegant structure

  • Moderate Growth Rate: Easily manageable with occasional trimming

  • Lighting Requirements: Medium to high lighting encourages optimal color development

  • CO₂ and Fertilizer: CO₂ injection and nutrient-rich water or substrate improve color and density

  • Propagation: Simple stem cuttings—clip healthy tops and replant directly in substrate

  • Tank Placement: Ideal for midground to background in high-tech aquascapes, nature aquariums, and Dutch-style layouts

💧 Why Rotala ramosior 'Florida' is a Must-Have

For aquarists looking to add vibrant coloration and vertical interest to their freshwater aquarium, Rotala ramosior 'Florida' offers an elegant solution. Its distinctive purple tones provide a stunning contrast to green aquatic plants, creating a dynamic visual effect that elevates the entire aquascape. Whether you're designing a nature aquarium, a color-focused Dutch-style layout, or simply want to add a unique species to your collection, this plant delivers both aesthetic appeal and ecological benefits.

Its moderate growth rate makes it easy to maintain, and its propagation through stem cuttings allows for effortless expansion throughout your tank. While it thrives best with CO₂ supplementation and strong lighting, it remains relatively easy to care for—making it suitable for both intermediate hobbyists and advanced aquascapers.

products.title: Cabomba Furcata
products.body_html:
Cabomba Furcata (Red Cabomba), also known as Fanwort or Cabomba Plant, is a striking aquatic plant that adds a vibrant splash of color and texture to freshwater aquariums. With its delicate, feathery leaves and stunning pink, reddish-purple hues, this plant creates a beautiful contrast against green foliage, enhancing the overall aesthetic of your aquascape.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Red Coloration: Displays eye-catching reddish-purple leaves under optimal lighting, making it a standout feature in any aquarium.
  • Feathery Appearance: The finely divided, fan-like leaves create a soft and elegant look, perfect for background planting or as a midground accent.
  • Rapid Growth: A fast-growing plant that helps absorb excess nutrients, improving water quality and reducing algae growth.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Requires moderate to high lighting to achieve its vibrant red coloration. Stronger lighting enhances its reddish hues.
  • Water Parameters: Thrives in slightly acidic to neutral water with a pH range of 6.0-7.5 and temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).
  • Nutrients: Benefits from nutrient-rich substrate and liquid fertilizers to support healthy growth. CO2 supplementation enhances its color and vitality but is not strictly necessary.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Natural Habitat: Provides shelter and spawning areas for fish and shrimp, promoting a more natural and stress-free environment.
  • Improves Water Quality: Absorbs excess nitrates and other nutrients, helping to maintain a balanced aquarium ecosystem.
  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds height, movement, and a vibrant red focal point to your aquascape, making it ideal for background planting in larger tanks.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy to Moderate Care: While relatively undemanding, it thrives best in well-lit setups with a nutrient-rich environment.
  • Pruning: Trim regularly to maintain shape and encourage bushy, compact growth. Plant cuttings can easily propagate to create new plants.

Conclusion:

Cabomba Furcata (Red Cabomba) is the perfect choice for aquarists looking to elevate their aquarium with a splash of red and a touch of elegance. Its vibrant color, delicate texture, and fast growth make it a versatile and visually stunning addition to freshwater aquascapes. Whether used as a background feature or a midground accent, this plant is sure to enhance the beauty and balance of your underwater world.

Bring home Cabomba Furcata today and transform your aquarium into a lush, vibrant aquatic paradise!

products.title: Hyptis Lorentziana (Purple Mint)
products.body_html:
Hyptis Lorentziana, commonly known as Purple Mint, is a rare and captivating aquarium plant known for its vibrant purple-red leaves. This plant is ideal for adding a unique touch of color to the background areas in your aquarium, making it a favorite among aquascaping enthusiasts who seek rare and exotic flora.

Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Hyptis Lorentziana
  • Common Name: Purple Mint
  • Family: Lamiaceae
  • Leaf Color: Deep purple-red
  • Growth Form: Moderate height with bushy growth

Unique Characteristics

Hyptis Lorentziana has been a subject of intrigue in the aquatic plant hobby. It was originally called "Hemigraphis traian" but was correctly identified in late 2009 as belonging to the family Lamiaceae. Native to South America, it thrives on the edges of ponds and wet areas, growing up to 2 meters tall in natural settings. This plant is rare in trade but is gaining popularity among hobbyists worldwide.

Ideal Conditions

To ensure the best growth and vibrant color for Hyptis Lorentziana, provide the following conditions:

  • Light: Strong lighting required
  • CO2: Beneficial for optimal growth
  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate preferred
  • Water Temperature: 20-28°C (68-82°F)
  • pH Range: 6.0-7.5
  • Nitrate Level: Around 10 mg/l
  • Phosphate Level: 1-2 mg/l

Care and Maintenance

This plant is relatively easy to care for and is suitable for both beginners and experienced aquarists:

  • Lighting: Ensure strong light levels to maintain its vibrant purple-red coloration.
  • CO2 Injection: Regular CO2 supplementation can enhance growth and color intensity.
  • Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its form and encourages bushier growth.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization supports vibrant growth and overall health.

Aquascaping Uses

Hyptis Lorentziana is versatile and can be used in various ways:

  • Background Plant: Ideal for adding a unique touch of purple-red to the background area.
  • Accent Plant: Its intense color makes it an excellent focal point in aquascapes.

The large purple-red leaves of Hyptis Lorentziana provide excellent contrast and visual interest, making it a great choice for both Dutch and Nature-style aquascapes.

Propagation

Propagation of Hyptis Lorentziana is straightforward. This plant propagates through stem cuttings. Cut a healthy stem directly above a leaf pair, around 8 to 10 cm above the ground, and replant it in the substrate. This encourages new growth and lateral shoot formation.

Hyptis Lorentziana is a must-have for any aquarist looking to enhance their aquarium with a vibrant and eye-catching plant. Its stunning purple-red leaves, moderate growth, and easy maintenance make it a standout choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 3+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 3+ fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Vallisneria nana 'Striped'
products.body_html:
🌿 Vallisneria nana 'Striped' – Hardy, Patterned Background Grass Plant

Vallisneria nana 'Striped', also known as Vallisneria nana 'Tiger', is a robust and undemanding background plant ideal for planted aquariums. This cultivar is a hybrid derived from Vallisneria nana, combining its narrow-leaved form with subtle variegation in the form of brown striping or speckled dot patterns on some leaves. Known for its hardiness, it is even more resilient than Vallisneria spiralis and can quickly propagate via runners to form dense, swaying backgrounds.

Reaching over 20 inches in height under optimal conditions, this Tiger Vallisneria retains its rich green coloration even under high-intensity lighting—unlike its cousin Vallisneria spiralis 'Leopard', which develops intense red hues and a more marbled texture. Its graceful, upright growth and ease of care make it a standout choice for aquascapers of all skill levels.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Vallisneria nana 'Striped'

  • Synonyms: Vallisneria nana 'Tiger'

  • Common Name: Tiger Vallisneria

  • Subtle Leaf Variegation: May display random brown stripes or speckles on green leaves

  • Hardy and Adaptable: More resilient than Vallisneria spiralis

  • Tall Growth: Can reach 20+ inches, great for background coverage

  • Runners for Propagation: Spreads easily to create lush plant groupings

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Moderate to high light

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ not required; nutrient-rich substrate or root tabs recommended

  • Growth Method: Grows vertically and propagates rapidly via runners

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Creates soft, natural movement in the background

  • Ideal for Dutch-style and jungle-style aquascapes

  • Pairs well with red or colorful stem plants for contrast

  • Helps soften hardscape transitions and background lines

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose bare-root plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual loose, bare-root plants

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Some leaf melting may occur when transitioning to a new tank. Stripe intensity and patterning may vary between individual plants. Provide stable lighting and substrate for best performance.

Always follow your local guidelines and never release aquarium plants into natural water systems. Dispose of plant trimmings responsibly.

products.title: Vallisneria Torta
products.body_html:
This pretty, small corkscrew Vallisneria is labeled by several water plant nurseries as 'Vallisneria Torta'. It is probably Vallisneria asiatica var. biwaensis, but as long as its identity is unclear, we offer the plant as Vallisneria Torta. According to descriptions by the nurseries, it is the smallest Vallisneria, reaching a height of just 20 cm. The leaves are light green, somewhat transparent, and spirally twisted.

As well as most other Vallisneria, Vallisneria Torta has no special demands. Moderate light intensity is sufficient. The plant propagates itself by runners. If it spreads too much, newly appearing runners can be removed.

Vallisneria sp. "Torta" looks best as a group in the midground or foreground and can also be used for nano aquariums. With its spiral, light green leaves it provides an interesting accent.

products.title: Ludwigia Red Skeleton
products.body_html:
Ludwigia Rubin 'Red Skeleton' commonly known as Ludwigia Red Skeleton. It is a rare and captivating mutation of Ludwigia repens 'Rubin' perfect for adding a unique touch to any planted aquarium. This aquatic plant is notable for its red stems, red under-leaves, and green upper-leaves with yellow-striped variegation in a striking skeleton pattern. Its distinctive beauty sets it apart, making it a standout choice for aquascaping enthusiasts. Often confused with Ludwigia sp. Atlantis which is a mutation of Ludwigia Repens. 

Key Features:

  • Distinctive Appearance: Features red stems, red under-leaves, and green upper-leaves with yellow-striped variegation in an intricate skeleton pattern.
  • Color Variation: Exhibits stunning deep red coloration under high lighting, while the leaves remain green in low light conditions.
  • Stable Mutation: Offspring consistently inherit the variegated genes, ensuring reliable coloration and patterning.

Ideal Conditions:

  • Lighting and Water Parameters: Thrives in moderate to high lighting. In low light, the leaves stay green; in high light, they turn a striking red. Prefers slightly acidic to neutral pH levels (6.5 to 7.5), with temperatures between 72°F to 82°F (22°C to 28°C).
  • Submerged Growth: Transforms into a beautiful deep red when grown submerged, adding elegance and vibrancy to your aquatic environment.

Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Eye-Catching Focal Point: The unique coloration and pattern make it a perfect centerpiece, drawing attention in any aquarium.
  • Versatile Planting Options: Can be used singly for a striking focal point or planted in groups for a dynamic and interesting background.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Easy to Grow: Adaptable and low-maintenance, making it suitable for both novice and experienced aquarists.
  • Lighting Requirements: Readily turns red under moderate lighting conditions, enhancing the visual appeal of your aquarium.

Conclusion: Ludwigia Rubin 'Red Skeleton' is a must-have for aquarists looking to add a touch of rare beauty and elegance to their freshwater aquariums. At Aquarium Plants Factory, we take pride in successfully cultivating this extraordinary species and we are the first aquatic plant nursery in the United States to release this plant to the market. The stable mutation ensures that offspring inherit the variegated genes from their parents, guaranteeing consistency and quality in every plant. Its impressive coloration, easy growth, and stable mutation make it an ideal choice for creating stunning aquascapes. Whether used as a focal point or a complementary background plant, Ludwigia Rubin 'Red Skeleton' enhances the visual appeal and ecological balance of any aquarium.

products.title: Hygrophila Kompakt
products.body_html:
Hygrophila corymbosa 'Kompakt' – Lush, Compact Stem Plant for Vibrant Midgrounds

Enhance your aquarium with the lush and compact beauty of Hygrophila corymbosa 'Kompakt', also known as Compact Hygrophila or Nomaphila siamensis 'Parvifolia'. This popular aquatic plant is highly regarded for its dense growth habit and striking appearance, making it an excellent choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

A cultivated variant of the classic Hygrophila corymbosa, 'Kompakt' features shorter internodes and bushy form, growing into neat, vibrant clusters. Its bright green, oval leaves create lush texture and visual depth, making it ideal for midground groupings and background transitions.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Hygrophila corymbosa 'Kompakt'

  • Common Names: Compact Hygrophila, Nomaphila siamensis 'Parvifolia'

  • Origin: Selective cultivated variant of Hygrophila corymbosa

  • Leaf Characteristics: Broad, oval, bright green leaves; bushy and compact growth habit

  • Growth Rate: Fast with regular trimming

  • Light Demand: Low to high; stronger lighting enhances leaf density and color

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required but promotes denser, more vigorous growth

🧪 Advantages of Our Selection:

  • Fresh-cut or potted formats for flexible aquascaping needs

  • Free from pests, algae, and pathogens

  • Safe for shrimp tanks and sensitive aquariums—no hitchhikers

  • Sustainably propagated—no wild collection required

🎨 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Excellent for creating dense midground hedges and structured plant groups

  • Adds texture, volume, and color to planted layouts

  • Responds well to trimming—easy to shape into desired forms

  • Complements background stem plants and foreground carpets beautifully

  • Provides natural shelter and foraging area for shrimp and small fish

🌱 Propagation & Growth:

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required — benefits from enriched root zone

products.title: Vallisneria Spiralis (Jungle Val)
products.body_html:
🌿 Vallisneria spiralis – Classic Eelgrass for Flowing Backgrounds

Vallisneria spiralis, also known as Straight Vallisneria, Jungle Val or Italian Eelgrass, is a classic and widely loved aquarium plant known for its tall, ribbon-like leaves and graceful movement. Native to parts of Asia and the Mediterranean, this plant thrives in a range of freshwater conditions and is one of the easiest background plants to grow. Its long, slightly twisted leaves can reach the surface of taller tanks, creating a lush, natural curtain that sways beautifully with the water flow.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Vallisneria spiralis

  • Synonyms: Italian Vallisneria, Straight Vallisneria

  • Common Name: Common Eelgrass, Jungle Val

  • Tall, Flowing Leaves: Long, narrow leaves can reach 24 inches or more, ideal for taller aquariums

  • Elegant Movement: Adds a natural, flowing motion to your aquascape

  • Beginner-Friendly: Very easy to care for and fast-growing

  • Spreads Quickly: Produces runners that colonize the substrate rapidly

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Low to high; performs well under a wide range of light levels

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ not required; benefits from root tabs or enriched substrate

  • Growth Method: Propagates through runners; forms dense thickets over time

  • Nutrient Substrate: Recommended

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Ideal for creating a lush, flowing green background

  • Softens hardscape lines and hides equipment naturally

  • Excellent for biotope, jungle-style, and nature-style aquariums

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as loose fresh-cut plants, bunched plants, or potted plants:

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual loose, bare-root plants

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants bundled with foam and a plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 2–3 individual bare-root plants potted in rockwool and net pot

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Some leaf melt may occur during initial adaptation to your tank. Consistent lighting and nutrient-rich substrate will support healthy regrowth and spread.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Always follow your local guidelines and never release aquarium plants into natural water systems. Dispose of plant trimmings responsibly.

products.title: Mayaca Fluviatilis
products.body_html:
🌿 Mayaca fluviatilis – Elegant Soft-Textured Aquarium Stem Plant

Mayaca fluviatilis is the quintessential “soft” plant for the sophisticated aquascape. Known for its fine, needle-like bright green leaves and delicate, flexible stems, this South American native creates a shimmering, moss-like thicket that moves gracefully with water flow. Its gentle texture and vibrant coloration bring a sense of calm, depth, and refinement to planted aquariums.

Whether you are designing a structured Dutch-style street or a more organic Nature Aquarium layout, Mayaca fluviatilis delivers a unique softness that few other stem plants can replicate.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Mayaca fluviatilis
  • Plant Type: Stem plant
  • Color: Bright to yellow-green
  • Leaf Shape: Fine, needle-like foliage
  • Growth Rate: Fast
  • Placement: Midground to background
  • Origin: South & Central America

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting: Low to high
  • CO₂: Not required (recommended for best density and color)
  • Care Level: Beginner-friendly
  • Propagation: Easily propagated by trimming and replanting stems
  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required
  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Under stronger lighting and stable nutrients, Mayaca fluviatilis develops tighter internodes and fuller growth, creating a lush, feathery mass ideal for dense plant groupings.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Creates a soft, flowing texture unlike most stem plants
  • Adds movement and shimmer as it sways with water flow
  • Excellent for Dutch aquascapes, Nature-style layouts, and jungle tanks
  • Blends beautifully with broader-leaf plants for contrast

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Mayaca fluviatilis cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient planting formats:

Fresh Cut Plants: Approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots.
Fresh Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ stems bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
Fresh Cut Potted: About 6+ stems potted securely in a net pot with rockwool for stability and easy planting.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Live aquatic plants may vary in size, leaf shape, and coloration due to natural growing conditions. Minor melting may occur as the plant adapts to new aquarium parameters—this is normal and temporary under proper care.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Cameroon Moss
products.body_html:
Cameroon Moss (Plagiochilaceae sp. Cameroon): The Perfect Addition for Freshwater Aquariums

Cameroon Moss, also known as Plagiochilaceae sp. Cameroon, is a popular aquatic moss originating from Cameroon, West Africa. Highly valued for its fast growth and ease of maintenance, this moss is an excellent choice for aquascaping and natural filtration in freshwater aquariums.

Key Features:

  • Rapid Growth: Cameroon Moss grows quickly, helping to maintain a healthy and vibrant aquarium environment.
  • Nutrient Absorption: It efficiently absorbs excess nutrients, enhancing water quality and reducing algae growth.
  • Versatile Use: This moss forms a dense, cushion-like green mat that can be attached to rocks, driftwood, or backgrounds. It also serves as a spawning mop for certain fish species.

Care Tips:

  • Lighting: Provide moderate to low lighting to support healthy growth.
  • Water Changes: Regular water changes are essential to maintain optimal water conditions.
  • Avoid Chemicals: Steer clear of chemical fertilizers or products that can harm the moss and negatively impact water quality.

Benefits:

Cameroon Moss not only adds a natural and aesthetic touch to your aquarium but also creates a habitat for aquatic inhabitants. Its low maintenance requirements make it an ideal choice for both beginner and experienced aquarium enthusiasts.

Enhance your freshwater aquarium with the beauty and functionality of Cameroon Moss. Its ability to improve water quality and provide a natural environment for your fish makes it a must-have for any aquascape.

Conclusion:

Vesicularia montagnei 'Mini' (Mini Christmas Moss) is an exceptional choice for aquarists looking to add a touch of natural elegance to their freshwater aquariums. Easy to care for and highly versatile, Mini Christmas Moss is perfect for creating lush, green carpets or accentuating hardscape elements in your aquarium.

products.title: Bucephalandra Round Wavy
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Round Wavy (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.

Bucephalandra ‘Giant Broad Leaf’, commonly known as Buce Giant Broad Leaf, is a striking and rare Bucephalandra variety admired for its wide, paddle-shaped leaves and commanding presence in aquascapes. Originating from the clean, fast-flowing rivers of Borneo, this slow-growing species stands out for its thick, glossy foliage that shimmers in deep green with subtle bluish tones under aquarium lighting. Its large, dramatic leaves bring instant depth and natural elegance to any aquascape—making it a prized gem among collectors and enthusiasts.

products.title: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is an extraordinary and rare aquatic plant that captivates with its deep, dark hues and unique foliage. This plant is a favorite among aquarists looking to add a touch of mystery and elegance to their aquascapes. Known for its ease of care and adaptability, Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is perfect for both novice and experienced aquarists.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost

  1. Enchanting Dark Foliage: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost features dark, almost black leaves with subtle iridescent highlights. These leaves create a striking contrast in your aquarium, making this plant a standout feature in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Bushy Growth: This plant has a compact and bushy growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a midground or foreground plant, providing depth and texture to your aquascape.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is known for its resilience and adaptability. It thrives in a range of water conditions and requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, this plant grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for replicating natural biotope aquariums.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and reattach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal for Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer will support healthy growth and vibrant colors. While CO2 supplementation can enhance its development, it is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: A moderate to strong water flow can mimic the plant’s natural environment and help keep its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The dark, iridescent foliage of Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost adds a stunning visual effect, enhancing the overall beauty of your aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.

Sold as Bunched Plants or Potted Plants:

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 1-2 plants bundled with a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Potted Plants: Approximately 1-2 plants potted in rockwool and a net pot for secure growth and easy placement.

Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue is a stunning and highly sought-after aquatic plant that adds a touch of elegance and uniqueness to any aquarium. Known for its striking blue hues and delicate foliage, this plant is a favorite among aquascapers and hobbyists alike. With its easy care requirements and captivating appearance, Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue is the perfect addition to both beginner and advanced planted tanks.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue

  1. Striking Blue Foliage: The most distinctive feature of Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue is its beautiful blue-tinted leaves. These iridescent leaves shimmer under aquarium lighting, creating a mesmerizing effect that enhances the visual appeal of any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it ideal for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, and its slow growth rate ensures it maintains its shape without frequent trimming.

  4. Unique Flowers: In addition to its striking foliage, Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue occasionally produces small, delicate flowers that add an extra layer of beauty to the aquarium. These flowers typically bloom above the water surface.

  5. Natural Habitat: This plant is native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, where it grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. This natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for replicating natural biotope aquariums.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue

  • Propagation: This plant can be easily propagated by cutting and replanting its rhizomes. Ensure each cutting has a few leaves and roots for successful propagation.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Alamanda Blue to Your Aquarium

products.title: Bucephalandra Dark Blue
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Dark Blue (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.

products.title: Pogostemon Stellatus Dassen Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen' in Vitro Culture® – Rare, Starburst-Leaf Stem Plant for Advanced Aquascapes

Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen' is a rare and stunning aquatic stem plant known for its fine, needle-like leaves arranged in a striking starburst pattern. This unique cultivar of Pogostemon stellatus originates from Asia and is prized for its ability to shift in color—ranging from light green to pink, orange, or purple under optimal conditions. Its dynamic structure and intense hues make it an ideal showpiece for Dutch-style aquascapes, color gradients, or high-tech planted tanks.

Each plant is offered in a 4-inch Vitro Culture® bag, containing multiple small, pest-free plantlets grown in vitamin-enriched gel or liquid. Our tissue culture propagation ensures a completely sterile start—100% free of snails, algae, pesticides, and pathogens—making it ideal for shrimp-safe tanks and premium aquascaping setups.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen'

  • Common Name: Pogostemon 'Dassen'

  • Leaf Shape: Fine, needle-like leaves in starburst whorls

  • Coloration: Green to vivid pink, orange, or purple with strong lighting and nutrients

  • Growth Form: Upright stem plant with dense branching

  • Vitro Culture® Format: 4-inch sealed bag with multiple plantlets

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Growth Rate: Medium to fast

  • Difficulty Level: Advanced – thrives best in stable, enriched tanks

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: High (essential for vibrant coloration and compact growth)

  • CO₂: Strongly recommended for optimal form and color

  • Substrate: Nutrient-rich substrate or root tab supplementation

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.2 | Temperature: 22–28°C (72–82°F)

  • Fertilization: Requires regular micronutrient and iron dosing to maintain coloration

  • Trimming: Regular pruning helps promote bushy, compact form

🧪 Vitro Culture® Advantages:

  • 100% Free from Snails, Algae, Pesticides, and Pathogens

  • Clean & Safe: Perfect for shrimp tanks and sensitive ecosystems

  • Long Shelf Life: Can be stored under light until ready to plant

  • Easy to Plant: Rinse, gently divide, and insert into substrate using tweezers

🐠 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Color Showpiece: Adds a brilliant pop of color and texture to your layout

  • Fine Texture: Balances well with broad-leaf plants and mosses

  • Dutch Layout Star: A must-have for advanced aquascapers building color tiers

  • High Impact: Makes an instant impression in feature spots or mass plantings

📦 What You’ll Receive:

One 4-inch sealed Vitro Culture® bag containing multiple Pogostemon stellatus 'Dassen' plantlets, ready for acclimation and planting.

products.title: Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla, also known as Bucephalandra Kedagang Pink, is a captivating and rare aquatic plant that brings a burst of color and elegance to any aquarium. Renowned for its vibrant pink hues and unique foliage, this plant is highly sought after by aquarists looking to create a visually stunning and diverse aquascape. Ideal for both beginners and experienced aquarists, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla is a must-have for any planted tank.

Key Features of Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla

  1. Vibrant Pink Foliage: Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla boasts striking pink leaves with shades that can range from light pink to deep magenta. This unique coloration adds a dramatic and eye-catching element to your aquarium, making it a focal point in any aquascape.

  2. Compact and Versatile Growth: This plant has a compact growth habit, making it perfect for various aquascaping styles. It can be used as a foreground or midground plant, providing depth and texture to your tank.

  3. Hardy and Adaptable: Known for its resilience, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla thrives in a range of water conditions. It requires minimal maintenance and can adapt to both low-tech and high-tech aquarium setups, making it suitable for all levels of aquarists.

  4. Natural Habitat: Native to the fast-flowing streams and rivers of Borneo, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla grows attached to rocks and submerged wood. Its natural habitat makes it an excellent choice for creating biotope aquariums that mimic its native environment.

  5. Easy Propagation: This plant is easily propagated through rhizome division. Simply cut a section of the rhizome with leaves and roots, and attach it to a new location in your aquarium. Each section will quickly establish itself and continue to grow.

Care Tips for Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla

  • Lighting: Low to moderate lighting is ideal. While it can tolerate higher light levels, too much light can promote algae growth on its leaves.
  • Water Conditions: This plant prefers water temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) with a pH range of 6.0 to 7.5. It can thrive in both soft and moderately hard water.
  • Substrate: Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla often does best when attached to rocks or driftwood, mimicking its natural growing conditions. It can also be planted in the substrate if desired.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with a balanced aquarium fertilizer supports healthy growth and vibrant colors. CO2 supplementation can enhance its development but is not strictly necessary.
  • Water Flow: Moderate to strong water flow helps mimic the plant’s natural environment, keeping its leaves clean and free from debris.

Benefits of Adding Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla to Your Aquarium

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The vibrant pink leaves of Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla create a stunning visual effect, enhancing the beauty of any aquascape.
  • Habitat Enrichment: Provides natural hiding spots and surfaces for biofilm, benefiting fish and invertebrates.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Like other aquatic plants, Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla helps maintain water quality by absorbing excess nutrients and oxygenating the water.

Bucephalandra Pink Godzilla is an exceptional choice for any aquarist looking to create a vibrant and healthy underwater environment. Its unique appearance and ease of care make it a standout plant for enhancing the beauty and biodiversity of any aquarium.


Sold as Bare-root Plants or Potted Plants:

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 1-2 plants potted in rockwool and a net pot for secure growth and easy placement.

products.title: Hygrophila Odora
products.body_html:
🌿 Hygrophila Odora – Narrow-Leaf Green Alternative to Water Wisteria & Hygrophila pinnatifida

Hygrophila odora is a rare and visually striking African stem plant that combines the delicate, finely divided look of  Water Wisteria (Hygrophila difformis) with the bold, architectural appeal of Hygrophila pinnatifida—while maintaining a clean, vibrant green coloration.

The leaf shape closely resembles Water Wisteria but is much narrower and more refined, giving the plant a sharper, more structured appearance. At the same time, its overall form and texture are very similar to Hygrophila pinnatifida, making Hygrophila odora an excellent green alternative for aquascapers who love pinnatifida’s look but prefer to avoid brown or reddish tones.

When grown submersed, Hygrophila odora produces elegant, deeply lobed green leaves on upright stems, making it ideal for midground to background placement. In emersed form, the leaves broaden slightly, develop a soft texture, and release a natural aromatic scent when touched—a rare and unique trait among aquarium plants.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Hygrophila odora
  • Common Name: Hygrophila Afrika
  • Origin: West Africa
  • Growth Rate: Medium
  • Placement: Midground to Background
  • Color: Bright to deep green
  • Leaf Shape: Narrow, finely divided (similar to Water Wisteria, but slimmer)
  • Comparable To: Hygrophila difformis (Water Wisteria), Hygrophila pinnatifida
  • Unique Trait: Aromatic foliage when grown emersed
  • Difficulty: Beginner to Advanced

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting: Medium to High
  • CO₂: Recommended for best structure and faster growth
  • Growth Method: Stem plant
  • Nutrient Substrate: Required
  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes
  • Propagation: Trim and replant healthy stem cuttings

With adequate nutrients and CO₂, Hygrophila odora grows strong, upright stems with consistent leaf development. It adapts well to submersed aquariums and also performs exceptionally in paludariums and ripariums when grown emersed.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Offers the fine, feathery look of Water Wisteria in a more compact, refined form
  • Delivers the bold structure of Hygrophila pinnatifida while staying green
  • Excellent contrast plant for red species, dark hardscape, and shadowed layouts
  • Perfect for nature-style, jungle, and collector aquascapes

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Hygrophila odora cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient planting formats:

Loose Bare-Root Plants:
Approximately 1 individual plant with 4+ healthy leaves, carefully cleaned and ready for planting.

Rooted Bunched Plants:
Approximately 1 rooted plant with 4+ leaves, secured with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.

Rooted Potted Plants:
Approximately 1 rooted plant with 4+ leaves, established in a net pot with rockwool for stability and easy planting.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Photos are for reference only. Actual plants may vary in size, shape, and coloration depending on growth stage and environmental conditions. Minor melting or leaf adjustment may occur as the plant adapts to a new aquarium—this is normal for stem plants.

products.title: Java Fern Narrow Leaf
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Narrow’ – Slender Java Fern with Upright Leaf Structure

Microsorum pteropus 'Narrow', commonly known as Java Fern Narrow, is a refined and elegant variant of the classic Java Fern, easily recognized by its long, narrow, upright leaves. This cultivar is a favorite among aquascapers for its graceful form and vertical growth, making it an excellent choice for midground or background placement in planted aquariums.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate Java Fern Narrow in its emersed form, ensuring a hardy plant ready to transition to submerged conditions. Once established, the slender green fronds gently sway in the current, adding subtle motion and height to your aquascape while maintaining a clean, open aesthetic.

Like all Microsorum species, Java Fern Narrow is a rhizome plant that must be attached to driftwood, stones, or other hardscape elements. It should never be buried in substrate, as doing so may lead to rhizome rot. This plant is slow-growing, low-maintenance, and thrives in low- to medium-light environments with no need for CO₂—ideal for beginners or those running low-tech aquariums.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Long, Narrow Leaves: Provides vertical contrast and an elegant, upright appearance

  • Hardscape Attachment: Best mounted on wood, rock, or decor—no need for planting in substrate

  • Slow Growth & Easy Care: Perfect for low-maintenance, low-tech setups

  • Versatile Use: Works well in both midground and background aquascape placements

  • Grown Emersed: Strong rhizomes transition smoothly to submerged growth

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Scientific Name: Microsorum pteropus ‘Narrow’

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus ‘Narrow’

  • Common Names: Java Fern Narrow

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Attach to driftwood, stone, or hardscape

  • Growth Rate: Slow

  • Lighting: Low to Medium

  • Origin: Southeast Asia

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Performs best under low to medium light

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: CO₂ is not required; occasional liquid fertilizer supports leaf strength

  • Water Parameters:

    • pH: 6.0–7.5

    • Temperature: 68–82°F (20–28°C)

    • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • CO₂ Requirement: Not required

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes

  • Propagation Method: Rhizome division

  • Bare-root Plants: Approximately 6+ leaf portion, loose rhizome

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 6+ leaf portion bundled with foam and plant weight

  • Potted Plants: Approximately 6+ leaf portion, potted in rockwool and net pot

products.title: Proserpinaca Palustris 'Cuba' (Mermaid Weed) Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
🌿 Proserpinaca Palustris 'Cuba' (Mermaid Weed) – Vibrant Serrated Aquarium Stem Plant

Proserpinaca palustris 'Cuba', commonly known as Mermaid Weed, is a unique and highly decorative aquarium stem plant admired for its dramatic leaf transformation and vibrant coloration. Under moderate conditions, the plant develops bright green leaves, while stronger lighting and CO₂ encourage the formation of finely serrated foliage with stunning copper, orange, and reddish tones.

This distinctive species creates beautiful vertical accents in aquascapes and offers striking texture compared to traditional smooth-leaf stem plants. Its delicate, needle-like leaves and dramatic color shifts make it a favorite choice for aquascapers seeking both contrast and visual depth in planted aquariums.

Grown in sterile laboratory conditions, this Vitro Culture® 4-inch bag provides clean, pest-free plant material ready to be divided into multiple healthy plantlets for planting in your aquarium.

Common Name: Mermaid Weed
Family: Haloragaceae

• Leaves shift from green to copper-red under strong lighting
• Elegant vertical growth ideal for midground or background placement
• Unique needle-like leaf form unlike most aquarium stem plants

CO₂: Recommended for best coloration and compact growth
Growth Rate: Medium

Water Temperature: 68°F – 82°F (20°C – 28°C)

Under strong lighting and proper nutrients, the plant develops deeper red and copper tones while maintaining compact serrated foliage.

• Snail-free and pest-free plant material

This allows aquascapers to create dense plant groupings and natural aquascapes with a single culture portion.

Mermaid Weed pairs beautifully with plants such as Rotala, Ludwigia, and other colorful stems to create a vibrant planted layout.

products.title: Bacopa Salzmannii 'Purple'
products.body_html:
💜 Bacopa salzmannii 'Purple' – Singapore Variant

Bacopa salzmannii 'Purple' (Singapore Variant) is a rare and highly coveted stem plant admired for its vivid magenta to deep purple tones—a true showstopper in any planted aquarium. Unlike the standard Bacopa salzmannii, this unique Singapore form maintains its striking purple coloration both emersed and submerged, making it an exceptional centerpiece or accent plant for aquascapes of all styles.

Beyond its beauty, this plant provides ecological balance by absorbing excess nutrients, improving water quality, and supporting a healthy aquatic environment.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bacopa salzmannii 'Purple' (Singapore Variant)

  • Common Name: Purple Bacopa, Singapore Purple Bacopa

  • Growth Form: Upright stem plant

  • Coloration: Vibrant purple to magenta—retains color above and below water

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Placement: Midground to background

  • Propagation: Stem cuttings from healthy growth

🔮 Unique Traits

  • Retains Purple Coloration in All Conditions: Unlike typical Bacopa salzmannii that turns green when grown emersed, this variant stays purple in both environments.

  • High-Contrast Focal Plant: Its bold color provides dramatic contrast against green plants, driftwood, and rockwork.

  • Singapore-Origin Lineage: Cultivated from a select strain renowned for its consistent hue and compact form.

💡 Optimal Conditions & Care

  • Lighting: Moderate to high—strong light enhances purple pigmentation

  • CO₂: Optional, but recommended for more vibrant color and lush growth

  • Water Parameters: 72–82°F (22–28°C); pH 6.0–7.5

  • Difficulty: Easy to moderate; ideal for most aquascapers

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds luxurious purple contrast to nature and Dutch-style layouts

  • Perfect for color transitions between red and green plant zones

  • Naturally helps reduce algae by absorbing excess nutrients

  • Creates a vibrant focal point when grouped or trimmed into dense clusters

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Nutrient Substrate: Required for optimal color and root development

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes – retains its purple hue even above water

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Bacopa salzmannii 'Purple' cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient planting formats:

Fresh Cut Plants: Approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots.
Fresh Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ stems bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
Fresh Cut Potted: About 6+ stems potted securely in a net pot with rockwool for stability and easy planting.

⚠️ Disclaimer

As with many stem plants, Bacopa salzmannii 'Purple' may experience brief melt or leaf loss during transition from emersed to submerged growth. This is normal—new underwater leaves will regrow once stable.

products.title: Java Fern Philippine Mini
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus ‘Philippine Mini’ – Compact Java Fern with Narrower Leaves

Microsorum pteropus ‘Philippine Mini’, also known as Java Fern Philippine Mini, is a compact and refined variant of the classic Java Fern Philippine. This cultivar is prized for its narrower, more delicate leaves, making it an excellent choice for aquascapers who want to add fine texture and structure to smaller or minimalist layouts.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate this variety emersed to ensure strong, adaptable growth. Once transitioned into your aquarium, it thrives in low- to medium-light conditions and requires minimal care—perfect for low-tech tanks. Like all Java Ferns, Philippine Mini is a rhizome plant, best attached to driftwood, rocks, or other hardscape elements rather than buried in substrate, which can cause rot.

Ideal for nano tanks, shrimp setups, or detailed aquascaping, Java Fern Philippine Mini brings understated elegance and natural movement to the midground or background without overpowering the layout.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Miniature Growth Form: Smaller, narrower leaves compared to the classic Java Fern Philippine

  • Hardscape-Friendly: Attaches easily to wood, rocks, and décor—no need for planting in substrate

  • Low Maintenance: Suitable for low-tech aquariums; grows well without CO₂

  • Refined Aesthetic: Perfect for nano tanks, shrimp aquariums, and minimalist aquascapes

  • Slow-Growing: Minimal trimming required and easy to manage

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus ‘Philippine Mini’

  • Common Names: Java Fern Philippine Mini

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Attach to driftwood, stone, or hardscape

  • Lighting: Low to Medium

products.title: Java Fern
products.body_html:
🌿 Java Fern (Leptochilus pteropus) – Classic Hardy Aquarium Plant

Leptochilus pteropus, formerly known as Microsorum pteropus and widely recognized as Java Fern, is one of the most iconic and beginner-friendly aquatic plants in the aquarium hobby. Renowned for its durability, adaptability, and timeless appearance, this plant features deep green, elongated leaves that bring a natural and mature look to any aquascape.

Naturally found attached to rocks and driftwood in slow-moving tropical waters, Java Fern thrives in a wide range of aquarium conditions, making it an essential choice for both beginners and experienced aquarists.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Leptochilus pteropus

  • Synonyms: Microsorum pteropus

  • Common Name: Java Fern

  • Hardy & Beginner-Friendly: Extremely resilient and easy to care for

  • Epiphytic Growth: Attaches to driftwood, rocks, or décor—no substrate required

  • Timeless Appearance: Deep green leaves add a natural, lush look

  • Low Maintenance: Thrives in a wide range of water parameters

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Low to moderate (tolerates a wide range)

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: CO₂ not required; benefits from liquid fertilizers

  • Growth Rate: Slow to moderate

  • Growth Method: Rhizome plant (must not be buried in substrate)

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

Java Fern propagates easily by dividing the rhizome or through plantlets that form on older leaves, making it simple to expand within your aquarium.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Perfect for Hardscape: Ideal for attaching to driftwood, rocks, and décor

  • Midground to Background Placement: Adds depth and structure to layouts

  • Natural Jungle Look: Creates a mature, established aquascape feel

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Java Fern (Leptochilus pteropus) cultivated and prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®, available in convenient planting formats:

  • Fresh Cut Plants: Approximately 6+ individual leaves with some roots attached. Leaves may arrive separated, and rhizomes are not guaranteed to be connected. This is normal for Java Fern and allows easy attachment to hardscape.
  • Fresh Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ leaves with some roots bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement. Leaves may be individual, and rhizomes may not be connected. This is normal for Java Fern and does not affect growth.
  • Fresh Cut Potted: Approximately 6+ leaves with some roots placed in a net pot with rockwool for stability. Leaves may be individual, and rhizomes may not be connected. This is normal and the plant will continue to grow once established.

⚠️ Disclaimer

Live aquatic plants may vary slightly in size, shape, and color depending on current stock and growing conditions. Java Fern is grown emersed and will transition naturally once placed in your aquarium. Some leaf melt may occur during this transition as the plant adapts to submerged conditions.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner.

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

Ludwigia Atlantis is a rare and visually captivating mutation of Ludwigia Repens, renowned for its stunning variegated foliage and dynamic color palette. This unique plant features green to pinkish-red stems with vibrant yellow-striped leaves, evoking the look of its cousin Ludwigia sp. Red Skeleton. When submerged, Ludwigia Atlantis transforms into a true showpiece—its leaves develop rich shades of warm reddish-orange, making it a standout focal point in any planted aquarium.

Highly adaptable and beginner-friendly, this plant delivers intense color even under moderate lighting. Whether you're crafting an elegant nature aquarium or an expressive aquascape, Ludwigia Atlantis offers a bold accent that will instantly elevate your layout.

Once underwater, Java Fern Black Forest maintains its dramatic leaf color and distinctive shape—making it a favorite for adding shadowy contrast and visual depth in midground to background placements.

products.title: Bolbitis Difformis
products.body_html:
Bolbitis heteroclita 'Difformis,' also known as Mini Bolbitis or Davallia sp., is a captivating miniature aquatic fern ideal for adding natural elegance to your aquarium or terrarium. Originating from the Philippines, this fern is prized for its delicate, finely divided leaves that create a lush, carpet-like effect.

Appearance

This miniature fern features finely divided, delicate leaves that form a lush, carpet-like cover. Its unique texture and vibrant green color add a touch of natural beauty to any aquascape.

Care Requirements

  • Lighting: Thrives in low to moderate light conditions.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers temperatures between 68-78°F (20-26°C) and a pH range of 5.5-7.5.
  • Substrate: Can be attached to driftwood or rocks, or planted in nutrient-rich substrates.

Propagation

Propagates easily through rhizome division. Simply cut the rhizome and replant the segments to encourage new growth.

Benefits and Placement

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a lush, natural look to your aquarium or terrarium.
  • Versatility: Perfect for foreground placement or as an accent on hardscape elements.
  • Low Maintenance: Easy to care for, making it suitable for aquarists of all levels.

Conclusion

Enhance your aquarium with Bolbitis heteroclita 'Difformis.' Its delicate beauty, ease of care, and versatility make it a must-have for any aquascape. Order this stunning miniature fern today and transform your tank into a lush, green paradise.

products.body_html:
🌱 Helanthium bolivianum – Fast-Growing Chain Sword for Lush Carpets

Sold as Tissue Culture plants in vitamin-rich gel | Bag size: Approx. 4 inches | Yields many small plants

Helanthium bolivianum, also known as Echinodorus bolivianum or the classic Chain Sword, is a vibrant and fast-spreading freshwater plant from South America. Known for its long, narrow, slightly curled leaves, this species is ideal for aquascapers looking to achieve dense, natural-looking foreground carpets or midground clusters.

Its bright green foliage creates a striking contrast in planted tanks and grows by sending out runners that rapidly form lush mats across the substrate. Grown using advanced Tissue Culture Technology, this plant arrives 100% free from algae, snails, and pathogens—ensuring a clean and healthy start in any aquarium setup.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Lucens Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne x willisii 'Lucens' commonly known as Cryptocoryne Lucens, is a versatile and resilient aquarium plant. Ideal for both beginners and experienced aquarists, it adds a touch of natural beauty to any tank.

Cryptocoryne Lucens features slender, lance-shaped leaves ranging from light to dark green. Its compact, rosette growth pattern creates a lush, dense carpet, perfect for the foreground or midground of your aquarium.

Care Requirements

Propagation

Propagates through runners. Allow the plant to spread naturally or separate and replant the runners to encourage new growth.

Benefits and Placement

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a lush, green appearance to your aquarium.
  • Versatility: Ideal for foreground and midground placement.
  • Low Maintenance: Easy to care for, making it suitable for all levels of aquarists.

Conclusion

Enhance your aquarium with Cryptocoryne Lucens. Its attractive appearance, ease of care, and adaptability make it a must-have for any aquascape. Order today and transform your tank into a vibrant, green paradise.
