products.title: Helanthium Quadricostatus (Echinodorus) Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus', also known as Echinodorus bolivianus 'Quadricostatus', is a popular aquatic plant among aquarium enthusiasts. It is a species of the Echinodorus genus and is native to Bolivia, Brazil, and Peru.

The plant is characterized by its long and narrow leaves that grow up to 20 inches in height and have a distinct reddish-brown coloration. Its leaves are arranged in a rosette pattern, and it forms runners that allow it to propagate and spread.

Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus' is a hardy plant that can thrive in a wide range of water conditions. It prefers moderate to high lighting and a nutrient-rich substrate. It can also tolerate a wide range of water hardness and pH levels.

In aquariums, Helanthium bolivianum 'Quadricostatus' is often used as a foreground or mid-ground plant, where its striking coloration and dense foliage can create a lush and vibrant underwater landscape. It also provides a natural habitat for small fish and invertebrates to hide and forage.

products.title: Schismatoglottis Prietoi Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Schismatoglottis Prietoi, an aquatic plant native to the Philippines, is a unique addition to the aquascaping world. It belongs to the family Araceae and is notable for its ability to thrive in fully aquatic environments, a rare trait within its genus. This small, colonial plant grows only up to 8 cm tall, featuring pale green to green, oblong to elliptical leaves with a smooth texture, sharply pointed tips, and broadly wavy edges.

Ideal for aquariums, Schismatoglottis Prietoi is known for its ease of care and adaptability to various water conditions. It prefers medium to low lighting and can flourish in temperatures ranging from 20 to 28°C. The plant's pH tolerance spans from 6.5 to 7.5, accommodating both soft and hard water types. While CO2 injection can promote faster growth, it's not a necessity for this plant.

Schismatoglottis Prietoi is particularly suited for smaller aquarium setups like Nano Cubes due to its compact growth pattern. It can be planted directly into the substrate or attached to hardscape materials like rocks and driftwood. This versatility, combined with its unique appearance and easy propagation through rhizomes, makes Schismatoglottis Prietoi a valuable plant for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

This species is a testament to the diversity and beauty of aquatic plants, offering a distinct look compared to more common aquarium plants like Anubias. It's a perfect choice for aquarists looking to create a natural, riverbed-like environment in their tanks.

products.title: Bucephalandra Rainbow Marble
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Rainbow Marble (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra which have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock or driftwood.

products.title: Rotala Indica (Ammannia Bonsai)
products.body_html:
Rotala Indica 'Bonsai,' also known as the true Rotala Indica, is often confused with Rotala Rotundifolia, a completely different species. Originally introduced as Ammannia sp. 'Bonsai,' its true identity was confirmed by Cavan Allen in 2009.

Distinctive Features: Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' is characterized by its thick, round leaves, with the tips turning a beautiful reddish hue under strong lighting. Unlike Rotala Rotundifolia, which tends to creep under intense light, Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' grows straight and upright, making it a striking addition to any aquascape.

Growth and Care: This plant is highly versatile, thriving in various aquarium settings. It is particularly popular in Street/Dutch-style aquariums and is ideal for small to nano tanks. Rotala Indica 'Bonsai' looks best when planted in small to medium groups, creating a lush and vibrant display.

Aquascaping Uses:

  • Dutch-Style Aquariums: Perfect for creating structured, layered layouts.
  • Nano Tanks: Its compact size makes it an excellent choice for smaller aquariums.
  • Group Planting: Grows beautifully in clusters, adding depth and color to your aquascape.

Enhance your aquarium with the unique beauty and growth habit of Rotala Indica 'Bonsai'. Shop now to add this true Rotala Indica to your collection and elevate your aquascaping designs.

Enhance your aquarium with our versatile selection of high-quality aquatic plants, available in three convenient formats:

  • Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6+ loose, fresh-cut stems without roots. Perfect for flexible planting arrangements.
  • Fresh-Cut Bunched: Approximately 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight for easy placement.
  • Fresh-Cut Potted: About 6+ fresh-cut stems without roots, securely potted in a net pot with rockwool, ensuring stability and healthy growth.

With proper care—moderate to high light, consistent CO₂ injection, and a nutrient-rich substrate—Pogostemon helferi forms dense, rosette-like clusters that resemble aquatic flowers. Though small in stature, it has a bold visual impact and becomes a lush carpet in stable, high-tech setups.

products.title: Bucephalandra Velvet Tricolor
products.body_html:
🌿 Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor" – Rare Bushy Epiphyte

Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor" (sometimes referred to as Velvet 3 Color) is a rare, small, and densely bushy Bucephalandra form with narrow, wavy leaves. Believed to originate from the Sanggau area in West Kalimantan, Borneo, its precise species classification and exact locality remain uncertain. Despite its rarity, this plant has proven to be highly suitable for aquariums, with excellent growth habits and steady propagation.

Young leaves emerge in shades of light brown-red, later maturing into deep dark brown-green, creating a striking multicolor effect that inspires its name. Its compact, ramified rhizome structure forms a bushy cluster, making it a unique addition to any aquascape.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Bucephalandra sp. "Velvet Tricolor"

  • Origin: West Kalimantan, Borneo (Indonesia)

  • Growth Form: Small, narrow-leaved Bucephalandra with bushy habit

  • Leaf Shape & Size: Linear, wavy leaves approx. 30 x 4.5 mm, with a 5 mm petiole

  • Coloration: Young leaves light brown-red, mature leaves dark brown-green

  • Height: Reaches around 6 cm when grown submerged

  • Rarity: Rarely available in the hobby, propagated in aquaria for over a year

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Lighting Needs: Medium lighting recommended

  • CO₂ & Fertilization: Benefits from CO₂ injection, good nutrient supply, and steady water current

  • Growth Method: Epiphyte – best attached to rocks or driftwood (avoid burying the rhizome)

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes, though usually propagated submerged in aquaria

  • Propagation: Easily propagated via rhizome cuttings, forming dense clusters over time

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Perfect as a low-growing bushy epiphyte in aquascapes

  • Offers subtle color contrast with red-brown to green hues

  • Excellent for hardscape decoration on rocks and driftwood

  • Suitable for nano aquascapes as well as larger aquariums due to its slow but steady propagation

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as Bare-root Plants:

  • 1 Plant: Loose bare-root with approximately 6+ leaves

  • 5 Plants: Loose bare-root with approximately 25+ leaves in total

  • 10 Plants: Loose bare-root with approximately 50+ leaves in total

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Like all Bucephalandra species, growth rate may vary depending on tank conditions. Ensure the rhizome is never buried in substrate, as this may cause rot. Stable water parameters and patience are key for long-term success.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Please check your local regulations regarding the import or keeping of aquatic plants. Never release aquarium plants into local waterways.

Rare Tri-Color Dwarf Lotus • Aquarium Water Lily

Nymphaea micrantha is a beautiful and rare dwarf lotus prized for its unique tri-color foliage, displaying shades of green, bronze, red, and purple depending on lighting and nutrients. Native to tropical West Africa, this compact water lily makes an excellent centerpiece plant for planted aquariums without overwhelming smaller tanks.

With proper care, Nymphaea micrantha produces elegant submerged leaves and may send surface leaves if allowed, creating a natural and dynamic aquascape.

products.title: Anubias Petite White Platinum
products.body_html:

Anubias Petite White Platinum is an exceptionally rare albino cultivar of Anubias barteri var. nana ‘Petite’, prized for its pure white to platinum foliage, miniature growth, and collector-grade rarity. With almost no chlorophyll, this plant delivers a striking contrast against dark hardscape and green plants, making it a true statement piece for premium aquascapes.

Due to its albino nature and extremely slow growth, this plant is recommended for experienced aquarists only. When placed correctly, it offers unmatched elegance and exclusivity in nano tanks and high-end layouts.

products.title: Rotala Macrandra Vitro Culture

Rotala Macrandra is a magnificent aquarium plant, however, it is quite demanding. This variety is a little simpler to grow and produces vibrant colors. It requires a lot of light to produce its gorgeous pink and red colors. It also preferred soft water and lots of CO2, micro, and macronutrients are essential for healthy growth.

Farm-raised Rotala Macrandra is easily broken in transit because of the fragile stems. It is also harder to adapt to the new environment when the plant reaches the mature stage. products.title: Echinodorus Gabrielii
products.body_html:
Echinodorus Gabrielii is a rare, exotic plant that is a great addition to any aquarium. This compact, Gabriel's Sword plant stays short and lush when it grows underwater, in the aquarium.

Echinodorus Gabrielii can be planted as a group in a larger aquarium or act as a focal, centerpiece in a smaller aquarium. This plant will provide cover for fish and other creatures living in the tank as well as oxygenate the water with its roots.

Like most other Echinodorus species, it required a nutrient-rich substrate because they are heavy root feeders. Which mean it will develop lots of roots to absorb the available nutrient in the aquaria. It's a great idea to provide a root tab as a secondary, additional nutrient for this species as needed.

products.title: Bacopa Platinum Vitro Culture
products.body_html:
Bacopa Monnieri Platinum is a unique version of the popular Bacopa Monnieri plant used in aquariums. At Aquarium Plants Factory, we're among the first to offer this special type of plant. The original Bacopa Monnieri, also called Moneywort or Water Hyssop, is easy to look after and grows fast in many settings. But this new version, the Platinum, is not common. It's harder to take care of because it doesn't have the usual green color that helps it photosensitive from sunlight.

You can put Bacopa Monnieri Platinum in the front part of your aquarium, but you need to look after it by trimming and taking care of it. When there's not a lot of light, it might get long and thin as it reaches for the light. But if you give it strong light and good nutrients, it will stay short and the stems will spread out really close to the bottom of the tank, like a creeping plant. Just remember, this special plant needs a bit more attention than the regular green one.

products.body_html:
🌿 Hygrophila Pinnatifida Vitro Culture

Elegant, Textured Midground Plant with Creeping Growth Habit

Hygrophila Pinnatifida is a unique and highly decorative aquarium plant originating from the Western Ghats of India. Loved for its deeply lobed leaves, bronze-to-reddish coloration, and distinctive horizontal growth habit, this species adds bold texture and contrast to planted aquariums. It naturally attaches to hardscape like rocks and driftwood, making it a versatile and visually captivating choice for aquascaping.

This listing features our Vitro Culture version, cultivated in sterile conditions and carefully sealed in a 4-inch bag. Ideal for advanced aquascapers and shrimp-safe environments, these lab-grown plants are 100% free from algae, pests, and pesticides.

🌱 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Hygrophila pinnatifida

  • Origin: India (Western Ghats)

  • Leaf Form: Deeply pinnate, textured leaves with bronze, olive, or reddish tones

  • Growth Habit: Creeping or bushy depending on trimming

  • Placement: Midground or hardscape attachment

  • Growth Rate: Medium

  • CO₂ Requirement: Recommended

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

💧 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Temperature Range: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Water Hardness: Soft to medium

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (can grow on wood/stone)

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes

🧪 Vitro Culture Advantages:

  • Delivered in a sealed 4-inch bag, easy to portion and plant

🌿 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Responds well to trimming—can grow bushy or spread horizontally

  • Works beautifully in nature-style or high-tech aquascapes

✂️ Propagation & Care Tips:

products.title: Java Fern Large Size
products.body_html:
🌿 Microsorum pteropus – Large Size Java Fern for Instant Impact

Microsorum pteropus, commonly known as Java Fern, is one of the most beloved and beginner-friendly aquatic plants in the hobby. This special large size variant, standing approximately 8–10 inches tall, is ideal for aquarists seeking a more mature, ready-to-display plant to add instant structure and greenery to their aquascape.

At Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate these large-size Java Ferns in emersed conditions, resulting in strong, vibrant leaves and thick rhizomes that transition beautifully when submerged. The classic, sword-like leaves are rich green in color and fan outward in a graceful arch, making them perfect for midground to background placement in medium to large aquariums.

Java Fern is a rhizome-based plant and should always be attached to driftwood, rocks, or other hardscape. Never bury the rhizome in substrate, as this may lead to rot. It is incredibly hardy, low maintenance, and adaptable to a wide range of water parameters—making it suitable for both novice and experienced aquarists.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Special Large Size: Grows approx. 8–10" tall—perfect for instant visual impact

  • Hardy & Adaptable: Tolerates various lighting and water conditions

  • Attachable Rhizome: Secure to wood or stone—do not bury

  • Low-Tech Friendly: Grows without CO₂ and requires minimal fertilization

  • Classic Aquascaping Plant: A staple for nature-style and traditional aquascapes

🔎 Botanical Information:

  • Scientific Name: Microsorum pteropus

  • Synonyms: Leptochilus pteropus

  • Common Names: Java Fern

  • Plant Type: Rhizome plant

  • Placement: Midground / Background (Attach to hardscape)

  • Lighting: Low to Medium

  • Origin: Southeast Asia

💡 Optimal Growing Conditions:

🌱 Growth & Propagation:

  • Can Grow Emersed: Yes

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 6+ leaf portion bundled with foam and plant weight (8–10 inches tall)

Lilaeopsis brasiliensis, known commonly as the Micro Sword, is a charming, grasslike aquatic plant native to the warm riverbanks of South America. With its fine, sword-shaped leaves and lush, vibrant green hue, it creates the perfect living carpet for aquascapes of all sizes.

Often admired for its soft texture and natural appeal, Micro Sword brings a tranquil meadow-like beauty to the foreground of aquariums. Under favorable conditions, it forms a dense, low-growing mat that sways gently with the current—evoking the serene underwater landscapes of tropical streams.

Compact, adaptable, and endlessly graceful, this species is ideal for both beginner and advanced aquascapers seeking an authentic, natural appearance.

products.title: Helanthium Tenellum (Dwarf Chain Sword)
products.body_html:
Helanthium Tenellum, previously known as Echinodorus Tenellus, is an excellent foreground plant for fish tanks and aquariums. This member of the chain sword group has been reclassified into the genus Helanthium, but it remains a popular choice among aquarists for its aesthetic appeal and ease of care.

Key Features:

  • Grass-Like Appearance: Helanthium Tenellum, commonly referred to as Dwarf Chain Sword or Pygmy Chain Sword, features compact, narrow leaves that resemble grass, making it an ideal foreground plant for creating a lush, green carpet in your aquarium.
  • Color Transformation: Under strong light conditions, the leaves of Helanthium Tenellum turn a beautiful reddish-green, adding a dynamic and colorful element to your aquascape.
  • Easy Propagation: This plant propagates easily through runners, allowing it to spread quickly and form a dense carpet that enhances the visual appeal of any tank.

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The grass-like, compact leaves of Helanthium Tenellum create a beautiful and natural-looking carpet in the foreground of your aquarium, providing a visually pleasing environment for both you and your aquatic inhabitants.
  • Low Maintenance: Helanthium Tenellum is a hardy plant that thrives in various water conditions. Its ability to propagate through runners makes it easy to maintain and expand within your tank.
  • Versatile: Suitable for both small and large aquariums, this plant can adapt to different light conditions, making it a versatile choice for aquascapers.

Ideal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under strong light conditions, which help enhance its reddish-green coloration.
  • Water Parameters: Adaptable to a wide range of water conditions, making it easy to care for in different aquarium setups.
  • Substrate: Requires a nutrient-rich substrate to support healthy growth and propagation.

Why Choose Helanthium Tenellum?

Helanthium Tenellum is perfect for aquarists looking to create a lush, green carpet in their aquarium. Its compact, grass-like leaves, ease of care, and ability to propagate quickly make it an ideal foreground plant. Whether you are setting up a new tank or enhancing an existing one, Helanthium Tenellum will add a touch of natural beauty and elegance to your underwater landscape.

Enhance your aquarium with the beautiful and easy-to-care-for Helanthium Tenellum. Enjoy the lush, green carpet it creates and the vibrant colors it brings to your underwater world.

This fast-growing background plant is a favorite in both low-tech and high-tech aquariums for its hardiness and ability to create a lush, swaying underwater meadow that mimics nature's aquatic grasslands.

Whether you’re designing a natural riverbank biotope or looking to add movement and contrast to your aquascape, Vallisneria 'Leopard' offers an exotic twist with minimal maintenance.

products.body_html:

Sold as: Loose Bare-Root, Rooted Bunched, or Rooted Potted Plants

🪷 Introduction

Cyperus helferi is a captivating, grass-like aquatic plant admired for its elegant form and flowing movement. Native to Southeast Asia, this species adds vertical contrast and natural rhythm to any aquascape. Its long, bright-green leaves sway gently with the current, evoking the tranquil beauty of a riverbank scene.

A true aquascaper’s favorite, Cyperus helferi can be grown both emersed and submerged, making it an adaptable choice for aquarium layouts or paludarium designs.

  • Scientific Name: Cyperus helferi

  • Common Name: Helfer’s Umbrella Grass

  • Growth Form: Grass-like rosette plant

  • Leaf Color: Bright to medium green

products.title: Eriocaulon Vietnam
products.body_html:
Vietnam Eriocaulon (Eriocaulon sp. Vietnam) is a rare and highly sought-after aquarium plant, originating from Thanh Hoa, Vietnam. Known for its grass-like appearance and broad, arching medium-green leaves, this species reaches a height of 15–30 cm, making it a beautiful midground or background choice in aquascapes. Often compared to Eriocaulon sp. "Goias" due to its similar structure, Vietnam Eriocaulon stands out with its ease of care and adaptability.

Unlike the more challenging Blyxa japonica, Vietnam Eriocaulon is easier to cultivate and requires less attention, thriving in a variety of water conditions. It grows well in moderate lighting and can even flourish in hard water environments, making it more versatile than other Eriocaulon species. While it appreciates nutrient-rich substrates and CO2 supplementation, it requires minimal maintenance, making it suitable for both intermediate and experienced aquarists.

Key Features:

  • Distinctive Appearance: Grass-like plant with medium-green, arching leaves, creating a striking focal point in any aquarium.
  • Compact and Versatile: Grows to a height of 15–30 cm, suitable for midground or background placement in aquascapes.
  • Easy to Grow: Simpler to cultivate than other species, including Blyxa japonica, making it more accessible to a wider range of hobbyists.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Thrives under moderate lighting, though it can adapt to slightly higher levels for optimal growth.
  • Water Parameters: Tolerates a wide range of water conditions, including hard water, with a pH between 6.0 and 7.5 and temperatures of 68°F to 78°F (20°C to 26°C).
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Benefits from a nutrient-rich environment to support healthy growth and vibrant foliage.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Low Maintenance: Requires minimal upkeep, making it an excellent choice for aquarists looking for a beautiful yet easy-to-manage plant.
  • Aquascaping Flexibility: Works well as a midground or background plant in a variety of setups, enhancing both nano and larger aquariums.
  • Hard Water Tolerance: Unlike many other Eriocaulon species, Vietnam Eriocaulon thrives in hard water, making it an adaptable option for different aquarium environments.

Conclusion:

Vietnam Eriocaulon is a unique and rare plant that offers the beauty of grass-like foliage with the ease of low maintenance. Ideal for aquarists seeking a striking yet manageable plant, its adaptability to moderate light and hard water conditions makes it a standout addition to both beginner and advanced planted tanks. Elevate your aquascape with the natural elegance of Vietnam Eriocaulon, a plant that offers beauty without the hassle.

Choose the format that best suits your aquascaping needs and create a vibrant, lush underwater environment with our premium aquatic plants.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Ferruginea Sekadauensis Vitro Culture

Cryptocoryne ferruginea 'Sekadauensis' is a rare and visually distinct aquarium plant native to Borneo, Indonesia, named after the Sekadau region in West Kalimantan. Known for its richly textured, lance-shaped leaves and variable coloration ranging from olive-green to reddish-brown with subtle metallic undertones, this Cryptocoryne species is a true collector’s gem. Now available in our Vitro Culture® bag, this plant is grown under sterile, lab-controlled conditions to ensure it arrives free from snails, algae, and pathogens—ideal for aquascaping in sensitive or shrimp-safe tanks.

Its compact, low-growing rosette form makes it perfect for foreground or midground planting, especially in nature-style layouts where subtle texture and color variation add depth and realism.

  • Leaf Shape: Narrow, lance-shaped leaves with gently ruffled edges

products.title: Microsorum Pteropus 'Needle'
products.body_html:
Microsorum Pteropus 'Needle' is a unique and attractive cultivar of the popular aquatic plant, Java Fern. As its name suggests, this plant is characterized by its long, narrow, and pointed leaves, which can grow up to 25 cm (10 inches) in length under optimal conditions. The plant is a slow-grower, but can reach a height of up to 20 cm (8 inches) with patience.

This low-maintenance plant is well-suited for both novice and experienced aquarists. It is adaptable to a wide range of water conditions, including slightly acidic to slightly alkaline pH levels and soft to moderately hard water. The plant can thrive in low to moderate light and does not require CO2 supplementation. However, regular fertilization is recommended to promote healthy growth and maintain its vibrant coloration.

One of the benefits of Microsorum Pteropus 'Needle' is that it is easy to care for and can be attached to driftwood or rocks in an aquarium using fishing line or thread until it establishes a strong hold. It is important not to bury the rhizome (horizontal stem) of the plant, as this can cause it to rot.

The 'Needle' variety of Java Fern is known for its striking and unique appearance, which can add a touch of texture and depth to any aquascape. The plant is suitable for midground or background placement in an aquarium and can be used to create a natural-looking aquatic environment.

In summary, Microsorum Pteropus 'Needle' is a beautiful and low-maintenance aquatic plant that can add a unique and eye-catching element to any aquarium. It is a great choice for aquarists who want to add a touch of texture and depth to their aquascape without too much effort.

  • Loose rhizome | approx. 6 leaf, loose, bare-root, small portion
  • Potted plants | approx. 6 leaf, potted plants with rockwool and net pot

Disclaimer: Pictures are not the actual plants you will receive, but a sample representation. Unless specific, we don't guarantee aquatic plants will be free of pests, pesticides, grow emerged, or submerge.

products.title: Dwarf Water Lettuce
products.body_html:
🌿 Dwarf Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes 'Dwarf') | Floating Aquarium & Pond Plant

Dwarf Water Lettuce (Pistia stratiotes 'Dwarf') is a compact, ornamental floating plant perfect for aquariums, nano tanks, and small ponds. Its petite, velvety leaves form rosettes that float gracefully on the water's surface, creating a soft, natural look while providing shade and shelter for aquatic life. The smaller size makes it easier to manage than standard Water Lettuce, especially in indoor aquariums or tight spaces.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Compact Floating Plant: Ideal for aquariums, nano tanks, and small outdoor ponds.

  • Natural Water Purification: Absorbs excess nutrients, helping reduce algae growth.

  • Attractive Rosettes: Soft green, lettuce-like leaves with delicate floating roots.

  • Aquatic Habitat Enhancement: Offers shade and hiding spots for fish, fry, and shrimp.

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Moderate to high; thrives in bright conditions with calm water.

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.0–7.5; temperature 70–85°F (21–29°C)

  • Placement: Floating on the surface; no planting required.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Enhances the natural appearance of aquariums and ponds.

  • Provides gentle surface coverage to reduce light penetration and algae.

  • Supports a healthy ecosystem by offering shade and protection for aquatic life.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

Sold as 1–2 loose Dwarf Water Lettuce plants (unrooted, floating rosettes).

⚠️ Disclaimer:

Dwarf Water Lettuce is a sensitive tropical species. It cannot survive freezing temperatures outdoors and may require careful seasonal management. Always monitor growth to prevent overpopulation in ponds.

products.title: Bucephalandra Achilles Black
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Achilles Black (Bucep / Buce plant) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Araceae. There are more than 30 species of Bucephalandra that have been discovered mostly by S.Y. Wong and P.C. Boyce. Most of the species are found in Borneo. Bucephalandra is usually found growing as dense mats over stones or rocks in streams or rivers in moist tropical forests. The colors of the leaf range from dark blue to green, often with white, yellow, red tinges or spots on the back of the leaves. Bucephalandra grows best when attached to the stone, rock, or driftwood.

products.title: Amazon Frogbit

This South American native brings a natural, serene aesthetic to both freshwater aquariums and outdoor ponds, while also serving important functional benefits.

With its ability to absorb excess nutrients, shade the water, and provide shelter for fish and fry, Amazon Frogbit is both beautiful and beneficial. It’s a fast grower and easy to maintain, making it an excellent choice for aquarists of all skill levels.

  • pH Range: 6.0–7.5

  • Water Hardness: Soft to moderately hard

  • Lighting: Moderate to bright lighting promotes lush growth and vibrant color

🐟 Aquarium & Pond Benefits:

  • Natural Shade: Helps reduce algae growth by limiting light penetration

  • Fish Shelter: Provides hiding spaces and comfort for fish, shrimp, and fry

  • Nutrient Control: Absorbs ammonia, nitrates, and other waste products

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a calm, floating element to topwater areas

🔁 Propagation & Growth:

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not Required

Amazon Frogbit grows quickly under optimal conditions. Be sure to trim as needed to prevent surface overgrowth or blockage of light to submerged plants. Not suitable for tanks with strong surface agitation or high water flow.

products.title: Dracaena Sanderiana – Lucky Bamboo
products.body_html:
🎋 Dracaena sanderiana – Lucky Bamboo
Semi-Aquatic Ornamental Plant | Can Grow in Aquariums with Emerged Foliage

Dracaena sanderiana, widely known as Lucky Bamboo, is a beautiful and resilient ornamental plant often used in aquariums, paludariums, and indoor decorative displays. Despite its common name, Lucky Bamboo is not a true bamboo but a member of the Dracaena family. Its smooth green stems and elegant narrow leaves create a clean, minimal aesthetic that pairs beautifully with modern aquascapes and indoor plant arrangements.

One unique feature of Dracaena sanderiana is that the stem can safely grow in aquarium water, making it popular for aquarists who want a plant that bridges both aquatic and terrestrial environments. The stems tolerate full submersion, but the leaves are not adapted for underwater life.

When fully submerged, the leaves will typically decline and melt after a short period of time. For long-term health, the leaves should grow above the water line, where they will continue to develop normally and can thrive permanently in that position. This makes Lucky Bamboo ideal for aquariums where the stem is placed in water while the foliage grows above the tank.

🌟 Key Features

Scientific Name: Dracaena sanderiana
Common Name: Lucky Bamboo
Plant Type: Semi-aquatic ornamental houseplant

• Aquarium Compatible Stem: The stem can grow submerged in aquarium water.
• Emergent Growth Habit: Leaves must grow above the water surface for long-term health.
• Elegant Upright Structure: Smooth green stalks with narrow pointed leaves create a clean decorative look.
• Versatile Placement: Perfect for aquariums, paludariums, ripariums, and indoor plant displays.

🌱 Growth & Care

Lighting: Low to moderate light
Water Parameters: Adaptable to most aquarium conditions
Placement: Stem can be placed underwater, but leaves must remain above water
Growth Form: Upright stem plant with emergent foliage

Important:
While the stem tolerates submersion, the leaves will only survive a short time underwater. For permanent growth in an aquarium environment, position the plant so that the foliage grows above the water line.

🪴 Aquarium & Decorative Benefits

• Natural Aquascape Accent: Adds vertical structure and a natural look to aquariums.
• Perfect for Open-Top Tanks: Ideal for aquariums where plants can grow above the water surface.
• Low Maintenance: Hardy and adaptable, making it suitable for beginners and experienced hobbyists alike.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Fresh, healthy Dracaena sanderiana (Lucky Bamboo) carefully prepared by Aquarium Plants Factory®:

Fresh Cut Plant:
• 1 healthy stem cutting approximately 8–12 inches tall with attached leaves.

⚠️ Care Disclaimer

Dracaena sanderiana is commonly used in aquariums, but only the stem should remain submerged.
Leaves are not aquatic and will not survive permanently underwater.

For best long-term results, position the plant so the stem grows in water while the leaves grow above the aquarium.

🚫 State Restrictions Notice ♻️ Be a Responsible Aquatic Plants Owner

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.

products.title: Aponogeton Ulvaceus
products.body_html:
Aponogeton ulvaceus – Flowing Green Elegance for Large Aquascapes

Aponogeton ulvaceus is a spectacular bulb plant renowned for its vibrant green, ruffled leaves that sway gracefully in the current, creating a captivating underwater display. Native to Madagascar, this fast-growing species is ideal for larger aquariums where its long, arching foliage can shine as a dramatic centerpiece.

🌿 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Aponogeton ulvaceus

  • Common Name: Ulvaceus Bulb Plant

  • Origin: Madagascar

  • Leaf Length: Up to 60 cm (24 inches) or more

  • Color: Bright to translucent green

  • Growth Rate: Fast

  • Light Demand: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Optional but enhances growth and color

  • Placement: Midground to background (best in large tanks)

🌱 Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Water Temperature: 72–82°F (22–28°C)

  • pH Range: 6.5–7.5

  • Tank Size Recommendation: 20 gallons or larger

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (feeds from bulb and water column)

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: No

💧 Aquascaping Benefits:

  • Adds dynamic movement with long, ruffled leaves

  • Creates a lush, natural look in open-style or biotope aquascapes

  • Ideal as a focal plant in larger tanks

  • Provides hiding spots and cover for fish

🌼 Propagation & Growth:

Aponogeton ulvaceus reproduces via bulb division and occasionally seeds. It may undergo dormancy after blooming, especially in cooler water—reduce lighting and stop fertilizing during this phase, then resume normal care to trigger regrowth.

📦 What You’ll Receive:

At Aquarium Plants Factory®, we offer two options to suit every aquarist’s preference:

  • Sprouted Bulb With Leaf – for a guaranteed head start.

  • Bulb Only Without Leaf – for experienced hobbyists who prefer to grow from a dormant stage.

⚠️ Important Note:

Bulbs without leaves are healthy and capable of sprouting under stable and proper conditions, usually within a few weeks. However, successful sprouting requires aquarist experience and consistent parameters.
If you’re unsure or want an immediate result, we strongly recommend choosing the Sprouted Bulb With Leaf.

products.title: Ammannia Gracilis
products.body_html:
Ammannia Gracilis, also known as Large Ammannia, Red Ammannia, or Pink Ammannia, is a stunning stem plant that has been popular among aquarists for years. Often mislabeled as Nesaea crassicaulis in trade, true Ammannia crassicaulis is a similar species that turns yellowish-green to reddish under water but is no longer commonly farmed as an aquarium plant.

Here at Aquarium Plants Factory, we cultivate Ammannia Gracilis emerged. When grown emerged, the leaves are green, but they change to a vibrant red color when adapted to submerged life.

Key Features:

  • Vibrant Colors: Ammannia Gracilis is renowned for its brilliant red and pink hues, which are particularly vibrant under high lighting conditions. When grown emersed, its leaves are green, but they transform to a rich red when submerged.
  • Distinctive Leaves: This plant has thin, elongated leaves that grow up to 12 cm long underwater. The leaves are brown-red on top and violet underneath, adding to its unique appearance.

Optimal Growing Conditions:

  • Lighting: Requires high lighting to achieve its vibrant colors. Medium lighting may result in a more yellow or greenish appearance.
  • CO2 and Nutrients: While CO2 supplementation is not mandatory, it is highly recommended for optimal growth and coloration. Regular fertilization with macro and micronutrients supports its rapid growth.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers slightly acidic to neutral water with a pH range of 5.0-7.5. Ideal water temperatures are between 72-82°F (22-28°C). It enjoys temperatures over 24°C but can tolerate up to 32°C.
  • Soft Water: Rather soft water is beneficial, though it can also thrive in medium-hard water with CO2 supplementation.

Planting and Propagation:

  • Substrate: Plant in a nutrient-rich substrate to ensure robust growth. Ammannia Gracilis thrives best when spaced about 2 inches apart to allow for proper light penetration.
  • Propagation: Easily propagated through stem cuttings. Cut healthy stems and replant them in the substrate. This plant is also propagated by stem-tip and side-shoot cuttings.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Pruning: Regular trimming is essential to prevent the plant from overshadowing other species in the aquarium. Trim the plant when it reaches 4-6 inches below the water surface to encourage bushier growth.
  • Water Flow: Avoid high water flow areas as Ammannia Gracilis prefers still or slow-moving water environments.

Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: The vibrant colors and unique growth pattern of Ammannia Gracilis make it an excellent focal point or background plant in aquascapes. It looks best as a group in the middle of larger aquariums and works nicely for "plant streets" in Dutch-style tanks.
  • Easy Care: Despite its requirement for high lighting and CO2, it is relatively easy to care for with the right setup, making it suitable for both novice and experienced aquarists.

Conclusion:

Elevate your aquarium with the stunning Ammannia Gracilis. Its vibrant red and pink colors, combined with its graceful growth pattern, make it a must-have for any aquascape enthusiast. With proper care and maintenance, Ammannia Gracilis will transform your aquarium into a vivid underwater paradise.

  • Sold as loose Fresh-cut plants, Bunched plants, or Potted plants

  • Potted plants |    Approx. 6 fresh-cut stems without root, potted with net pot and rockwool

products.title: Samolus Valerandi
products.body_html:
Samolus Valerandi, also known by its trade name Samolus Parviflorus, is a versatile and attractive aquatic plant that adds a delicate touch to both aquariums and paludariums. This adaptable species is characterized by its lush rosettes of small, bright green leaves, resembling miniature lettuce, which bring a natural elegance to any setup. With its ability to grow both submerged and emersed, it is a favorite among aquascapers looking for a versatile plant to enhance their aquascapes.

Key Features:

  • Compact Rosette Growth: Forms small, dense clusters of bright green leaves that create a lush, leafy appearance, perfect for adding texture and depth to aquascapes.
  • Adaptable Plant: Thrives in a variety of conditions and can grow submerged in aquariums or emersed in paludariums, terrariums, and ripariums, making it suitable for a range of aquascaping styles.
  • Perfect for Foreground and Midground: The compact nature of Samolus Valerandi makes it ideal for use as a foreground or midground plant, bringing a vibrant green accent to your aquascape.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Grows best under moderate lighting, which encourages healthy, compact growth and leaf development. It can also adapt to both low and high lighting levels.
  • Water Parameters: Prefers temperatures between 68°F to 82°F (20°C to 28°C) and a pH level of 6.0 to 7.5. A nutrient-rich substrate and regular fertilization will promote lush, vigorous growth.

Aquarium and Paludarium Benefits:

  • Natural Look and Feel: With its bright green color and rosette structure, this plant enhances the visual appeal of any aquarium or paludarium, complementing various aquascaping themes.
  • Water Quality Improvement: Helps absorb excess nutrients and stabilize water conditions, contributing to a healthy, balanced aquatic environment.
  • Shelter and Habitat: Provides cover and grazing areas for small fish, shrimp, and other aquatic inhabitants, fostering a natural and stress-free habitat.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Low Maintenance: A hardy and easy-care plant that suits both beginner and experienced aquarists. It requires minimal upkeep while maintaining its compact, attractive shape.
  • Easy Propagation: Propagates through offshoots and runners, allowing for quick and easy spread within your aquatic setup.

Conclusion:

Samolus Valerandi (Samolus Parviflorus) is a versatile, low-maintenance plant that brings a touch of greenery and a natural aesthetic to any aquascape. Whether you want to add a bright accent to your aquarium's foreground or create a lush edge in a paludarium, this adaptable species will thrive and enhance the beauty of your setup. Add the delicate charm and versatility of Samolus Valerandi to your aquatic landscape and enjoy the vibrant look and natural benefits it provides.

products.title: Hydrotriche Hottoniiflora
products.body_html:
Hydrotriche hottoniiflora is an aquatic plant species that belongs to the family Hydrocharitaceae. It is commonly known as the feathered water sprite or the water nymph.

This plant is native to tropical Africa and is widely distributed throughout the continent. It is a popular aquarium plant due to its delicate, feathery leaves that add an elegant touch to any aquascape. Hydrotriche hottoniiflora is also known for its fast growth rate, making it an excellent choice for beginners who want to quickly fill in their aquarium with lush vegetation.

In addition to its aesthetic appeal, Hydrotriche hottoniiflora is also a beneficial plant for the aquarium ecosystem. It helps to oxygenate the water, absorb excess nutrients, and provide hiding places for fish and invertebrates.

To successfully grow Hydrotriche hottoniiflora in an aquarium, it requires moderate to high lighting and nutrient-rich water. It can be propagated by cutting the stem and replanting it, and it is relatively easy to care for.

Overall, Hydrotriche hottoniiflora is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to add a touch of elegance and benefit the ecosystem of their aquarium.

products.title: Alternanthera Lilacina
products.body_html:
Alternanthera reineckii 'Lilacina', also known as Purple Scarlet Temple and Alternanthera Purple Lila, is a stunning aquatic plant renowned for its vibrant colors and ease of propagation. This plant features beautiful light pink to red upper leaves and striking violet-purple undersides, making it a standout addition to any aquarium.

Key Features:

  • Striking Coloration: Light pink and red upper leaves contrast beautifully with the violet-purple undersides, adding a burst of color to your aquascape.
  • Easy Propagation: Can be easily propagated by trimming and replanting, making it a convenient choice for aquarists.

Optimal Conditions:

  • Lighting: Does not require high lighting to maintain its vibrant colors, thriving well under moderate light conditions.
  • CO2: Does not require CO2 supplementation, although providing CO2 can enhance growth and coloration.
  • Water Parameters: Adaptable to a wide range of water conditions, making it suitable for various aquarium setups.

Aquarium Benefits:

  • Aesthetic Appeal: Adds a dramatic and colorful element to aquascapes, enhancing the overall visual appeal.
  • Popular in Dutch-Style Aquascapes: Well-known and highly regarded in Dutch-style aquascaping for its vibrant colors and structural form.
  • Versatile Placement: Ideal for midground or background planting, providing depth and color contrast in the aquarium.

Care and Maintenance:

  • Regular Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages bushier growth.
  • Nutrient-Rich Substrate: Thrives in a nutrient-rich substrate, which supports healthy growth and vibrant coloration.
  • Moderate Lighting: While it doesn't require high light, providing moderate lighting will help maintain its stunning colors.

Conclusion:

Alternanthera reineckii 'Lilacina' (Purple Scarlet Temple / Alternanthera Purple Lila) is a captivating and easy-to-care-for plant that adds a splash of vibrant color to any aquarium. Its light pink, red, and violet-purple leaves make it a popular choice for Dutch-style aquascapes and other planted tank setups. With its simple propagation and adaptability, Alternanthera reineckii 'Lilacina' is an excellent choice for aquarists of all levels.

Transform your aquascape with the striking beauty of Alternanthera reineckii 'Lilacina' and enjoy the dynamic, colorful impact it brings to your underwater environment.

products.title: Ludwigia Super Red Mini
products.body_html:
🌿 Ludwigia Palustris Super Red – Vivid Miniature Stem Plant for Striking Aquascapes

Ludwigia Palustris Super Red, commonly referred to as Ludwigia Super Red Mini, is a highly sought-after stem plant known for its vibrant red coloration and compact leaf size. This eye-catching variety adds brilliant contrast to green and neutral-toned aquascapes, making it a favorite for both beginners and seasoned aquascapers alike.

Its miniaturized leaves and intense pigmentation make it ideal for nano tanks, midground displays, or accent groupings in larger layouts. Under high light and CO₂ supplementation, the red hues become even more pronounced—delivering a bold, showstopping visual impact.

Ludwigia Palustris Super Red is a stem plant that propagates easily through cuttings. Simply snip a healthy stem with several nodes and replant it directly into the substrate. Roots will form at the nodes, and new shoots will emerge quickly with proper lighting and nutrients. Regular trimming helps maintain its compact shape and encourages bushier, fuller growth.

🌟 Key Features:

  • Scientific Name: Ludwigia palustris ‘Super Red’

  • Common Name: Ludwigia Super Red Mini

  • Coloration: Deep red to crimson under optimal conditions

  • Leaf Size: Miniature compared to standard Ludwigia species

  • Placement: Midground to background depending on tank size

  • Growth Rate: Moderate

  • Lighting Requirement: Medium to high

  • CO₂ Requirement: Recommended for best coloration

products.title: Cryptocoryne Spiralis
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne Spiralis ( Crypt ) is one of the most popular, and widely available aquatic plants in the aquarium hobby. It has several color variations, and it can be found in green, red, pink, bronze and brown colors. The size of the leaves can vary greatly, and even the texture of the leaves can differ from plant to plant. Crypt Wendtii is an extraordinarily hardy plant, and it will thrive under low lighting and in both soft and hard water conditions. It is an excellent plant for both experienced and beginner aquarists.

products.title: Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata'
products.body_html:
🌿 Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata' – Unique Serrated Leaf Form

Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata' is a rare and distinctive form of the popular Bacopa monnieri. While the standard variety is known for its smooth, rounded leaves, the 'Serrata' variant stands out with its delicately serrated, elongated foliage. This subtle difference creates extra texture and depth in aquascapes, making it a favorite among collectors and aquascaping enthusiasts who want something special.

🌟 Key Features

  • Scientific Name: Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata'

  • Common Name: Serrated Bacopa, Serrated Moneywort, Water Hyssop Variant

  • Common Bacopa vs. 'Serrata':

    • Common Bacopa monnieri → Smooth, rounded leaves with a softer look.

    • Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata' → Slightly elongated leaves with serrated margins, giving a more textured, refined appearance.

  • Growth Habit: Upright stem plant that thrives both submerged and emersed.

  • Lighting Needs: Low to medium lighting is sufficient, though higher light enhances leaf definition.

  • CO₂ & Nutrients: Benefits from CO₂ and nutrient-rich conditions but remains hardy without them.

🌱 Growth & Propagation

  • Lighting Needs: Low–medium, higher light improves form.

  • CO₂ & Fertilizers: Not required, but promotes faster growth and lush leaves.

  • Growth Method: Stem plant; grows vertically, making it perfect for midground or background placement.

  • Nutrient Substrate: Not required (can be anchored or left floating until rooted).

  • Can Be Grown Emersed: Yes — will adapt beautifully in paludariums or humid greenhouse setups.

🖼️ Aquascaping Benefits

  • Adds a textured, refined look compared to smooth-leaved Bacopa.

  • Works beautifully as a midground accent or background filler.

  • Excellent for contrast planting alongside smooth-leaved Bacopa or fine-leaved stems like Rotala or Cabomba.

📦 What You’ll Receive

Choose from our selection of fresh-cut aquarium plants, available in three convenient formats to suit your needs:

  • Loose Fresh-Cut Plants: Includes approximately 6 stems of loose, fresh-cut plants without roots.

  • Bunched Plants: Approximately 6 fresh-cut plants without roots, bundled with foam and a plant weight.

  • Potted Plants: About 6 fresh-cut plants without roots, potted in a net pot with rock wool.

⚠️ Disclaimer

As with all stem plants, Bacopa monnieri 'Serrata' may experience minor leaf melt or trimming after shipping. Simply replant healthy stems, and new growth will quickly establish.

Certain aquatic plants may be restricted or regulated in some U.S. states. Always check your local and state regulations before purchasing or introducing plants into natural waterways. Never release aquarium plants into the wild.
